**Claude** *(3/9/2026, 7:51:12 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/README.md`**
```md
# Tune-In

An online web platform that allows users to create personalized accounts to review and rate music they have listened to. The platform functions similarly to a social media experience — think Letterboxd, but for music.

## Key Features

- **User Accounts** — Create and manage a personalized profile to track your music reviews and listening history.
- **Reviews & Ratings** — Write reviews and assign ratings to music you have listened to.
- **Social Interaction** — Search for other user profiles, follow them, and interact with their reviews.
- **Review Lists** — Organize your reviews into curated lists for easy browsing and sharing.
- **Listening Queue** — Build a queue of songs and albums you have yet to review, so you never lose track of what's next.

## Additional Planned Features

- Dark mode
- Advanced filtering
```

You'll need to supply your own Firebase project config (from the Firebase console) to drop into `firebase-config.js`. Shall I proceed?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:04:48 AM)*

```js
import { initializeApp } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-app.js";
import { getAuth } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";

// Replace each value below with the config from your Firebase project console.
// See setup instructions in the README.
const firebaseConfig = {
  apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY",
  authDomain: "YOUR_AUTH_DOMAIN",
  projectId: "YOUR_PROJECT_ID",
  storageBucket: "YOUR_STORAGE_BUCKET",
  messagingSenderId: "YOUR_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID",
  appId: "YOUR_APP_ID"
};

const app = initializeApp(firebaseConfig);
export const auth = getAuth(app);
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:05:44 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/auth.js`**
```js
import {
  createUserWithEmailAndPassword,
  signInWithEmailAndPassword,
  signOut,
  onAuthStateChanged,
  updateProfile
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { auth } from "./firebase-config.js";

// --- Tab switching ---
const loginTab = document.getElementById("login-tab");
const signupTab = document.getElementById("signup-tab");
const loginForm = document.getElementById("login-form");
const signupForm = document.getElementById("signup-form");

loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
  loginTab.classList.add("active");
  signupTab.classList.remove("active");
  loginForm.classList.remove("hidden");
  signupForm.classList.add("hidden");
  clearError();
});

signupTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
  signupTab.classList.add("active");
  loginTab.classList.remove("active");
  signupForm.classList.remove("hidden");
  loginForm.classList.add("hidden");
  clearError();
});

// --- Sign Up ---
signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;

  try {
    const { user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});

// --- Log In ---
loginForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const email = document.getElementById("login-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("login-password").value;

  try {
    await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});

// --- Redirect if already logged in ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (user) window.location.href = "profile.html";
});

// --- Helpers ---
function showError(message) {
  const el = document.getElementById("auth-error");
  el.textContent = message;
  el.classList.remove("hidden");
}

function clearError() {
  const el = document.getElementById("auth-error");
  el.textContent = "";
  el.classList.add("hidden");
}

function friendlyError(code) {
  const messages = {
    "auth/email-already-in-use": "An account with that email already exists.",
    "auth/invalid-email": "Please enter a valid email address.",
    "auth/weak-password": "Password must be at least 6 characters.",
    "auth/user-not-found": "No account found with that email.",
    "auth/wrong-password": "Incorrect password. Please try again.",
    "auth/invalid-credential": "Incorrect email or password. Please try again.",
    "auth/too-many-requests": "Too many attempts. Please try again later."
  };
  return messages[code] || "Something went wrong. Please try again.";
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/auth.js

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:06:33 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js`**
```js
import {
  onAuthStateChanged,
  signOut,
  updateProfile
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { auth } from "./firebase-config.js";

const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
const saveBtn = document.getElementById("save-btn");
const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");

// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
});

// --- Update display name ---
editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
  if (!newName) return;

  try {
    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
  } catch {
    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
  }
});

// --- Log out ---
logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
  await signOut(auth);
  window.location.href = "index.html";
});

function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
  statusMsg.textContent = message;
  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:06:47 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/auth.css`**
```css
*, *::before, *::after {
  box-sizing: border-box;
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
}

body {
  min-height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0d1b2a 0%, #0a3d4a 60%, #0d5c5c 100%);
  font-family: 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #e0f7f7;
}

.auth-card {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 2.5rem 2rem;
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 400px;
}

.auth-card h1 {
  text-align: center;
  font-size: 1.8rem;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  margin-bottom: 1.75rem;
  letter-spacing: -0.5px;
}

/* Tabs */
.tabs {
  display: flex;
  margin-bottom: 1.75rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
}

.tab-btn {
  flex: 1;
  background: none;
  border: none;
  padding: 0.6rem;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  color: #a7d8d4;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
  transition: color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
}

.tab-btn.active {
  color: #3dd6c8;
  border-bottom-color: #3dd6c8;
}

/* Forms */
.auth-form {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 1rem;
}

.auth-form.hidden {
  display: none;
}

label {
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  display: block;
  margin-bottom: 0.3rem;
}

input {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 0.65rem 0.85rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #e0f7f7;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  outline: none;
  transition: border-color 0.2s;
}

input:focus {
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

.submit-btn {
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
  padding: 0.7rem;
  background: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0d1b2a;
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 1rem;
  border: none;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s;
}

.submit-btn:hover {
  background: #5eead4;
}

/* Error */
.error-msg {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  color: #f87171;
  text-align: center;
  margin-top: 0.25rem;
}

.error-msg.hidden {
  display: none;
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:07:02 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/profile.css`**
```css
*, *::before, *::after {
  box-sizing: border-box;
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
}

body {
  min-height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0d1b2a 0%, #0a3d4a 60%, #0d5c5c 100%);
  font-family: 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #e0f7f7;
}

.profile-card {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 2.5rem 2rem;
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 440px;
}

.profile-card h1 {
  font-size: 1.8rem;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
  letter-spacing: -0.5px;
}

/* User info */
.user-info {
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}

.info-row {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 0.2rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.85rem;
}

.info-label {
  font-size: 0.8rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
}

.info-value {
  font-size: 1.05rem;
  color: #e0f7f7;
}

/* Edit form */
.edit-section h2 {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.edit-section {
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  padding-top: 1.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
}

.edit-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 0.75rem;
  align-items: center;
}

input {
  flex: 1;
  padding: 0.65rem 0.85rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #e0f7f7;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  outline: none;
  transition: border-color 0.2s;
}

input:focus {
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

.save-btn {
  padding: 0.65rem 1.1rem;
  background: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0d1b2a;
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  border: none;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.save-btn:hover {
  background: #5eead4;
}

/* Status message */
.status {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  min-height: 1.2rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.status.success { color: #4ade80; }
.status.error   { color: #f87171; }

/* Logout */
.logout-btn {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 0.7rem;
  background: transparent;
  color: #f87171;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  border: 1px solid rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.4);
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
}

.logout-btn:hover {
  background: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.1);
  border-color: #f87171;
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:07:10 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/login.html`**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — Sign In</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/auth.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <div class="auth-card">
    <h1>Tune-In</h1>

    <div class="tabs">
      <button class="tab-btn active" id="login-tab">Log In</button>
      <button class="tab-btn" id="signup-tab">Sign Up</button>
    </div>

    <!-- Log In Form -->
    <form class="auth-form" id="login-form">
      <div>
        <label for="login-email">Email</label>
        <input type="email" id="login-email" required placeholder="you@example.com" />
      </div>
      <div>
        <label for="login-password">Password</label>
        <input type="password" id="login-password" required placeholder="••••••••" />
      </div>
      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn">Log In</button>
    </form>

    <!-- Sign Up Form -->
    <form class="auth-form hidden" id="signup-form">
      <div>
        <label for="signup-name">Display Name</label>
        <input type="text" id="signup-name" required placeholder="Your name" />
      </div>
      <div>
        <label for="signup-email">Email</label>
        <input type="email" id="signup-email" required placeholder="you@example.com" />
      </div>
      <div>
        <label for="signup-password">Password</label>
        <input type="password" id="signup-password" required placeholder="Min. 6 characters" />
      </div>
      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn">Create Account</button>
    </form>

    <p class="error-msg hidden" id="auth-error"></p>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/auth.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — My Profile</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/profile.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <div class="profile-card">
    <h1>My Profile</h1>

    <div class="user-info">
      <div class="info-row">
        <span class="info-label">Display Name</span>
        <span class="info-value" id="display-name">—</span>
      </div>
      <div class="info-row">
        <span class="info-label">Email</span>
        <span class="info-value" id="user-email">—</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="edit-section">
      <h2>Edit Display Name</h2>
      <form id="edit-form">
        <div class="edit-row">
          <input type="text" id="edit-name" placeholder="New display name" required />
          <button type="submit" class="save-btn" id="save-btn">Save</button>
        </div>
      </form>
    </div>

    <p class="status" id="status-msg"></p>

    <button class="logout-btn" id="logout-btn">Log Out</button>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/profile.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

```js
1→import {
     2→  createUserWithEmailAndPassword,
     3→  signInWithEmailAndPassword,
     4→  signOut,
     5→  onAuthStateChanged,
     6→  updateProfile
     7→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     8→import { auth } from "./firebase-config.js";
     9→
    10→// --- Tab switching ---
    11→const loginTab = document.getElementById("login-tab");
    12→const signupTab = document.getElementById("signup-tab");
    13→const loginForm = document.getElementById("login-form");
    14→const signupForm = document.getElementById("signup-form");
    15→
    16→loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
    17→  loginTab.classList.add("active");
    18→  signupTab.classList.remove("active");
    19→  loginForm.classList.remove("hidden");
    20→  signupForm.classList.add("hidden");
    21→  clearError();
    22→});
    23→
    24→signupTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
    25→  signupTab.classList.add("active");
    26→  loginTab.classList.remove("active");
    27→  signupForm.classList.remove("hidden");
    28→  loginForm.classList.add("hidden");
    29→  clearError();
    30→});
    31→
    32→// --- Sign Up ---
    33→signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    34→  e.preventDefault();
    35→  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
    36→  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
    37→  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;
    38→
    39→  try {
    40→    const { user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    41→    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    42→    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    43→  } catch (err) {
    44→    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
    45→  }
    46→});
    47→
    48→// --- Log In ---
    49→loginForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    50→  e.preventDefault();
    51→  const email = document.getElementById("login-email").value.trim();
    52→  const password = document.getElementById("login-password").value;
    53→
    54→  try {
    55→    await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    56→    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    57→  } catch (err) {
    58→    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
    59→  }
    60→});
    61→
    62→// --- Redirect if already logged in ---
    63→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    64→  if (user) window.location.href = "profile.html";
    65→});
    66→
    67→// --- Helpers ---
    68→function showError(message) {
    69→  const el = document.getElementById("auth-error");
    70→  el.textContent = message;
    71→  el.classList.remove("hidden");
    72→}
    73→
    74→function clearError() {
    75→  const el = document.getElementById("auth-error");
    76→  el.textContent = "";
    77→  el.classList.add("hidden");
    78→}
    79→
    80→function friendlyError(code) {
    81→  const messages = {
    82→    "auth/email-already-in-use": "An account with that email already exists.",
    83→    "auth/invalid-email": "Please enter a valid email address.",
    84→    "auth/weak-password": "Password must be at least 6 characters.",
    85→    "auth/user-not-found": "No account found with that email.",
    86→    "auth/wrong-password": "Incorrect password. Please try again.",
    87→    "auth/invalid-credential": "Incorrect email or password. Please try again.",
    88→    "auth/too-many-requests": "Too many attempts. Please try again later."
    89→  };
    90→  return messages[code] || "Something went wrong. Please try again.";
    91→}
    92→
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:09:26 AM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js`**
```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     6→import { auth } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
     9→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    10→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    11→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    12→const saveBtn = document.getElementById("save-btn");
    13→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→
    16→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    17→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    18→  if (!user) {
    19→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    20→    return;
    21→  }
    22→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    23→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    24→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    25→});
    26→
    27→// --- Update display name ---
    28→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    29→  e.preventDefault();
    30→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    31→  if (!newName) return;
    32→
    33→  try {
    34→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    35→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    36→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    37→  } catch {
    38→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    39→  }
    40→});
    41→
    42→// --- Log out ---
    43→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    44→  await signOut(auth);
    45→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    46→});
    47→
    48→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    49→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    50→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    51→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    52→}
    53→
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:09:42 AM)*

Found a few issues. Here's the full breakdown:

---

**Bug 1 — `auth.js` redirect loop (serious)**
`js/auth.js:63` — `onAuthStateChanged` runs immediately when the page loads. When a user successfully signs up or logs in, Firebase fires the auth state change *before* `window.location.href = "profile.html"` finishes navigating. This can cause a redirect race. More importantly, if the user is already logged in and visits `login.html`, this will redirect them correctly — but the timing means it could also fire mid-login and interfere. The fix is to only redirect from `onAuthStateChanged` if the page isn't already in the middle of a form submission.

**Bug 2 — `saveBtn` is selected but never used (minor)**
`js/profile.js:12` — `const saveBtn = document.getElementById("save-btn")` is declared but never referenced anywhere in the file. Not a breaking bug, just dead code.

**Bug 3 — `profile.js` edit form can submit while `auth.currentUser` is `null` (serious)**
`js/profile.js:34` — The edit form listener is attached immediately, but `auth.currentUser` is `null` until Firebase resolves the auth state asynchronously. If someone submits the form very quickly before `onAuthStateChanged` fires, `updateProfile(null, ...)` will throw an unhandled error (the `catch` block has no parameter, which is valid JS but masks what went wrong). The fix is to gate the submit handler inside the `onAuthStateChanged` callback, or check `auth.currentUser` is non-null before proceeding.

**Bug 4 — Hidden signup form fields still validate (minor)**
`login.html:32-46` — The signup form has `required` on its inputs. When the login tab is active and the signup form has `.hidden` (which uses `display: none`), browsers will still block submission on the *login* form if required fields in the hidden signup form are empty in some older browsers. The safe fix is to add `disabled` to hidden form inputs when switching tabs, or use `novalidate` and handle validation manually.

---

Want me to fix all of these now?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 10:11:10 AM)*

Yes, please fix these. Why was there a redirect loop in the fist place? What does that mean exactly within the context of onAuthStateChanged?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:11:36 AM)*

Great question — let me explain first, then fix everything.

---

**Why the redirect loop happens:**

`onAuthStateChanged` is a *persistent listener*, not a one-time check. Firebase calls it in two situations:

1. **Immediately on page load** — Firebase checks localStorage for a saved session and fires the callback right away with either a `user` object or `null`.
2. **Any time auth state changes** — including right after a successful login or signup.

So when a user submits the login form, this happens:
1. `signInWithEmailAndPassword` resolves successfully
2. Our form's `try` block fires `window.location.href = "profile.html"` — redirect #1
3. Firebase also fires `onAuthStateChanged` because state just changed — our listener sees `user` is truthy and fires `window.location.href = "profile.html"` again — redirect #2

Two competing redirects race each other. It usually works by accident, but it's fragile. The deeper risk is if `profile.html` ever had a bug that kicked a user back to `login.html` — then `onAuthStateChanged` would immediately redirect them back to `profile.html`, creating a true infinite loop.

The fix is a simple `isSubmitting` flag so the listener backs off when a form submission is already handling the redirect.

---

Now fixing all four bugs:

```
const signupForm = document.getElementById("signup-form");

loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {

  signupTab.classList.remove("active");
  loginForm.classList.remove("hidden");
```

*Insert:*
```
// --- Tab switching ---
const loginTab = document.getElementById("login-tab");
const signupTab = document.getElementById("signup-tab");
const loginForm = document.getElementById("login-form");
const signupForm = document.getElementById("signup-form");

loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
  loginTab.classList.add("active");
  signupTab.classList.remove("active");
  loginForm.classList.remove("hidden");
  signupForm.classList.add("hidden");
  // Disable hidden form inputs so browser validation ignores them
  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = true);
  loginForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
  clearError();
});

signupTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
  signupTab.classList.add("active");
  loginTab.classList.remove("active");
  signupForm.classList.remove("hidden");
  loginForm.classList.add("hidden");
  loginForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = true);
  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
  clearError();
});
```

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/auth.js`**

*Remove:*

```
  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();

  const password = document.getElementById("login-password").value;

  try {

    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
```

*Insert:*
```
// Flag to prevent onAuthStateChanged from racing with form submission redirects
let isSubmitting = false;

// --- Sign Up ---
signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  isSubmitting = true;
  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;

  try {
    const { user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    isSubmitting = false;
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});

// --- Log In ---
loginForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  isSubmitting = true;
  const email = document.getElementById("login-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("login-password").value;

  try {
    await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    isSubmitting = false;
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});

// --- Redirect if already logged in ---
// Only fires when the page loads with an existing session, not during form submissions
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (user && !isSubmitting) window.location.href = "profile.html";
});
```

*Remove:*
```
// --- Update display name ---
editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
  if (!newName) return;

  try {
    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
  } catch {
    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
  }
});
```
*Insert:*
```
// --- Update display name ---
editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
  if (!newName) return;

  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    return;
  }

  try {
    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
  }
});
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:12:20 AM)*

All four bugs fixed and pushed. Quick summary of each change:

- **Bug 1** — Added `isSubmitting` flag. Form submissions set it to `true` before the async call, so when `onAuthStateChanged` fires a split second later, it sees the flag and stands down. The flag resets to `false` only if the submission *fails*, so the page-load redirect behavior is preserved.
- **Bug 2** — Removed the unused `saveBtn` variable in `profile.js`.
- **Bug 3** — Added a `if (!auth.currentUser)` guard before calling `updateProfile`, with a user-facing message if it triggers.
- **Bug 4** — Tab switching now toggles `disabled` on the hidden form's inputs, so the browser's built-in validation only applies to the active form.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:13:43 AM)*

Yes, but there's one prerequisite and one constraint to be aware of first:

**Prerequisite:** `js/firebase-config.js` still has placeholder values. Auth won't work until you fill in your real Firebase project config. This is the step I promised to walk you through — want to do that now so testing is actually possible?

**Constraint:** Because the scripts use `type="module"`, browsers block them when opened as a local `file://` URL due to CORS restrictions. You need a local server to serve the files over HTTP.

---

**Easiest way to run a local server:**

If you have VS Code, install the **Live Server** extension, then right-click `index.html` → *Open with Live Server*. It serves the project at `http://127.0.0.1:5500` automatically.

Alternatively, if you have Node installed:
```bash
npx serve .
```
Or Python:
```bash
python -m http.server 5500
```

---

**What to test once the server is running:**

| Test | Expected result |
|---|---|
| Click "Sign In" from landing page | Navigates to `login.html` |
| Submit login form with empty fields | Browser blocks submission |
| Submit signup with a new email | Redirects to `profile.html`, shows name + email |
| Revisit `login.html` while logged in | Immediately redirects to `profile.html` |
| Edit display name and save | Name updates on the page |
| Click Log Out | Redirects to `index.html` |
| Visit `profile.html` while logged out | Redirects to `login.html` |

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:14:03 AM)*

Here's the step-by-step walkthrough:

---

**Step 1 — Create a Firebase project**

1. Go to [https://console.firebase.google.com](https://console.firebase.google.com)
2. Click **"Add project"**
3. Name it `tune-in` (or whatever you'd like)
4. Disable Google Analytics when prompted (not needed for this project)
5. Click **"Create project"**

---

**Step 2 — Register a Web App**

1. From your project dashboard, click the **`</>`** (Web) icon
2. Give it a nickname like `tune-in-web`
3. Leave "Firebase Hosting" unchecked for now
4. Click **"Register app"**
5. You'll be shown a `firebaseConfig` object — **keep this page open**, you'll need it next

---

**Step 3 — Enable Email/Password Authentication**

1. In the left sidebar go to **Build → Authentication**
2. Click **"Get started"**
3. Under the **Sign-in method** tab, click **Email/Password**
4. Toggle **Enable** on, leave passwordless off
5. Click **Save**

---

**Step 4 — Fill in your config**

Open `js/firebase-config.js` and replace the placeholder values with the real ones from Step 2. It should look something like this when filled in:

```js
const firebaseConfig = {
  apiKey: "AIzaSy...",
  authDomain: "tune-in-xxxxx.firebaseapp.com",
  projectId: "tune-in-xxxxx",
  storageBucket: "tune-in-xxxxx.appspot.com",
  messagingSenderId: "123456789",
  appId: "1:123456789:web:abc123"
};
```

---

Once you've filled in the config, start your local server and you're ready to test. Let me know when you're at any step and I can help you through it.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 10:41:58 AM)*

Actually, good news — Firebase web config values are **safe to commit and make public**. Unlike a backend secret key, these values just identify your Firebase project to the SDK. Firebase's security model is designed around **Firebase Security Rules** (which we'll configure later), not by keeping the config secret.

This is different from something like a private server API key — Google intentionally designs Firebase web configs to be embedded in client-side code.

So go ahead and fill in `js/firebase-config.js` with your values, then let me know and we can commit it and move on to testing.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:04:56 AM)*

The second feature is **Reviews & Ratings** — writing reviews and assigning ratings to music. Here's my plan before writing anything:

**New pages:**
- `review-new.html` — form to submit a new review (artist, album/song title, rating, review text)
- `reviews.html` — lists all of the logged-in user's reviews

**New files:**
- `js/review-new.js` — handles form submission, writes to Firestore
- `js/reviews.js` — fetches and renders the user's reviews
- `css/reviews.css` — shared styles for both pages

**Firebase:**
- Add **Firestore** to the project (new database)
- Each review stored as a document in a `reviews` collection with fields: `userId`, `artist`, `title`, `rating` (1–5), `reviewText`, `createdAt`

**Updates:**
- `profile.html` — add a "My Reviews" link and a "Write a Review" button
- `js/firebase-config.js` — export Firestore alongside auth

**Rating system:** 1–5 stars, selected via clickable star buttons.

**Note:** For now, users enter the artist/album manually — no external music API yet. That could be a future enhancement.

A couple of questions before I proceed:
1. Should ratings be per **song**, per **album**, or **both** (user's choice)?

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css`**
```css
*, *::before, *::after {
  box-sizing: border-box;
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
}

body {
  min-height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: flex-start;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0d1b2a 0%, #0a3d4a 60%, #0d5c5c 100%);
  font-family: 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #e0f7f7;
  padding: 2.5rem 1rem;
}

/* ── Nav ── */
.top-nav {
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 560px;
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
  margin-bottom: 1.75rem;
}

.nav-logo {
  font-size: 1.2rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  text-decoration: none;
}

.nav-links {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1rem;
}

.nav-links a {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  color: #a7d8d4;
  text-decoration: none;
  transition: color 0.2s;
}

.nav-links a:hover {
  color: #3dd6c8;
}

/* ── Card ── */
.card {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 2rem;
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 560px;
}

.card h1 {
  font-size: 1.6rem;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
  letter-spacing: -0.5px;
}

/* ── Type toggle ── */
.type-toggle {
  display: flex;
  gap: 0.75rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.type-btn {
  flex: 1;
  padding: 0.55rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #a7d8d4;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
}

.type-btn.active {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  font-weight: 600;
}

/* ── Form fields ── */
.form-group {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 0.3rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.1rem;
}

label {
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
}

input, textarea {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 0.65rem 0.85rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #e0f7f7;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  font-family: inherit;
  outline: none;
  transition: border-color 0.2s;
  resize: vertical;
}

input:focus, textarea:focus {
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

/* ── Star rating ── */
.star-group {
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.stars {
  display: flex;
  gap: 0.4rem;
  margin-top: 0.3rem;
}

.star {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  font-size: 2rem;
  color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: color 0.15s, transform 0.1s;
  padding: 0;
  line-height: 1;
}

.star.selected,
.star.hovered {
  color: #3dd6c8;
}

.star:hover {
  transform: scale(1.15);
}

/* ── Submit ── */
.submit-btn {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 0.75rem;
  background: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0d1b2a;
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 1rem;
  border: none;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
  transition: background 0.2s;
}

.submit-btn:hover {
  background: #5eead4;
}

/* ── Status / error ── */
.status-msg {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  min-height: 1.2rem;
  margin-top: 0.75rem;
  text-align: center;
}

.status-msg.success { color: #4ade80; }
.status-msg.error   { color: #f87171; }

/* ── Review list ── */
.reviews-list {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 1rem;
}

.review-card {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
}

.review-header {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: flex-start;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}

.review-title {
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #e0f7f7;
}

.review-artist {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  margin-top: 0.1rem;
}

.review-meta {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: flex-end;
  gap: 0.25rem;
}

.review-stars {
  color: #3dd6c8;
  font-size: 1rem;
  letter-spacing: 1px;
}

.type-badge {
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  color: #0d1b2a;
  background: #3dd6c8;
  padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem;
  border-radius: 99px;
  font-weight: 700;
}

.review-text {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: #a7d8d4;
  line-height: 1.6;
  margin-top: 0.75rem;
}

.review-date {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  margin-top: 0.6rem;
}

.empty-state {
  text-align: center;
  color: #5eead4;
  padding: 2rem 0;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

.new-review-btn {
  display: inline-block;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
  padding: 0.6rem 1.25rem;
  background: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0d1b2a;
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  border-radius: 6px;
  text-decoration: none;
  transition: background 0.2s;
}

.new-review-btn:hover {
  background: #5eead4;
}
```

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — Write a Review</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/reviews.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <nav class="top-nav">
    <a href="index.html" class="nav-logo">Tune-In</a>
    <div class="nav-links">
      <a href="reviews.html">My Reviews</a>
      <a href="profile.html">Profile</a>
    </div>
  </nav>

  <div class="card">
    <h1>Write a Review</h1>

    <form id="review-form">
      <!-- Song or Album toggle -->
      <div class="type-toggle">
        <button type="button" class="type-btn active" data-type="song">Song</button>
        <button type="button" class="type-btn" data-type="album">Album</button>
      </div>
      <input type="hidden" id="review-type" value="song" />

      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="review-artist">Artist</label>
        <input type="text" id="review-artist" placeholder="Artist name" required />
      </div>

      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="review-title" id="title-label">Song Title</label>
        <input type="text" id="review-title" placeholder="Song title" required />
      </div>

      <div class="star-group">
        <label>Rating</label>
        <div class="stars" id="star-container">
          <button type="button" class="star" data-value="1">★</button>
          <button type="button" class="star" data-value="2">★</button>
          <button type="button" class="star" data-value="3">★</button>
          <button type="button" class="star" data-value="4">★</button>
          <button type="button" class="star" data-value="5">★</button>
        </div>
        <input type="hidden" id="review-rating" value="0" />
      </div>

      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="review-text">Review</label>
        <textarea id="review-text" rows="5" placeholder="Write your thoughts..."></textarea>
      </div>

      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn">Submit Review</button>
    </form>

    <p class="status-msg" id="status-msg"></p>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/review-new.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:09:46 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/review-new.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { collection, addDoc, serverTimestamp } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

// --- Auth guard ---
let currentUser = null;
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUser = user;
});

// --- Type toggle ---
const typeButtons = document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn");
const reviewTypeInput = document.getElementById("review-type");
const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
const titleInput = document.getElementById("review-title");

typeButtons.forEach((btn) => {
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    btn.classList.add("active");
    const selected = btn.dataset.type;
    reviewTypeInput.value = selected;
    titleLabel.textContent = selected === "song" ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    titleInput.placeholder = selected === "song" ? "Song title" : "Album title";
  });
});

// --- Star rating ---
const stars = document.querySelectorAll(".star");
const ratingInput = document.getElementById("review-rating");
let selectedRating = 0;

stars.forEach((star) => {
  star.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
    const val = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    stars.forEach(s => {
      s.classList.toggle("hovered", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= val);
    });
  });

  star.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
    stars.forEach(s => s.classList.remove("hovered"));
  });

  star.addEventListener("click", () => {
    selectedRating = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    ratingInput.value = selectedRating;
    stars.forEach(s => {
      s.classList.toggle("selected", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= selectedRating);
    });
  });
});

// --- Form submission ---
const form = document.getElementById("review-form");
const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");

form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();

  if (!currentUser) {
    showStatus("You must be logged in to submit a review.", true);
    return;
  }

  if (selectedRating === 0) {
    showStatus("Please select a star rating.", true);
    return;
  }

  const review = {
    userId: currentUser.uid,
    type: reviewTypeInput.value,
    artist: document.getElementById("review-artist").value.trim(),
    title: document.getElementById("review-title").value.trim(),
    rating: selectedRating,
    reviewText: document.getElementById("review-text").value.trim(),
    createdAt: serverTimestamp()
  };

  try {
    await addDoc(collection(db, "reviews"), review);
    window.location.href = "reviews.html";
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to submit review. Please try again.", true);
  }
});

function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
  statusMsg.textContent = message;
  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status-msg error" : "status-msg success";
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/review-new.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:10:02 AM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");

// --- Auth guard ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  await loadReviews(user.uid);
});

async function loadReviews(userId) {
  try {
    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    if (snapshot.empty) {
      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
      `;
      return;
    }

    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    const reviews = [];
    snapshot.forEach(doc => reviews.push({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
    reviews.sort((a, b) => {
      const aTime = a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
      const bTime = b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
      return bTime - aTime;
    });

    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => `
      <div class="review-card">
        <div class="review-header">
          <div>
            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
          </div>
          <div class="review-meta">
            <span class="type-badge">${review.type}</span>
            <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(review.rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - review.rating)}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
        <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
      </div>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js

```js
    18→const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
    19→const titleInput = document.getElementById("review-title");
    20→
    21→typeButtons.forEach((btn) => {
    22→  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    23→    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    24→    btn.classList.add("active");
    25→    const selected = btn.dataset.type;
    26→    reviewTypeInput.value = selected;
    27→    titleLabel.textContent = selected === "song" ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    28→    titleInput.placeholder = selected === "song" ? "Song title" : "Album title";
    29→  });
    30→});
    31→
    32→// --- Star rating ---
    33→const stars = document.querySelectorAll(".star");
    34→const ratingInput = document.getElementById("review-rating");
    35→let selectedRating = 0;
    36→
    37→stars.forEach((star) => {
    38→  star.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
    39→    const val = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    40→    stars.forEach(s => {
    41→      s.classList.toggle("hovered", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= val);
    42→    });
    43→  });
    44→
    45→  star.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
    46→    stars.forEach(s => s.classList.remove("hovered"));
    47→  });
    48→
    49→  star.addEventListener("click", () => {
    50→    selectedRating = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    51→    ratingInput.value = selectedRating;
    52→    stars.forEach(s => {
    53→      s.classList.toggle("selected", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= selectedRating);
    54→    });
    55→  });
    56→});
    57→
    58→// --- Form submission ---
    59→const form = document.getElementById("review-form");
    60→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    61→
    62→form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    63→  e.preventDefault();
    64→
    65→  if (!currentUser) {
    66→    showStatus("You must be logged in to submit a review.", true);
    67→    return;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  if (selectedRating === 0) {
    71→    showStatus("Please select a star rating.", true);
    72→    return;
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  const review = {
    76→    userId: currentUser.uid,
    77→    type: reviewTypeInput.value,
    78→    artist: document.getElementById("review-artist").value.trim(),
    79→    title: document.getElementById("review-title").value.trim(),
    80→    rating: selectedRating,
    81→    reviewText: document.getElementById("review-text").value.trim(),
    82→    createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    83→  };
    84→
    85→  try {
    86→    await addDoc(collection(db, "reviews"), review);
    87→    window.location.href = "reviews.html";
    88→  } catch (err) {
    89→    showStatus("Failed to submit review. Please try again.", true);
    90→  }
    91→});
    92→
    93→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    94→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    95→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status-msg error" : "status-msg success";
    96→}
    97→
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:36:49 AM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
     6→
     7→// --- Auth guard ---
     8→onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  await loadReviews(user.uid);
    14→});
    15→
    16→async function loadReviews(userId) {
    17→  try {
    18→    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    19→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    20→
    21→    if (snapshot.empty) {
    22→      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
    23→        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
    24→      `;
    25→      return;
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    29→    const reviews = [];
    30→    snapshot.forEach(doc => reviews.push({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
    31→    reviews.sort((a, b) => {
    32→      const aTime = a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    33→      const bTime = b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    34→      return bTime - aTime;
    35→    });
    36→
    37→    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => `
    38→      <div class="review-card">
    39→        <div class="review-header">
    40→          <div>
    41→            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    42→            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    43→          </div>
    44→          <div class="review-meta">
    45→            <span class="type-badge">${review.type}</span>
    46→            <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(review.rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - review.rating)}</span>
    47→          </div>
    48→        </div>
    49→        ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    50→        <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
    51→      </div>
    52→    `).join("");
    53→
    54→  } catch (err) {
    55→    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
    56→  }
    57→}
    58→
    59→function formatDate(timestamp) {
    60→  if (!timestamp) return "";
    61→  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
    62→  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
    63→}
    64→
    65→function escapeHtml(str = "") {
    66→  return str
    67→    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    68→    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    69→    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    70→    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
    71→}
    72→
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:37:19 AM)*

Found several issues across both files. Here's the full breakdown:

---

**Bug 1 — Double-submit possible (`review-new.js:62`)**
There's no loading state on the submit button. If Firestore is slow, a user can click "Submit Review" multiple times and write duplicate reviews to the database. The button needs to be disabled immediately on submit and re-enabled if an error occurs.

**Bug 2 — `review.type` rendered without escaping (`reviews.js:45`)**
`review.type` is injected directly into `innerHTML` without going through `escapeHtml`. Since it comes from Firestore data, it should be treated as untrusted. All other fields go through `escapeHtml` — this one was missed.

**Bug 3 — `review.rating` not validated before `.repeat()` (`reviews.js:46`)**
`"★".repeat(review.rating)` will throw a `RangeError` if `review.rating` is somehow `null`, `NaN`, or a negative number (e.g. from a malformed Firestore document). It needs to be clamped to a safe 0–5 range first.

**Bug 4 — Star `mouseleave` flickers (`review-new.js:45`)**
`mouseleave` is attached to each individual star. When you move your cursor between stars, the event fires on the star you're leaving before it fires on the star you're entering — causing a brief flash where all stars are unhighlighted. The `mouseleave` should be on the container instead of individual stars.

**Minor — `id="star-container"` is declared but never used (`review-new.html:41`)**
The container has an ID that nothing in the JS references. It was intended for the `mouseleave` fix above.

---

All four are worth fixing — Bug 1 and 4 are the most noticeable to a user. Want me to fix them all now?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:40:53 AM)*

Yes, fix them all. Please elaborate on the RangeError

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:41:02 AM)*

**On the RangeError:**

`String.prototype.repeat()` requires its argument to be a non-negative finite integer. If it receives anything else, JavaScript throws a `RangeError: Invalid count value`. Here's how it could happen:

- A Firestore document gets written with a missing or corrupted `rating` field → `review.rating` is `undefined` → `"★".repeat(undefined)` → `repeat(0)` actually works here, but `"★".repeat(null)` also coerces to 0... however `"★".repeat(NaN)` is fine too. The real danger is if `rating` ends up as a **negative number** or **Infinity**, both of which throw immediately.
- More practically: if a future feature (like an edit review form) has a bug that writes a bad value, or if someone manually inserts a document into Firestore with `rating: -1`, every single call to `loadReviews` would crash and the entire reviews list would fail to render — not just that one card.

Clamping to `Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0))` makes it bulletproof regardless of what's in the database.

---

Now fixing all four:

---

```
// --- Star rating ---
const starContainer = document.getElementById("star-container");
const stars = document.querySelectorAll(".star");
const ratingInput = document.getElementById("review-rating");
let selectedRating = 0;

stars.forEach((star) => {
  star.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
    const val = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    stars.forEach(s => {
      s.classList.toggle("hovered", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= val);
    });
  });

  star.addEventListener("click", () => {
    selectedRating = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    ratingInput.value = selectedRating;
    stars.forEach(s => {
      s.classList.toggle("selected", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= selectedRating);
    });
  });
});

// mouseleave on the container prevents flickering between individual stars
starContainer.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
  stars.forEach(s => s.classList.remove("hovered"));
});
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:41:40 AM)*

All fixed and pushed. Quick summary of each change:

- **Double-submit** — Button is disabled and changes to "Submitting..." the moment the form is submitted. It only re-enables if the Firestore write fails.
- **Star flicker** — Removed `mouseleave` from individual stars entirely. It now lives only on the container, so it fires once when the cursor actually leaves the star group rather than between each star.
- **Unescaped `review.type`** — Now passed through `escapeHtml` like every other field.
- **RangeError** — Rating is clamped with `Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0))` before being passed to `.repeat()`. Any bad value from Firestore will safely render as 0 stars instead of crashing the entire list.

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:42:36 AM)*

What is the optimal way to test these chnages?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:42:46 AM)*

Same process as before — run your local server and test these specific scenarios:

**Double-submit fix:**
- Go to "Write a Review", fill out the form, and click "Submit Review"
- The button should immediately change to "Submitting..." and become unclickable while it writes to Firestore
- You should only land on `reviews.html` once with one review added

**Star flicker fix:**
- On the review form, hover slowly across the stars left to right and right to left
- Stars should highlight smoothly without any flash of all stars going dark between each one

**RangeError fix:**
- This one can't be triggered through the UI since the form enforces a valid 1–5 rating — it's a defensive guard against bad database data. You can test it manually by going into your **Firebase console → Firestore → reviews collection**, opening one of your documents, and temporarily changing the `rating` field to `-1` or deleting it entirely, then reloading `reviews.html`. It should render 0 stars instead of crashing the page.

**Unescaped type badge:**
- Similarly hard to trigger through normal UI. In Firestore you could manually set `type` to something like `<b>test</b>` on a document and confirm it renders as literal text on `reviews.html` rather than bold HTML.

The star flicker and double-submit are the ones you'll notice most immediately. Want to run through them?

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:45:07 AM)*

The review is taking a long time to submit

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:45:15 AM)*

Check the browser console for any errors while it's hanging — that will tell us exactly what's happening. A few likely causes:

1. **Firestore wasn't enabled yet** — did you get a chance to complete the Firestore setup step (Create database → test mode) from earlier?
2. **Security rules blocking the write** — if it wasn't started in test mode, rules may be rejecting the request silently before timing out
3. **Network/console error** — there may be a permission or configuration error we can see

Open the console (`F12 → Console`) and paste any red errors you see here.

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:45:50 AM)*

I did not enable test mode. How do I implement that again?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:45:56 AM)*

1. Go to your Firebase console → open your **tune-in** project
2. In the left sidebar click **Firestore Database**
3. Click the **Rules** tab at the top
4. Replace whatever is there with this:

```
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    match /{document=**} {
      allow read, write: if true;
    }
  }
}
```

5. Click **Publish**

This opens all reads and writes, which is fine for development and testing. We'll lock it down with proper security rules later when the project is closer to done.

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:47:22 AM)*

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:47:28 AM)*

That means Firestore hasn't been created yet. Here's how to set it up:

1. In the left sidebar click **Firestore Database**
2. Click **"Create database"**
3. When prompted for security rules, select **"Start in test mode"**
4. Click **Next**, select a server location closest to you (e.g. `us-east1`), then click **"Done"**

Once it finishes provisioning (takes about 30 seconds), the Rules tab will appear and test mode will already be set. Then try submitting a review again.

```js
     8→onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  await loadReviews(user.uid);
    14→});
    15→
    16→async function loadReviews(userId) {
    17→  try {
    18→    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    19→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    20→
    21→    if (snapshot.empty) {
    22→      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
    23→        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
    24→      `;
    25→      return;
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    29→    const reviews = [];
    30→    snapshot.forEach(doc => reviews.push({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
    31→    reviews.sort((a, b) => {
    32→      const aTime = a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    33→      const bTime = b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    34→      return bTime - aTime;
    35→    });
    36→
    37→    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    38→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    39→      return `
    40→        <div class="review-card">
    41→          <div class="review-header">
    42→            <div>
    43→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    44→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    45→            </div>
    46→            <div class="review-meta">
    47→              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
    48→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    49→            </div>
    50→          </div>
    51→          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    52→          <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
    53→        </div>
    54→      `;
    55→    }).join("");
    56→
    57→  } catch (err) {
    58→    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
    59→  }
    60→}
    61→
    62→function formatDate(timestamp) {
    63→  if (!timestamp) return "";
    64→  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
    65→  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
    66→}
    67→
    68→function escapeHtml(str = "") {
    69→  return str
    70→    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    71→    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    72→    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    73→    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
    74→}
    75→
```

```
// --- Delete via event delegation ---
reviewsList.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  const btn = e.target.closest(".delete-btn");
  if (!btn) return;

  if (!confirm("Delete this review? This cannot be undone.")) return;

  const reviewId = btn.dataset.id;
  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Deleting...";

  try {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "reviews", reviewId));
    const card = reviewsList.querySelector(`.review-card[data-id="${reviewId}"]`);
    card.remove();

    if (reviewsList.querySelectorAll(".review-card").length === 0) {
      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
      `;
    }
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Delete";
  }
});
```

- Each review card now has a **Delete** button in the bottom-right corner
- Clicking it shows a confirmation prompt before doing anything

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:53:33 AM)*

Found two issues:

---

**Bug 1 — `doc` variable shadowing (`reviews.js:30`)**
This is the most important one. On line 2 we import `doc` from Firestore — this is the function used on line 77 to build the document reference for `deleteDoc`. But on line 30, the `forEach` callback also uses `doc` as its parameter name, which shadows the imported `doc` within that scope:

```js
snapshot.forEach(doc => reviews.push({ id: doc.id, ...doc.data() }));
//               ^^^
//               This shadows the imported `doc` function inside the callback
```

It doesn't crash right now because the shadowing only applies inside the `forEach` callback, and `deleteDoc(doc(...))` is called outside it. But it's a latent bug — if anyone adds code using `doc()` inside that `forEach`, it will silently call the callback parameter instead of the Firestore function, producing a confusing error. The fix is simply renaming the callback parameter to `docSnap`.

**Bug 2 — `.new-review-btn` is dead CSS (`reviews.css:291`)**
This class was written in anticipation of a button that was never added to any HTML. It's unused and should be removed to keep the stylesheet clean.

---

Want me to fix both now?

*Insert:*

**User** *(3/13/2026, 11:55:30 AM)*

Before we move on, let's walk through API security. Do I need to leverage .gitignore to ensure it is secure?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:55:45 AM)*

Good question. The short answer is: **not for Firebase specifically, but it's worth understanding why.**

---

**Why your Firebase config is safe to commit**

Firebase web config values (`apiKey`, `authDomain`, etc.) are **not secret credentials**. They're identifiers that tell the Firebase SDK which project to connect to — similar to a public username. Google explicitly designs them to be embedded in client-side code that anyone can see.

The actual security layer is **Firebase Security Rules**, not the config values. Even if someone copies your `apiKey`, they can only interact with your database within the boundaries your rules define. Right now your rules are wide open (test mode), which is fine for development — but before you go live, we'll need to tighten them so users can only read/write their own data.

---

**When you DO need `.gitignore`**

You'd use `.gitignore` to protect secrets that grant privileged access outside of Firebase's rules system, such as:

- A **Firebase Admin SDK service account key** (a JSON file used in backend/server code that bypasses all security rules entirely)
- A **third-party API key** — e.g. if you later integrate a music API like Spotify or Last.fm, those keys would be secret and should never be committed
- Any **`.env` file** containing sensitive values

Your `.gitignore` already covers `.env`, so you're protected on that front. The Firebase client config in `firebase-config.js` is fine as-is.

---

**The one thing to address before going live**

Tightening your Firestore security rules. Right now they allow anyone to read and write anything. Before the project is done, we'll update them to something like:

```
allow read, write: if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;
```

This ensures users can only access their own data. Want to handle that now, or save it for when we're closer to finishing the features?

---

```md
1→# Tune-In
     2→
     3→An online web platform that allows users to create personalized accounts to review and rate music they have listened to. The platform functions similarly to a social media experience — think Letterboxd, but for music. Users will be able to connect with each other, share music and reviews, and build community.
     4→
     5→## Key Features
     6→
     7→- **User Accounts** — Create and manage a personalized profile to track your music reviews and listening history.
     8→- **Reviews & Ratings** — Write reviews and assign ratings to music you have listened to.
     9→- **Social Interaction** — Search for other user profiles, follow them, and interact with their reviews.
    10→- **Review Lists** — Organize your reviews into curated lists for easy browsing and sharing.
    11→- **Listening Queue** — Build a queue of songs and albums you have yet to review, so you never lose track of what's next.
    12→
    13→## Additional Planned Features
    14→
    15→- Dark mode
    16→- Advanced filtering
    17→
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 11:58:38 AM)*

Here's the full breakdown of remaining work:

---

## Feature 3 — Social Interaction
This is the most complex feature and has a required prerequisite first.

**Prerequisite — Firestore user documents**
Currently, user data only lives in Firebase Auth. For search and public profiles to work, each user needs a Firestore document. This needs to be created at signup before anything else in this feature can be built.

- **3a** — On signup, write a user document to a `users` collection (`uid`, `displayName`, `email`, `createdAt`)
- **3b** — User search page: search by display name, render matching user cards
- **3c** — Public profile page: view another user's display name and their reviews
- **3d** — Follow/unfollow: store follower relationships in Firestore, show follow button on public profiles
- **3e** — Following feed: a page showing recent reviews from users you follow

---

## Feature 4 — Review Lists
- **4a** — Create a named list (stored in Firestore under the user's account)
- **4b** — Add an existing review to a list from the reviews page
- **4c** — View a list page showing all reviews in it
- **4d** — Remove a review from a list / delete a list entirely

---

## Feature 5 — Listening Queue
- **5a** — Add a song or album to your queue (artist, title, type — no review yet)
- **5b** — View your queue as a list
- **5c** — Remove an item from the queue
- **5d** — "Review now" shortcut: clicking it on a queue item pre-fills the review form and removes it from the queue on submission

---

## Additional Features
- **Dark mode** — CSS custom properties (variables) for the color palette, a toggle button that switches a class on `<body>`, preference saved to `localStorage`
- **Advanced filtering** — Filter your reviews page by type (song/album), rating, and date range

---

## Responsive Design Pass
Rather than retrofitting responsiveness at the end, I'd recommend doing a **responsive pass after each feature** — testing at mobile widths (375px) and tablet widths (768px) and adjusting layouts as we go. A final polish pass at the end for consistency across all pages.

---

## Security Rules
Before the project is considered done, tighten Firestore rules so users can only read/write their own data. This should be done after Feature 3 is complete since social features require some public read access.

---

**Suggested build order:**
`3a → 3b → 3c → 3d → 3e → 4a → 4b → 4c → 4d → 5a → 5b → 5c → 5d → Dark mode → Advanced filtering → Security rules → Final responsive polish`

Want to start with 3a?

---

```js
    16→loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {

    18→  signupTab.classList.remove("active");

    20→  signupForm.classList.add("hidden");
    21→  // Disable hidden form inputs so browser validation ignores them
    22→  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = true);
    23→  loginForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
    24→  clearError();
    25→});
    26→
    27→signupTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
    28→  signupTab.classList.add("active");
    29→  loginTab.classList.remove("active");
    30→  signupForm.classList.remove("hidden");
    31→  loginForm.classList.add("hidden");

    33→  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
    34→  clearError();
    35→});
    36→
    37→// Flag to prevent onAuthStateChanged from racing with form submission redirects
    38→let isSubmitting = false;
    39→
    40→// --- Sign Up ---
    41→signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    42→  e.preventDefault();
    43→  isSubmitting = true;
    44→  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();

    57→
    58→// --- Log In ---

    60→  e.preventDefault();

    96→    "auth/invalid-email": "Please enter a valid email address.",
```

```
    const { user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
```

```
      displayName,
      email,
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:03:55 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js`**
```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     6→import { auth } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
     9→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    10→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    11→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    12→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    13→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    14→
    15→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    16→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    17→  if (!user) {
    18→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    19→    return;
    20→  }
    21→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    22→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    23→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    24→});
    25→
    26→// --- Update display name ---
    27→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    28→  e.preventDefault();
    29→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    30→  if (!newName) return;
    31→
    32→  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
    33→  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    34→    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    35→    return;
    36→  }
    37→
    38→  try {
    39→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    40→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    41→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    42→  } catch (err) {
    43→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    44→  }
    45→});
    46→
    47→// --- Log out ---
    48→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    49→  await signOut(auth);
    50→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    51→});
    52→
    53→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    54→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    55→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    56→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    57→}
    58→
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:04:52 PM)*

Done and pushed. Here's a summary of what changed:

**3a — Firestore user documents:**
- On signup, a document is written to the `users` collection using the user's UID as the document ID, storing `uid`, `displayName`, `email`, and `createdAt`
- When a user edits their display name on the profile page, both Firebase Auth and the Firestore `users` document are updated in sync — this is important for 3b/3c when other users need to search and display names

**Responsive pass:**
- **Landing page** — logo scales down from `4rem` to `2.75rem` on mobile, tighter padding
- **Login page** — body shifts to top-aligned on mobile with padding, card padding reduced, heading scales down
- **Profile page** — same body adjustment, edit row (input + save button) stacks vertically on small screens
- **Reviews pages** — card padding reduced, review header stacks vertically so title/artist sit above the badge/stars, stars slightly smaller on mobile

Ready to move on to 3b — user search?

```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";

     8→
     9→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
    10→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    11→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    12→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    13→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→
    16→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    17→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    18→  if (!user) {
    19→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    20→    return;
    21→  }
    22→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    23→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    24→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    25→});
    26→
    27→// --- Update display name ---
    28→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    29→  e.preventDefault();
    30→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    31→  if (!newName) return;
    32→
    33→  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
    34→  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    35→    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    36→    return;
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  try {
    40→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    41→    await updateDoc(doc(db, "users", auth.currentUser.uid), { displayName: newName });
    42→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    43→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    44→  } catch (err) {
    45→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    46→  }
    47→});
    48→
    49→// --- Log out ---
    50→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    51→  await signOut(auth);
    52→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    53→});
    54→
    55→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    56→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    57→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    58→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    59→}
    60→
```
