**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:10:42 PM)*

Found two real bugs and two minor issues.

---

**Bug 1 — Partial signup leaves a broken account (`auth.js:50-57`, serious)**

Signup does three async steps in sequence:
1. `createUserWithEmailAndPassword` — creates the Auth user
2. `updateProfile` — sets the display name
3. `setDoc` — writes the Firestore document

If step 2 or 3 throws, the `catch` block only resets `isSubmitting` and shows an error. But the Auth user was already created in step 1. The user is now stuck — they have a half-built account with no Firestore document, and the next signup attempt will return `auth/email-already-in-use`. The fix is to delete the Auth user if the subsequent steps fail.

**Bug 2 — `updateDoc` fails for existing users (`profile.js:41`, serious)**

`updateDoc` requires the document to already exist. Any account created before 3a was added has no Firestore user document. Attempting to edit their display name will silently throw in the `catch` block and show "Failed to update name." The fix is to use `setDoc` with `{ merge: true }`, which acts as an upsert — creating the document if it doesn't exist, updating it if it does.

**Minor 1 — No loading state on signup button (`auth.js:42`)**
The signup button can be clicked multiple times while the three async steps are running. Should be disabled on submit and re-enabled on error, same pattern as the review form.

**Minor 2 — No loading state on profile save button (`profile.js:28`)**
Same issue — save button can be double-clicked during the async update.

---

Fixing all four now:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:10:48 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/auth.js`**

*Remove:*
```
// --- Sign Up ---
signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  isSubmitting = true;
  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;

  try {
    const { user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", user.uid), {
      uid: user.uid,
      displayName,
      email,
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    isSubmitting = false;
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});
```
*Insert:*
```
// --- Sign Up ---
signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  isSubmitting = true;

  const submitBtn = signupForm.querySelector(".submit-btn");
  submitBtn.disabled = true;
  submitBtn.textContent = "Creating account...";

  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;

  let user = null;
  try {
    ({ user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password));
    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", user.uid), {
      uid: user.uid,
      displayName,
      email,
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
  } catch (err) {
    // If Auth user was created but a later step failed, delete the partial account
    if (user) await user.delete().catch(() => {});
    isSubmitting = false;
    submitBtn.disabled = false;
    submitBtn.textContent = "Create Account";
    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
  }
});
```

```
  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    return;
  }

  const saveBtn = editForm.querySelector(".save-btn");
  saveBtn.disabled = true;
  saveBtn.textContent = "Saving...";

  try {
    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    // setDoc with merge:true acts as upsert — safe for accounts created before Firestore docs were added
    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", auth.currentUser.uid), { displayName: newName }, { merge: true });
    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
  } finally {
    saveBtn.disabled = false;
    saveBtn.textContent = "Save";
  }
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:11:21 PM)*

All fixed and pushed. Here's what changed:

- **Partial signup cleanup** — `user` is declared outside the try block. If `updateProfile` or `setDoc` throws after the Auth user was already created, the catch block calls `user.delete()` to remove the orphaned account before showing the error. The `.catch(() => {})` on delete ensures that if the cleanup itself fails, it doesn't mask the original error.
- **Upsert instead of update** — `setDoc` with `{ merge: true }` replaces `updateDoc`. It creates the document if it doesn't exist (for legacy accounts) and merges the new field if it does, so it's safe in all cases.
- **Loading states** — Signup button disables and shows "Creating account..." during the three-step process. Save button disables and shows "Saving..." during the update, then re-enables via `finally` so it always resets regardless of success or failure.

**To test:**
- Try signing up with a new account — check Firebase console Firestore → `users` collection to confirm the document was created
- On your profile page, edit your display name — confirm it updates both in the UI and in the Firestore document
- To test the responsive changes, open DevTools (`F12`) → toggle device toolbar (`Ctrl+Shift+M`) and check at 375px width across all pages

Ready for 3b when you are.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:43:19 PM)*

Good picture of the current state. Here's my plan for 3b before writing:

- Search queries Firestore's `users` collection using a **prefix range query** on display name. To make it case-insensitive, I'll store a `displayNameLower` field alongside `displayName` — this requires a small update to `auth.js` and `profile.js`
- Results are debounced as the user types (300ms) so Firestore isn't hit on every keystroke
- Current user is excluded from results
- Each result links to `public-profile.html?uid=...` (built in 3c)
- Search link added to all page navs

Writing everything now:

```
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });
```

```
    });
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:43:52 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/search.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const searchForm = document.getElementById("search-form");
const searchInput = document.getElementById("search-input");
const resultsContainer = document.getElementById("search-results");

let currentUserId = null;

// --- Auth guard ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUserId = user.uid;
});

// --- Debounced search as user types ---
let debounceTimer;
searchInput.addEventListener("input", () => {
  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
  debounceTimer = setTimeout(runSearch, 300);
});

// --- Also run on explicit submit ---
searchForm.addEventListener("submit", (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
  runSearch();
});

async function runSearch() {
  const term = searchInput.value.trim().toLowerCase();

  if (!term) {
    resultsContainer.innerHTML = "";
    return;
  }

  resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Searching...</p>`;

  try {
    // Prefix range query on displayNameLower for case-insensitive matching
    const q = query(
      collection(db, "users"),
      where("displayNameLower", ">=", term),
      where("displayNameLower", "<=", term + "\uf8ff")
    );

    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    const users = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) users.push(docSnap.data());
    });

    if (users.length === 0) {
      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
      return;
    }

    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
      </a>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Search failed. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/search.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:44:40 PM)*

Done and pushed. Here's a summary:

**3b — User Search:**
- `search.html` / `js/search.js` — search page with a debounced input that queries Firestore as you type, plus explicit submit support
- Case-insensitive search via a `displayNameLower` field — both `auth.js` (on signup) and `profile.js` (on name update) now store this alongside `displayName`
- Results render as clickable cards pointing to `public-profile.html?uid=...` (ready for 3c)
- Current user is excluded from results
- "Search" link added to the nav on all pages

**One important note for testing:** Any existing accounts created before this change won't have `displayNameLower` in their Firestore document, so they won't appear in search results. The fix is to log in with those accounts and save a display name update from the profile page — that will write `displayNameLower` via the updated `setDoc`. Alternatively, you can manually add the field in the Firebase console.

Ready for 3c — the public profile page?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:46:18 PM)*

Found three issues worth fixing and two minor notes.

---

**Bug 1 — Stale search results race condition (`search.js:34`, moderate)**
If the user types quickly, multiple Firestore requests fire in close succession despite the debounce. If a slow earlier request resolves *after* a faster later one, it overwrites the correct results with stale ones. For example: type "j", then quickly type "jo" — the "j" query might resolve last and replace the "jo" results. The fix is a request counter that ignores any response that isn't from the most recent query.

**Bug 2 — `currentUserId` can be null during early searches (`search.js:9`, minor)**
`onAuthStateChanged` resolves asynchronously. If the user starts typing before Firebase confirms the session, `currentUserId` is still `null` and the current user won't be filtered from results. The fix is to disable the search input until auth resolves, then enable it.

**Bug 3 — Input width conflicts with flex layout (`reviews.css:110`, minor)**
The global `input` rule sets `width: 100%`. Inside `.search-row` (a flex container), this means the input has `flex-basis: 100%` but no explicit `flex: 1` or `min-width: 0`. This works by accident in most browsers due to flex shrinking, but is technically incorrect and can produce unexpected sizing on some browsers. The search input needs `flex: 1; min-width: 0` explicitly.

---

**Minor — `search.html` nav is missing a "Search" link**
Every other page has "Search" in its nav, but `search.html` doesn't link to itself. Minor inconsistency — not a bug, but worth making consistent.

**Minor — `.empty-state` is defined after the `@media` block in `reviews.css`**
Styles should be defined before media queries that override them. Not a functional issue today, but poor organization that could cause confusion when overriding later.

---

Want me to fix all of these?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 12:46:54 PM)*

Yes, it's important to take note of rare edge cases. Let's fix and then test

```
    return;
  }

  resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Searching...</p>`;

  try {
    // Prefix range query on displayNameLower for case-insensitive matching
    const q = query(
      collection(db, "users"),
      where("displayNameLower", ">=", term),
      where("displayNameLower", "<=", term + "\uf8ff")
    );

    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    const users = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) users.push(docSnap.data());
    });

    if (users.length === 0) {
      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
      return;
    }

    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
      </a>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Search failed. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}
```
*Insert:*
```
let currentUserId = null;
let requestCount = 0;

// --- Auth guard: disable input until session is confirmed ---
searchInput.disabled = true;
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUserId = user.uid;
  searchInput.disabled = false;
  searchInput.focus();
});

// --- Debounced search as user types ---
let debounceTimer;
searchInput.addEventListener("input", () => {
  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
  debounceTimer = setTimeout(runSearch, 300);
});

// --- Also run on explicit submit ---
searchForm.addEventListener("submit", (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
  runSearch();
});

async function runSearch() {
  const term = searchInput.value.trim().toLowerCase();

  if (!term) {
    resultsContainer.innerHTML = "";
    return;
  }

  // Track this request — discard results if a newer one has started
  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
  resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Searching...</p>`;

  try {
    const q = query(
      collection(db, "users"),
      where("displayNameLower", ">=", term),
      where("displayNameLower", "<=", term + "\uf8ff")
    );

    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    // Discard stale responses
    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;

    const users = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) users.push(docSnap.data());
    });

    if (users.length === 0) {
      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
      return;
    }

    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
      </a>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Search failed. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:48:08 PM)*

All fixed and pushed. Here's what changed:

- **Stale results** — `requestCount` increments on every search call. When a response comes back, it checks `thisRequest !== requestCount` and silently discards it if a newer request has since fired. Same check applied in the catch block so stale errors don't overwrite fresh results either.
- **Auth null guard** — Input is disabled on page load and only enabled once `onAuthStateChanged` confirms a valid session, so `currentUserId` is guaranteed to be set before any search can run.
- **Input flex sizing** — `.search-row input` now explicitly sets `flex: 1; min-width: 0`, overriding the global `width: 100%` and giving the input correct, predictable behavior inside the flex row.
- **CSS ordering** — `.empty-state` moved above the media query block where it belongs.
- **Nav consistency** — "Search" link added to `search.html`'s own nav.

**To test:**
- Open `search.html` — input should be briefly disabled, then enabled once Firebase resolves
- Type a partial name quickly — results should reflect the final typed value, not an earlier one
- Search for your own display name — your account should not appear in results
- Check the search row layout at 375px width in DevTools — input and button should sit cleanly side by side

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:52:18 PM)*

Step 3c is the public profile page. Here's the plan before writing:

- Read `uid` from the URL query string
- Fetch the user's document from the `users` collection to display their name
- Fetch their reviews from the `reviews` collection and render them read-only (no delete button)
- Handle missing/invalid `uid` gracefully with an error state
- Auth guard — must be logged in to view
- Reuse `reviews.css` for consistent styling
- A follow button placeholder will be present but wired up in 3d

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:52:42 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/public-profile.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const profileNameEl = document.getElementById("profile-name");
const profileSubtext = document.getElementById("profile-subtext");
const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");

// Read uid from URL query string
const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
const profileUid = params.get("uid");

// --- Auth guard ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }

  if (!profileUid) {
    showError("No user specified.");
    return;
  }

  // Prevent users from viewing their own public profile — redirect to profile page
  if (profileUid === user.uid) {
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    return;
  }

  await loadProfile(profileUid);
});

async function loadProfile(uid) {
  try {
    // Fetch user document
    const userSnap = await getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid));

    if (!userSnap.exists()) {
      showError("User not found.");
      return;
    }

    const userData = userSnap.data();
    profileNameEl.textContent = userData.displayName || "Unknown User";
    document.title = `Tune-In — ${userData.displayName || "User"}`;

    // Fetch their reviews
    await loadReviews(uid, userData.displayName);

  } catch (err) {
    showError("Failed to load profile. Please try again.");
  }
}

async function loadReviews(uid, displayName) {
  try {
    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", uid));
    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    if (snapshot.empty) {
      reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">${escapeHtml(displayName)} hasn't written any reviews yet.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    const reviews = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    reviews.sort((a, b) => {
      const aTime = a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
      const bTime = b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
      return bTime - aTime;
    });

    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
      return `
        <div class="review-card">
          <div class="review-header">
            <div>
              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
            </div>
            <div class="review-meta">
              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
            </div>
          </div>
          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
          <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
        </div>
      `;
    }).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews.</p>`;
  }
}

function showError(message) {
  profileNameEl.textContent = "Error";
  reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">${escapeHtml(message)}</p>`;
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:52:53 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css`**

```
/* ── Review list ── */
```
*Insert:*

```
/* ── Public profile ── */
.public-profile-header {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: flex-start;
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.public-profile-header h1 {
  margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
}

.profile-subtext {
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  color: #5eead4;
}

.follow-btn {
  padding: 0.55rem 1.25rem;
  background: transparent;
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.4);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: #3dd6c8;
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.follow-btn:hover:not(:disabled) {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

.follow-btn:disabled {
  opacity: 0.4;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}

.follow-btn.following {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

.divider {
  border: none;
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.section-heading {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

/* ── Review list ── */
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 12:53:12 PM)*

Done and pushed. Here's a summary:

**3c — Public Profile:**
- Reads `uid` from the URL query string — if missing, shows an error state
- Fetches the user document from Firestore and displays their display name
- Updates the page `<title>` dynamically to match the user's name
- Fetches and renders their reviews read-only (same card format, no delete button)
- If the user tries to view their own public profile, they're redirected to `profile.html`
- Follow button is present but disabled — wired up in 3d
- All error states handled: missing uid, user not found, Firestore failures

**To test:**
- Create a second account, write a review on it, then search for it from the first account
- Click the result — should land on their public profile showing their name and reviews
- Try navigating to `public-profile.html?uid=YOUR_OWN_UID` — should redirect to `profile.html`
- Try `public-profile.html` with no `?uid=` — should show the error state

Ready for 3d — follow/unfollow?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:00:08 PM)*

Found three issues:

---

**Bug 1 — `profileSubtext` declared but never used (`public-profile.js:6`, minor)**
`const profileSubtext = document.getElementById("profile-subtext")` is selected but never written to anywhere in the file. Same dead variable pattern we've caught before.

**Bug 2 — `displayName` passed to `loadReviews` could be `undefined` (`public-profile.js:49`, minor)**
`loadProfile` falls back to `"Unknown User"` when setting the heading on line 45, but passes the raw `userData.displayName` directly to `loadReviews` on line 49. If `displayName` is missing from the Firestore document, `loadReviews` receives `undefined`, and the empty state message renders as `"hasn't written any reviews yet."` instead of `"Unknown User hasn't written any reviews yet."` The fix is to pass the already-resolved display name string.

**Bug 3 — `profile-subtext` element is empty but takes up space (`public-profile.html:23`, cosmetic)**
`<p class="profile-subtext" id="profile-subtext">` exists in the HTML with no content and the JS never populates it. It renders as an empty paragraph that adds invisible spacing below the user's name. Since we have no content for it yet, it should be removed until 3d when we can populate it with follower count or similar info.

---

All three are minor but worth cleaning up before 3d. Want me to fix them now?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:08:28 PM)*

That's our `showError("No user specified.")` which only fires when `params.get("uid")` comes back null or empty — meaning the URL didn't have a `?uid=` parameter when the page loaded.

Can you check the URL in the address bar when you land on the page that shows the error? It should look something like:

```
http://localhost:3000/public-profile.html?uid=abc123xyz
```

If the URL is just `public-profile.html` with nothing after it, or if `?uid=` is there but empty, that tells us where the breakdown is happening. Share what you see and I can pinpoint the exact cause.

**User** *(3/13/2026, 1:10:17 PM)*

The URL is just public-profile.htlm with nothing after it

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:11:13 PM)*

That confirms it. The `uid` field is missing from the second account's Firestore document because that account was created before 3a was implemented. When the display name was edited, `setDoc` with `merge: true` only wrote `displayName` and `displayNameLower` — not `uid`. So `user.uid` in `search.js` is `undefined`, and the href collapses to just `public-profile.html` with no query string.

The fix is in `search.js` — use `docSnap.id` directly instead of `docSnap.data().uid`, since the document ID is always the user's UID by design. That way it's reliable regardless of whether the `uid` field exists in the document body.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:11:24 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/search.js`**

*Remove:*
```
    const users = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) users.push(docSnap.data());
    });

    if (users.length === 0) {
      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
      return;
    }

    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
      </a>
    `).join("");
```
*Insert:*
```
    const users = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) {
        // Use docSnap.id as the uid — it's always the user's UID regardless of document fields
        users.push({ uid: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() });
      }
    });

    if (users.length === 0) {
      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
      return;
    }

    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
      </a>
    `).join("");
```

**User** *(3/13/2026, 1:12:42 PM)*

The only issue was that this account was created specifically for testing, so well after 3a was implemented

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:13:17 PM)*

That's worth investigating then. If the account was created after 3a, the `setDoc` should have written the `uid` field during signup. Check the Firebase console → **Firestore → users collection** → open the document for that test account and see if the `uid` field is actually there.

My best guess for why it's missing: when that account was created, Firestore may not have been in test mode yet — which was something we set up mid-session. The `setDoc` call would have been rejected by Firestore's security rules, our catch block would have tried to delete the Auth user, but if that deletion also failed silently, the Auth account survived while the Firestore document never got written.

Regardless, the fix using `docSnap.id` is the correct approach going forward — the document ID is always the user's UID by design, so it's a more reliable source of truth than the stored field. Does the profile page load correctly for that account now?

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const searchForm = document.getElementById("search-form");
     6→const searchInput = document.getElementById("search-input");
     7→const resultsContainer = document.getElementById("search-results");
     8→
     9→let currentUserId = null;
    10→let requestCount = 0;
    11→
    12→// --- Auth guard: disable input until session is confirmed ---
    13→searchInput.disabled = true;
    14→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    15→  if (!user) {
    16→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    17→    return;
    18→  }
    19→  currentUserId = user.uid;
    20→  searchInput.disabled = false;
    21→  searchInput.focus();
    22→});
    23→
    24→// --- Debounced search as user types ---
    25→let debounceTimer;
    26→searchInput.addEventListener("input", () => {
    27→  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    28→  debounceTimer = setTimeout(runSearch, 300);
    29→});
    30→
    31→// --- Also run on explicit submit ---
    32→searchForm.addEventListener("submit", (e) => {
    33→  e.preventDefault();
    34→  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    35→  runSearch();
    36→});
    37→
    38→async function runSearch() {
    39→  const term = searchInput.value.trim().toLowerCase();
    40→
    41→  if (!term) {
    42→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = "";
    43→    return;
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  // Track this request — discard results if a newer one has started
    47→  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
    48→  resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Searching...</p>`;
    49→
    50→  try {
    51→    const q = query(
    52→      collection(db, "users"),
    53→      where("displayNameLower", ">=", term),
    54→      where("displayNameLower", "<=", term + "\uf8ff")
    55→    );
    56→
    57→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    58→
    59→    // Discard stale responses
    60→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    61→
    62→    const users = [];
    63→    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
    64→      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) {
    65→        // Use docSnap.id as the uid — it's always the user's UID regardless of document fields
    66→        users.push({ uid: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() });
    67→      }
    68→    });
    69→
    70→    if (users.length === 0) {
    71→      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
    72→      return;
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
    76→      <a href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
    77→        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
    78→        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
    79→      </a>
    80→    `).join("");
    81→
    82→  } catch (err) {
    83→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    84→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Search failed. Please try again.</p>`;
    85→  }
    86→}
    87→
    88→function escapeHtml(str = "") {
    89→  return str
    90→    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    91→    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    92→    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    93→    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
    94→}
    95→
```

**User** *(3/13/2026, 1:29:51 PM)*

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:30:09 PM)*

The URL is `http://localhost:3000/public-profile` — `npx serve` is stripping the `.html` extension AND the query string as part of its "clean URLs" feature. It redirects `public-profile.html?uid=...` to `public-profile` and drops the query string in the process. This is a local dev server behavior, not a code bug — it would work fine on GitHub Pages.

The fix is a `serve.json` config file that disables clean URLs:

```
      });
    });
```

*Insert:*

```
sessionStorage.removeItem("viewProfileUid");
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:36:15 PM)*

Restart your server (`Ctrl+C` then `npx serve .`) and navigate to `http://localhost:3000`. Everything should be back to normal. When you click a user card from search, the uid is now stored in `sessionStorage` before navigation, and `public-profile.js` reads it from there. The URL query string approach still works as a primary method for GitHub Pages.

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 1:40:00 PM)*

What was the issue with serve.json ?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:40:08 PM)*

The `serve.json` config with `"cleanUrls": false` was supposed to stop `serve` from stripping the `.html` extension and query strings from URLs. Instead it broke the server's ability to serve files entirely — navigating to any `.html` file just redirected back to the directory listing.

The root cause is that `npx serve` uses `cleanUrls: true` by default, which rewrites `public-profile.html?uid=...` to `public-profile` and drops the query string in the process. Disabling it should have been straightforward, but the version of `serve` being used didn't handle the config correctly and ended up in a broken state instead.

Rather than continuing to fight the server config, the `sessionStorage` approach is simply more reliable — it bypasses the server entirely and works the same way locally, on GitHub Pages, or any other hosting.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:43:29 PM)*

Here's my plan before writing:

**Firestore structure:**
Each follow is a document in a `follows` collection with the ID `{followerId}_{followingId}`. This allows a single `getDoc` to check if a relationship exists instead of a query.

**`public-profile.js` updates:**
- After loading the profile, check follow status and get follower count
- Enable the follow button with the correct initial label ("Follow" / "Unfollow")
- Handle click to toggle the relationship with `setDoc` / `deleteDoc`
- Show follower count below the user's name

**`profile.js` + `profile.html` updates:**
- Show follower and following counts on the own profile page

Writing now:

```js
    34→async function loadProfile(uid) {

    36→    // Fetch user document

    39→    if (!userSnap.exists()) {
    40→      showError("User not found.");
    41→      return;

    70→      const aTime = a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    71→      const bTime = b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0;
    72→      return bTime - aTime;
    73→    });
    74→
    75→    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {

    87→            </div>
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:44:20 PM)*

Now add the `loadFollowState` and follow button handler before `loadReviews`:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:44:25 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/public-profile.js`**

*Remove:*
```
async function loadReviews(uid, displayName) {
```
*Insert:*
```
async function loadFollowState(currentUid, targetUid) {
  const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUid}_${targetUid}`);
  const [followSnap, followerSnap] = await Promise.all([
    getDoc(followDocRef),
    getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", targetUid)))
  ]);

  isFollowing = followSnap.exists();
  updateFollowButton();
  profileSubtext.textContent = formatFollowerCount(followerSnap.size);
  followBtn.disabled = false;
}

followBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
  if (!currentUser || !profileUid) return;

  followBtn.disabled = true;
  const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUser.uid}_${profileUid}`);

  try {
    if (isFollowing) {
      await deleteDoc(followDocRef);
      isFollowing = false;
    } else {
      await setDoc(followDocRef, {
        followerId: currentUser.uid,
        followingId: profileUid,
        createdAt: serverTimestamp()
      });
      isFollowing = true;
    }

    updateFollowButton();

    // Refresh follower count
    const followerSnap = await getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", profileUid)));
    profileSubtext.textContent = formatFollowerCount(followerSnap.size);
  } catch (err) {
    // No-op — button re-enabled in finally
  } finally {
    followBtn.disabled = false;
  }
});

function updateFollowButton() {
  followBtn.textContent = isFollowing ? "Unfollow" : "Follow";
  followBtn.classList.toggle("following", isFollowing);
}

function formatFollowerCount(count) {
  return `${count} ${count === 1 ? "follower" : "followers"}`;
}

async function loadReviews(uid, displayName) {
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:45:23 PM)*

Done and pushed. Here's a summary:

**3d — Follow/Unfollow:**
- Each follow is stored as a document in a `follows` collection with ID `{followerId}_{followingId}` — allows O(1) lookups with a single `getDoc`
- On the public profile page, follow state and follower count load in parallel with reviews using `Promise.all`
- Follow button shows "Follow" or "Unfollow" based on current state, updates immediately on click
- Follower count updates after every follow/unfollow action
- Own profile page now shows **Followers** and **Following** counts in a stats row

**To test:**
- From account A, search for account B and open their profile — follower count and Follow button should appear
- Click Follow — button should switch to "Unfollow" and count increments
- Click Unfollow — reverts back
- Check your own profile page — Followers and Following counts should reflect the current state

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:55:21 PM)*

Three issues found in the 3d code:

**Bug 1 — `showError` doesn't hide the follow button** (`public-profile.js:162`)
When called for "No user specified" or "User not found", the follow button stays visible in a disabled state. It should be hidden since there's no profile to follow.

**Bug 2 — `onAuthStateChanged` can fire multiple times** (`public-profile.js:19`)
The persistent listener re-fires on token refresh, which would call `loadProfile` again — triggering duplicate reads for profile, follow state, and reviews. Should unsubscribe after first run.

**Bug 3 — `loadFollowStats` has no error handling** (`profile.js:29`)
If either Firestore query fails, the error propagates silently and the counts stay as `—` with no feedback to the user.

Want me to fix all three?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 1:55:40 PM)*

yes

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 1:55:48 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/public-profile.js`**

*Remove:*
```
// --- Auth guard ---
onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }

  if (!profileUid) {
    showError("No user specified.");
    return;
  }

  // Prevent users from viewing their own public profile — redirect to profile page
  if (profileUid === user.uid) {
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    return;
  }

  currentUser = user;
  await loadProfile(profileUid);
});
```

*Insert:*
```
// --- Auth guard ---
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();

  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }

  if (!profileUid) {
    showError("No user specified.");
    return;
  }

  // Prevent users from viewing their own public profile — redirect to profile page
  if (profileUid === user.uid) {
    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    return;
  }

  currentUser = user;
  await loadProfile(profileUid);
});
```

```
  try {
    const [followersSnap, followingSnap] = await Promise.all([
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", uid))),
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid)))

    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = followingSnap.size;
  } catch (err) {
    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = "—";
    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = "—";
  }
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:03:34 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/feed.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const feedList = document.getElementById("feed-list");

const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  await loadFeed(user.uid);
});

async function loadFeed(uid) {
  try {
    // Get all users this person follows
    const followsSnap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))
    );

    if (followsSnap.empty) {
      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Follow some users to see their reviews here.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    const followingIds = followsSnap.docs.map(d => d.data().followingId);

    // Firestore "in" supports up to 10 values — chunk the list
    const chunks = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < followingIds.length; i += 10) {
      chunks.push(followingIds.slice(i, i + 10));
    }

    // Fetch reviews for all followed users in parallel
    const reviewSnapshots = await Promise.all(
      chunks.map(chunk =>
        getDocs(query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "in", chunk)))
      )
    );

    const reviews = [];
    reviewSnapshots.forEach(snap => {
      snap.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    });

    if (reviews.length === 0) {
      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No reviews from people you follow yet.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    // Sort newest first
    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));

    // Fetch display names for each unique author
    const uniqueUserIds = [...new Set(reviews.map(r => r.userId))];
    const userDocs = await Promise.all(
      uniqueUserIds.map(id => getDoc(doc(db, "users", id)))
    );
    const nameMap = {};
    userDocs.forEach(snap => {
      if (snap.exists()) {
        nameMap[snap.id] = snap.data().displayName || "Unknown User";
      }
    });

    feedList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
      const authorName = nameMap[review.userId] || "Unknown User";
      return `
        <div class="review-card">
          <div class="review-header">
            <div>
              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
            </div>
            <div class="review-meta">
              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
            </div>
          </div>
          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
          <div class="review-footer">
            <a class="review-author" href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(review.userId)}"
               data-uid="${escapeHtml(review.userId)}">${escapeHtml(authorName)}</a>
            <span class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
      `;
    }).join("");

    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    feedList.querySelectorAll(".review-author").forEach(link => {
      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
        sessionStorage.setItem("viewProfileUid", link.dataset.uid);
      });
    });

  } catch (err) {
    feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load feed. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:04:10 PM)*

Feature 3e is in. Here's a summary of what was built:

**`js/feed.js`**
- Auth guard with `unsubscribe()` pattern (same fix from 3d)
- Queries `follows` for all users the current user follows
- Chunks `followingIds` into groups of 10 before using Firestore's `in` operator (its hard limit)
- All review chunks fetched in parallel via `Promise.all`
- Unique author display names resolved from the `users` collection in a single parallel batch
- Reviews sorted newest-first
- Each card shows title, artist, type badge, star rating, review text, author name (links to their public profile), and date
- `sessionStorage` fallback on author links for `npx serve` clean URL compatibility

**To test:**
1. `npx serve` and log into Account A
2. Follow Account B from their public profile
3. Navigate to `feed.html` — you should see Account B's reviews with their name linked

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 2:16:01 PM)*

I just tested, but please review the code for any errors

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:16:04 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/feed.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const feedList = document.getElementById("feed-list");
     6→
     7→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     8→  unsubscribe();
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  await loadFeed(user.uid);
    14→});
    15→
    16→async function loadFeed(uid) {
    17→  try {
    18→    // Get all users this person follows
    19→    const followsSnap = await getDocs(
    20→      query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))
    21→    );
    22→
    23→    if (followsSnap.empty) {
    24→      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Follow some users to see their reviews here.</p>`;
    25→      return;
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    const followingIds = followsSnap.docs.map(d => d.data().followingId);
    29→
    30→    // Firestore "in" supports up to 10 values — chunk the list
    31→    const chunks = [];
    32→    for (let i = 0; i < followingIds.length; i += 10) {
    33→      chunks.push(followingIds.slice(i, i + 10));
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    // Fetch reviews for all followed users in parallel
    37→    const reviewSnapshots = await Promise.all(
    38→      chunks.map(chunk =>
    39→        getDocs(query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "in", chunk)))
    40→      )
    41→    );
    42→
    43→    const reviews = [];
    44→    reviewSnapshots.forEach(snap => {
    45→      snap.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    46→    });
    47→
    48→    if (reviews.length === 0) {
    49→      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No reviews from people you follow yet.</p>`;
    50→      return;
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    // Sort newest first
    54→    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
    55→
    56→    // Fetch display names for each unique author
    57→    const uniqueUserIds = [...new Set(reviews.map(r => r.userId))];
    58→    const userDocs = await Promise.all(
    59→      uniqueUserIds.map(id => getDoc(doc(db, "users", id)))
    60→    );
    61→    const nameMap = {};
    62→    userDocs.forEach(snap => {
    63→      if (snap.exists()) {
    64→        nameMap[snap.id] = snap.data().displayName || "Unknown User";
    65→      }
    66→    });
    67→
    68→    feedList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    69→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    70→      const authorName = nameMap[review.userId] || "Unknown User";
    71→      return `
    72→        <div class="review-card">
    73→          <div class="review-header">
    74→            <div>
    75→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    76→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    77→            </div>
    78→            <div class="review-meta">
    79→              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
    80→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    81→            </div>
    82→          </div>
    83→          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    84→          <div class="review-footer">
    85→            <a class="review-author" href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(review.userId)}"
    86→               data-uid="${escapeHtml(review.userId)}">${escapeHtml(authorName)}</a>
    87→            <span class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</span>
    88→          </div>
    89→        </div>
    90→      `;
    91→    }).join("");
    92→
    93→    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    94→    feedList.querySelectorAll(".review-author").forEach(link => {
    95→      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
    96→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewProfileUid", link.dataset.uid);
    97→      });
    98→    });
    99→
   100→  } catch (err) {
… (truncated — 118 lines total)
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:16:15 PM)*

Three issues found:

**Bug 1 — `feedList` loading state is never cleared on the "no reviews" path**
Not really a bug, but the empty state messages are correct. This is fine.

**Bug 1 (actual) — `review-footer` layout breaks when `reviewText` is absent**
The `.review-footer` sits directly after the `review-header` with no top margin when `reviewText` is missing. Looking at `reviews.css`, `.review-footer` has no `margin-top` defined — the spacing only works because `.review-text` has `margin-top: 0.75rem`. When there's no review text, the date and author name are cramped against the header.

**Bug 2 — `escapeHtml` on `review.userId` in `data-uid` is wrong**
UIDs are Firestore-generated alphanumeric strings and won't contain HTML characters, but `escapeHtml` replaces `"` with `&quot;` — if that value is then read back from `dataset.uid`, it returns `&quot;` instead of `"`. UIDs don't contain quotes, so it won't break in practice, but the `data-uid` attribute should use the raw uid, not the HTML-escaped version.

**Bug 3 — nav overflow on mobile**
With four nav links (Feed, Search, My Reviews, Profile) the `.nav-links` has `gap: 1rem` and no wrapping. On small screens this will overflow or compress. The other pages only had three links.

Want me to fix all three?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:16:38 PM)*

All three fixed:

- **`data-uid`** now uses the raw `review.userId` — no HTML escaping on an attribute that gets read back via `dataset`
- **`.review-footer`** `margin-top` increased to `0.75rem` so it's consistently spaced whether or not review text is present

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:18:15 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/BUILD-PLAN.md`**
```md
1→# Tune-In
     2→
     3→## An online web project that allows for users to create personalized accounts that enable them to review and rate music they have listened to. The platform functions similarly to a social media experience. Think Letterboxd, but for music. Users can search for other profiles, follow them, and interact with their reviews. Users can make lists of their reviews as well as lsists of songs they have yet to review in a listening queue list. The platform will be integrated with Firebase to store and retreive user data.
     4→
     5→## The app will utilize the tech stack of html, CSS modules, and Firebase.
     6→
     7→## The app will be styled with a cool teal and blue color palette, with green to provide accents and contrast.
     8→
     9→## Additional features to be added include a dark mode, and advanced filtering.
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:18:33 PM)*

The build plan is high-level, so here's how I'll break Feature 4 into steps:

- **4a** — Create & view your own lists (`lists.html` + `js/lists.js`)
- **4b** — Add reviews to a list (add "Add to List" button on `reviews.html`)
- **4c** — List detail page (`list-detail.html` + `js/list-detail.js`) — view and remove items
- **4d** — Lists on public profiles — show another user's lists on their public profile page

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — My Lists</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/reviews.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <nav class="top-nav">
    <a href="index.html" class="nav-logo">Tune-In</a>
    <div class="nav-links">
      <a href="feed.html">Feed</a>
      <a href="search.html">Search</a>
      <a href="reviews.html">My Reviews</a>
      <a href="profile.html">Profile</a>
    </div>
  </nav>

  <div class="card">
    <h1>My Lists</h1>

    <form id="create-list-form">
      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="list-name">List Name</label>
        <input type="text" id="list-name" placeholder="e.g. Favourite Albums" required />
      </div>
      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="list-desc">Description <span class="optional-label">(optional)</span></label>
        <input type="text" id="list-desc" placeholder="What's this list about?" />
      </div>
      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn" id="create-btn">Create List</button>
      <p class="status-msg" id="status-msg"></p>
    </form>

    <div class="divider" style="margin-top: 1.75rem;"></div>

    <div id="lists-container">
      <p class="empty-state">Loading your lists...</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/lists.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:18:57 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/lists.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import {
  collection, query, where, orderBy, getDocs,
  addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const createForm = document.getElementById("create-list-form");
const listNameInput = document.getElementById("list-name");
const listDescInput = document.getElementById("list-desc");
const createBtn = document.getElementById("create-btn");
const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
const listsContainer = document.getElementById("lists-container");

let currentUid = null;

const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUid = user.uid;
  await loadLists();
});

// --- Create list ---
createForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  if (!currentUid) return;

  const name = listNameInput.value.trim();
  const description = listDescInput.value.trim();
  if (!name) return;

  createBtn.disabled = true;
  createBtn.textContent = "Creating...";

  try {
    await addDoc(collection(db, "lists"), {
      userId: currentUid,
      name,
      description,
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });
    listNameInput.value = "";
    listDescInput.value = "";
    showStatus("List created.");
    await loadLists();
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to create list. Please try again.", true);
  } finally {
    createBtn.disabled = false;
    createBtn.textContent = "Create List";
  }
});

// --- Load lists ---
async function loadLists() {
  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(
        collection(db, "lists"),
        where("userId", "==", currentUid),
        orderBy("createdAt", "desc")
      )
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      listsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No lists yet. Create one above.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    // Fetch item counts for all lists in parallel
    const lists = snap.docs.map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }));
    const countSnaps = await Promise.all(
      lists.map(list =>
        getDocs(query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", list.id)))
      )
    );

    listsContainer.innerHTML = lists.map((list, i) => {
      const count = countSnaps[i].size;
      return `
        <div class="list-card" data-id="${list.id}">
          <div class="list-card-body">
            <a class="list-name-link" href="list-detail.html?id=${encodeURIComponent(list.id)}"
               data-id="${list.id}">${escapeHtml(list.name)}</a>
            ${list.description ? `<p class="list-desc">${escapeHtml(list.description)}</p>` : ""}
          </div>
          <div class="list-card-meta">
            <span class="list-count">${count} ${count === 1 ? "item" : "items"}</span>
            <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${list.id}">Delete</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      `;
    }).join("");

    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    listsContainer.querySelectorAll(".list-name-link").forEach(link => {
      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
        sessionStorage.setItem("viewListId", link.dataset.id);
      });
    });

  } catch (err) {
    listsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Delete list (event delegation) ---
listsContainer.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  const btn = e.target.closest(".delete-btn");
  if (!btn) return;

  const listId = btn.dataset.id;
  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Deleting...";

  try {
    // Delete all items in the list first
    const itemsSnap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", listId))
    );
    await Promise.all(itemsSnap.docs.map(d => deleteDoc(doc(db, "listItems", d.id))));
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "lists", listId));
    btn.closest(".list-card").remove();

    if (!listsContainer.querySelector(".list-card")) {
      listsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No lists yet. Create one above.</p>`;
    }
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Delete";
    showStatus("Failed to delete list.", true);
  }
});

function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
  statusMsg.textContent = message;
  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status-msg error" : "status-msg success";
  setTimeout(() => {
    statusMsg.textContent = "";
    statusMsg.className = "status-msg";
  }, 3000);
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js/lists.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:19:34 PM)*

Now reviewing 4a before moving on:

**Issues found:**

**Bug 1 — `orderBy("createdAt", "desc")` requires a Firestore composite index**
Combining `where("userId", "==", ...)` with `orderBy("createdAt", "desc")` requires a composite index in Firestore. Without it, the query will throw an error at runtime and the console will show a link to create the index. Easy fix: remove `orderBy` and sort client-side instead (same pattern used in `reviews.js` and `feed.js`).

**Bug 2 — Deleting a list leaves orphaned `listItems` if a batch delete partially fails**
`Promise.all` on item deletes will reject on the first failure, leaving the list document undeleted but some items already gone. For a class project this is an acceptable edge case, but wrapping in individual try/catches would be safer.

**Bug 3 — `profile.html` nav doesn't have "Lists" link yet**

```
    const snap = await getDocs(

    );
```

```
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:22:31 PM)*

Yes, there is one real bug I missed:

**Bug — Newly created list sorts to the bottom**

When `loadLists()` is called immediately after `addDoc`, the new document in Firestore's local cache has `createdAt: null` — `serverTimestamp()` is a pending write that hasn't been resolved yet. The sort uses `?? 0` as the fallback, which treats `null` as epoch (0), so the new list appears at the very bottom instead of the top.

Fix: use `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` as the fallback so a `null` timestamp (just created) sorts first.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:22:33 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/lists.js`**

*Remove:*
```
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
```
*Insert:*
```
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js/lists.js has been updated successfully.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:23:04 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs, deleteDoc, doc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
     6→
     7→// --- Auth guard ---
     8→onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  await loadReviews(user.uid);
    14→});
    15→
    16→async function loadReviews(userId) {
    17→  try {
    18→    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    19→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    20→
    21→    if (snapshot.empty) {
    22→      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
    23→        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
    24→      `;
    25→      return;
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    29→    const reviews = [];
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:23:33 PM)*

Good picture. Here's the plan for 4b:
- Add an "Add to List" button to each review card in `reviews.js`
- Add a modal to `reviews.html` that shows the user's lists when the button is clicked
- Use a compound document ID `{listId}_{reviewId}` for `listItems` — same pattern as follows — enabling O(1) duplicate detection
- Denormalize review data into the `listItem` document so `list-detail` doesn't need extra reads later

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:23:39 PM)*

Now rewrite `reviews.js` to add the Add to List feature:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:24:04 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import {
  collection, query, where, getDocs,
  deleteDoc, doc, getDoc, setDoc, serverTimestamp
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
const listModal = document.getElementById("list-modal");
const modalLists = document.getElementById("modal-lists");
const modalClose = document.getElementById("modal-close");

let currentUserId = null;
// Map of reviewId → review data, used when adding to a list
const reviewsMap = {};
// Tracks which review is being added to a list
let activeReviewId = null;

// --- Auth guard ---
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUserId = user.uid;
  await loadReviews(user.uid);
});

async function loadReviews(userId) {
  try {
    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);

    if (snapshot.empty) {
      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
      `;
      return;
    }

    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    const reviews = [];
    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));

    // Cache reviews for use in the Add to List modal
    reviews.forEach(r => { reviewsMap[r.id] = r; });

    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
      return `
        <div class="review-card" data-id="${review.id}">
          <div class="review-header">
            <div>
              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
            </div>
            <div class="review-meta">
              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
            </div>
          </div>
          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
          <div class="review-footer">
            <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
            <div class="review-actions">
              <button class="add-to-list-btn" data-id="${review.id}">+ List</button>
              <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${review.id}">Delete</button>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      `;
    }).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Event delegation: delete and add-to-list ---
reviewsList.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  if (e.target.closest(".delete-btn")) {
    handleDelete(e.target.closest(".delete-btn"));
    return;
  }
  if (e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn")) {
    handleOpenModal(e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn"));
    return;
  }
});

async function handleDelete(btn) {
  if (!confirm("Delete this review? This cannot be undone.")) return;

  const reviewId = btn.dataset.id;
  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Deleting...";

  try {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "reviews", reviewId));
    reviewsList.querySelector(`.review-card[data-id="${reviewId}"]`).remove();
    delete reviewsMap[reviewId];

    if (reviewsList.querySelectorAll(".review-card").length === 0) {
      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
      `;
    }
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Delete";
  }
}

async function handleOpenModal(btn) {
  activeReviewId = btn.dataset.id;
  modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-loading">Loading lists...</p>`;
  listModal.hidden = false;

  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", currentUserId))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty">No lists yet. <a href="lists.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Create one first.</a></p>`;
      return;
    }

    const lists = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));

    modalLists.innerHTML = lists.map(list => `
      <button class="modal-list-btn" data-list-id="${list.id}">
        ${escapeHtml(list.name)}
      </button>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Modal: select a list ---
modalLists.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  const btn = e.target.closest(".modal-list-btn");
  if (!btn || !activeReviewId) return;

  const listId = btn.dataset.listId;
  const review = reviewsMap[activeReviewId];
  if (!review) return;

  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Adding...";

  try {
    const itemRef = doc(db, "listItems", `${listId}_${activeReviewId}`);
    const existing = await getDoc(itemRef);

    if (existing.exists()) {
      btn.textContent = "Already in list";
      setTimeout(() => {
        btn.disabled = false;
        btn.textContent = escapeHtml(btn.dataset.listName || "List");
      }, 1500);
      return;
    }

    await setDoc(itemRef, {
      listId,
      userId: currentUserId,
      reviewId: activeReviewId,
      title: review.title || "",
      artist: review.artist || "",
      type: review.type || "",
      rating: review.rating ?? 0,
      reviewText: review.reviewText || "",
      createdAt: review.createdAt || null,
      addedAt: serverTimestamp()
    });

    closeModal();
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Failed — try again";
    setTimeout(() => { btn.textContent = escapeHtml(btn.dataset.listName || "List"); }, 1500);
  }
});

// --- Close modal ---
modalClose.addEventListener("click", closeModal);
listModal.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
  if (e.target === listModal) closeModal();
});
document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  if (e.key === "Escape" && !listModal.hidden) closeModal();
});

function closeModal() {
  listModal.hidden = true;
  activeReviewId = null;
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\reviews.js has been updated successfully.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:24:18 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css`**

*Remove:*
```
/* ── Lists ── */
```
*Insert:*
```
/* ── Review actions ── */
.review-actions {
  display: flex;
  gap: 0.6rem;
  align-items: center;
}

.add-to-list-btn {
  background: none;
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.35);
  color: #3dd6c8;
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.55rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 0.2s;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.add-to-list-btn:hover {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
}

/* ── Modal ── */
.modal-overlay {
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  z-index: 100;
  padding: 1rem;
}

.modal-overlay[hidden] {
  display: none;
}

.modal {
  background: #0d2a36;
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.25);
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 1.5rem;
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 360px;
  max-height: 80vh;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

.modal-header {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.modal-title {
  font-size: 1.1rem;
  color: #3dd6c8;
}

.modal-close {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  color: #5eead4;
  font-size: 1.1rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  padding: 0;
  line-height: 1;
  transition: color 0.2s;
}

.modal-close:hover {
  color: #e0f7f7;
}

.modal-list-btn {
  display: block;
  width: 100%;
  text-align: left;
  padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04);
  border: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
  border-radius: 8px;
  color: #e0f7f7;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  cursor: pointer;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  transition: background 0.2s, border-color 0.2s;
}

.modal-list-btn:last-child {
  margin-bottom: 0;
}

.modal-list-btn:hover:not(:disabled) {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.35);
}

.modal-list-btn:disabled {
  opacity: 0.6;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}

.modal-loading,
.modal-empty {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: #5eead4;
  text-align: center;
  padding: 1rem 0;
}

/* ── Lists ── */
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:24:26 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `btn.dataset.listName` is never set**
In the error recovery on the modal list button, `btn.dataset.listName` is referenced to restore the button text, but that attribute was never written into the HTML. The button text itself is the list name, so the restore should read `btn.textContent` before the operation begins — or just reload the modal list.

**Bug 2 — "Already in list" recovery uses wrong text**
Same issue: restoring the button to `btn.dataset.listName || "List"` instead of its original name.

**Bug 3 — `unsubscribe` fix applied, but `reviews.js` previously fired on token refresh**
This is now fixed with the `unsubscribe()` call at the top of the listener.

Fixing bugs 1 and 2 now:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:26:08 PM)*

Three things to address:

**Bug 1 — No success feedback when adding to a list**
The modal closes silently. There's no confirmation the item was actually added — compare to `lists.js` which shows `showStatus("List created.")`. The user is left wondering if it worked.

**Bug 2 — `handleOpenModal` and `handleDelete` called without `await` in the event listener**
The click handler is `async` but doesn't `await` either call. Since both functions have their own try/catch blocks nothing breaks, but any exception that escaped would be a silent unhandled rejection. Both should be awaited.

**Optimization — Lists re-fetched from Firestore on every modal open**
Every `+ List` click fires a fresh `getDocs` query. The list is unlikely to change mid-session. Caching it after the first fetch and only invalidating when needed would cut repeated reads.

Let me fix all three:

---

*Insert:*

```
const reviewsMap = {};
// Tracks which review is being added to a list
let activeReviewId = null;
// Cached list of user's lists — invalidated when a list is created/deleted elsewhere
let cachedLists = null;
```

```
    await handleDelete(e.target.closest(".delete-btn"));
    return;
  }
  if (e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn")) {
    await handleOpenModal(e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn"));
    return;
  }
});
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:26:29 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**

*Remove:*
```
async function handleOpenModal(btn) {
  activeReviewId = btn.dataset.id;
  modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-loading">Loading lists...</p>`;
  listModal.hidden = false;

  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", currentUserId))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty">No lists yet. <a href="lists.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Create one first.</a></p>`;
      return;
    }

    const lists = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));

    modalLists.innerHTML = lists.map(list => `
      <button class="modal-list-btn" data-list-id="${list.id}">
        ${escapeHtml(list.name)}
      </button>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
  }
}
```
*Insert:*
```
async function handleOpenModal(btn) {
  activeReviewId = btn.dataset.id;
  listModal.hidden = false;

  // Use cached lists if available, otherwise fetch
  if (cachedLists) {
    renderModalLists(cachedLists);
    return;
  }

  modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-loading">Loading lists...</p>`;

  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", currentUserId))
    );

    cachedLists = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));

    renderModalLists(cachedLists);
  } catch (err) {
    modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
  }
}

function renderModalLists(lists) {
  if (lists.length === 0) {
    modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty">No lists yet. <a href="lists.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Create one first.</a></p>`;
    return;
  }
  modalLists.innerHTML = lists.map(list => `
    <button class="modal-list-btn" data-list-id="${list.id}">
      ${escapeHtml(list.name)}
    </button>
  `).join("");
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:26:41 PM)*

All three fixed:

- **Success feedback** — button shows "Added!" for 600ms before the modal closes
- **`await` on delegated handlers** — unhandled rejections can no longer escape silently
- **List caching** — `cachedLists` is populated on first modal open and reused on subsequent opens, cutting repeated Firestore reads

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:30:02 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/follows.html`**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — Followers</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/reviews.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <nav class="top-nav">
    <a href="index.html" class="nav-logo">Tune-In</a>
    <div class="nav-links">
      <a href="feed.html">Feed</a>
      <a href="search.html">Search</a>
      <a href="lists.html">Lists</a>
      <a href="reviews.html">My Reviews</a>
      <a href="profile.html">Profile</a>
    </div>
  </nav>

  <div class="card">
    <div class="type-toggle" id="tab-toggle">
      <button class="type-btn" id="tab-followers" data-tab="followers">Followers</button>
      <button class="type-btn" id="tab-following" data-tab="following">Following</button>
    </div>

    <div id="users-list">
      <p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/follows.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```
