---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:30:19 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/follows.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import {
  collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const usersList = document.getElementById("users-list");
const tabFollowers = document.getElementById("tab-followers");
const tabFollowing = document.getElementById("tab-following");

let currentUid = null;
// Cache both tabs after first load to avoid re-fetching on tab switch
const cache = { followers: null, following: null };

const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
let activeTab = params.get("tab") === "following" ? "following" : "followers";

const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUid = user.uid;
  setActiveTab(activeTab);
  await loadTab(activeTab);
});

// --- Tab toggle ---
tabFollowers.addEventListener("click", async () => {
  if (activeTab === "followers") return;
  activeTab = "followers";
  setActiveTab("followers");
  await loadTab("followers");
});

tabFollowing.addEventListener("click", async () => {
  if (activeTab === "following") return;
  activeTab = "following";
  setActiveTab("following");
  await loadTab("following");
});

function setActiveTab(tab) {
  tabFollowers.classList.toggle("active", tab === "followers");
  tabFollowing.classList.toggle("active", tab === "following");
  document.title = tab === "followers" ? "Tune-In — Followers" : "Tune-In — Following";
}

// --- Load a tab ---
async function loadTab(tab) {
  if (cache[tab]) {
    renderUsers(cache[tab], tab);
    return;
  }

  usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>`;

  try {
    const isFollowers = tab === "followers";
    const field = isFollowers ? "followingId" : "followerId";
    const uidField = isFollowers ? "followerId" : "followingId";

    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "follows"), where(field, "==", currentUid))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      cache[tab] = [];
      renderUsers([], tab);
      return;
    }

    // Fetch user documents for all follows in parallel
    const uids = snap.docs.map(d => d.data()[uidField]);
    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uids.map(uid => getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid))));

    const users = userSnaps
      .filter(s => s.exists())
      .map(s => ({ uid: s.id, displayName: s.data().displayName || "Unknown User" }))
      .sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));

    cache[tab] = users;
    renderUsers(users, tab);
  } catch (err) {
    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Render ---
function renderUsers(users, tab) {
  if (users.length === 0) {
    const msg = tab === "followers"
      ? "Nobody is following you yet."
      : "You're not following anyone yet.";
    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">${msg}</p>`;
    return;
  }

  usersList.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
    <a class="user-card" href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(user.uid)}"
       data-uid="${user.uid}">
      <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
      <span class="user-arrow">›</span>
    </a>
  `).join("");

  // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
  usersList.querySelectorAll(".user-card").forEach(card => {
    card.addEventListener("click", () => {
      sessionStorage.setItem("viewProfileUid", card.dataset.uid);
    });
  });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\follows.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:30:40 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `follows.html?tab=followers` will have the query string stripped by `npx serve`**
Same issue as the public-profile uid — clean URLs drops query strings. The tab defaults to `"followers"` when `params.get("tab")` returns null, so the followers tab always works. But navigating to the following tab from `profile.html` (`?tab=following`) will arrive with no query string and default to followers instead. Need the sessionStorage fallback.

**Bug 2 — Tab click handlers not awaited**
Same pattern as the reviews.js fix — `async` functions called without `await`.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:30:42 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/follows.js`**

*Remove:*
```
const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
let activeTab = params.get("tab") === "following" ? "following" : "followers";
```
*Insert:*
```
const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
const tabParam = params.get("tab") || sessionStorage.getItem("followsTab");
sessionStorage.removeItem("followsTab");
let activeTab = tabParam === "following" ? "following" : "followers";
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:32:01 PM)*

Two issues found:

**Bug 1 — Tab click handlers don't guard against `currentUid` being null**
If a tab button is clicked in the brief window before `onAuthStateChanged` fires, `currentUid` is still `null`. The Firestore query `where("followingId", "==", null)` would run and return empty results, silently showing the wrong empty state. A simple guard at the top of each handler fixes this.

**Bug 2 — Race condition on rapid tab switching**
The tab handlers call `loadTab` without tracking in-flight requests. If the user clicks Followers, then quickly clicks Following before the first query resolves, both queries run concurrently. Whichever finishes last overwrites `usersList.innerHTML` — so the wrong tab's content could be displayed. The same `requestCount` pattern from `search.js` fixes this.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:32:04 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/follows.js`**

*Remove:*
```
let currentUid = null;
// Cache both tabs after first load to avoid re-fetching on tab switch
const cache = { followers: null, following: null };
```
*Insert:*
```
let currentUid = null;
// Cache both tabs after first load to avoid re-fetching on tab switch
const cache = { followers: null, following: null };
// Discard stale async responses when tabs are switched rapidly
let requestCount = 0;
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:32:21 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/follows.js`**

*Remove:*
```
// --- Load a tab ---
async function loadTab(tab) {
  if (cache[tab]) {
    renderUsers(cache[tab], tab);
    return;
  }

  usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>`;

  try {
    const isFollowers = tab === "followers";
    const field = isFollowers ? "followingId" : "followerId";
    const uidField = isFollowers ? "followerId" : "followingId";

    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "follows"), where(field, "==", currentUid))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      cache[tab] = [];
      renderUsers([], tab);
      return;
    }

    // Fetch user documents for all follows in parallel
    const uids = snap.docs.map(d => d.data()[uidField]);
    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uids.map(uid => getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid))));

    const users = userSnaps
      .filter(s => s.exists())
      .map(s => ({ uid: s.id, displayName: s.data().displayName || "Unknown User" }))
      .sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));

    cache[tab] = users;
    renderUsers(users, tab);
  } catch (err) {
    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}
```
*Insert:*
```
// --- Load a tab ---
async function loadTab(tab) {
  if (cache[tab]) {
    renderUsers(cache[tab], tab);
    return;
  }

  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
  usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>`;

  try {
    const isFollowers = tab === "followers";
    const field = isFollowers ? "followingId" : "followerId";
    const uidField = isFollowers ? "followerId" : "followingId";

    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "follows"), where(field, "==", currentUid))
    );

    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;

    if (snap.empty) {
      cache[tab] = [];
      renderUsers([], tab);
      return;
    }

    // Fetch user documents for all follows in parallel
    const uids = snap.docs.map(d => d.data()[uidField]);
    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uids.map(uid => getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid))));

    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;

    const users = userSnaps
      .filter(s => s.exists())
      .map(s => ({ uid: s.id, displayName: s.data().displayName || "Unknown User" }))
      .sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));

    cache[tab] = users;
    renderUsers(users, tab);
  } catch (err) {
    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\follows.js has been updated successfully.

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";

     4→  addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp

     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const createForm = document.getElementById("create-list-form");
     9→const listNameInput = document.getElementById("list-name");
    10→const listDescInput = document.getElementById("list-desc");
    11→const createBtn = document.getElementById("create-btn");
    12→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    13→const listsContainer = document.getElementById("lists-container");
    14→
    15→let currentUid = null;
    16→
    17→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    18→  unsubscribe();
    19→  if (!user) {
    20→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    21→    return;
    22→  }
    23→  currentUid = user.uid;
    24→  await loadLists();
    25→});
    26→
    27→// --- Create list ---
    28→createForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    29→  e.preventDefault();
    30→  if (!currentUid) return;
    31→
    32→  const name = listNameInput.value.trim();
    33→  const description = listDescInput.value.trim();
    34→  if (!name) return;
    35→
    36→  createBtn.disabled = true;
    37→  createBtn.textContent = "Creating...";
    38→
    39→  try {
    40→    await addDoc(collection(db, "lists"), {
    41→      userId: currentUid,
    42→      name,
    43→      description,
    44→      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    45→    });
    46→    listNameInput.value = "";
    47→    listDescInput.value = "";
    48→    showStatus("List created.");
    49→    await loadLists();
    50→  } catch (err) {
    51→    showStatus("Failed to create list. Please try again.", true);
    52→  } finally {
    53→    createBtn.disabled = false;
    54→    createBtn.textContent = "Create List";
    55→  }
    56→});
    57→
    58→// --- Load lists ---
    59→async function loadLists() {

    63→    );

    68→    }
    69→

    72→      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
    73→      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));

    77→      )
    78→    );
    79→

    88→          </div>

    90→            <span class="list-count">${count} ${count === 1 ? "item" : "items"}</span>
    91→            <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${list.id}">Delete</button>
    92→          </div>
    93→        </div>
    94→      `;
    95→    }).join("");
    96→
    97→    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    98→    listsContainer.querySelectorAll(".list-name-link").forEach(link => {
    99→      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
   100→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewListId", link.dataset.id);
```

```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import {
  collection, query, where, getDocs,
  doc, getDoc, deleteDoc
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const listTitle = document.getElementById("list-title");
const listDesc = document.getElementById("list-desc");
const itemsList = document.getElementById("items-list");

// Read listId from URL query string with sessionStorage fallback
const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
const listId = params.get("id") || sessionStorage.getItem("viewListId");
sessionStorage.removeItem("viewListId");

let isOwner = false;

const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }

  if (!listId) {
    showError("No list specified.");
    return;
  }

  await loadList(user.uid);
});

async function loadList(currentUid) {
  try {
    // Fetch the list document to get name, description, and verify ownership
    const listSnap = await getDoc(doc(db, "lists", listId));

    if (!listSnap.exists()) {
      showError("List not found.");
      return;
    }

    const listData = listSnap.data();
    isOwner = listData.userId === currentUid;

    listTitle.textContent = listData.name;
    document.title = `Tune-In — ${listData.name}`;

    if (listData.description) {
      listDesc.textContent = listData.description;
    }

    await loadItems();
  } catch (err) {
    showError("Failed to load list. Please try again.");
  }
}

async function loadItems() {
  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", listId))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">This list has no items yet.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    // Sort by addedAt descending — most recently added first
    const items = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0));

    itemsList.innerHTML = items.map(item => {
      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(item.rating) || 0));
      return `
        <div class="review-card" data-id="${item.id}">
          <div class="review-header">
            <div>
              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(item.title)}</div>
              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(item.artist)}</div>
            </div>
            <div class="review-meta">
              <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(item.type)}</span>
              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
            </div>
          </div>
          ${item.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(item.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
          <div class="review-footer">
            <p class="review-date">${formatDate(item.createdAt)}</p>
            ${isOwner ? `<button class="delete-btn" data-id="${item.id}">Remove</button>` : ""}
          </div>
        </div>
      `;
    }).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load items. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Remove item via event delegation ---
itemsList.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  const btn = e.target.closest(".delete-btn");
  if (!btn || !isOwner) return;

  const itemId = btn.dataset.id;
  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Removing...";

  try {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "listItems", itemId));
    itemsList.querySelector(`.review-card[data-id="${itemId}"]`).remove();

    if (itemsList.querySelectorAll(".review-card").length === 0) {
      itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">This list has no items yet.</p>`;
    }
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Remove";
  }
});

function showError(message) {
  listTitle.textContent = "Error";
  itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">${escapeHtml(message)}</p>`;
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\list-detail.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:33:44 PM)*

Now reviewing the code:

**Bug 1 — `addedAt` pending `serverTimestamp()` sort issue**
Same as the `lists.js` bug fixed earlier — items added to a list have `addedAt: serverTimestamp()`. If `loadItems()` were called immediately after adding, the newest item's `addedAt` would be `null` in the local cache, sorting it to the bottom. However, since the user navigates to `list-detail.html` separately rather than triggering `loadItems()` right after `setDoc`, this isn't reachable in practice. The sort fallback of `?? 0` is fine here.

**Bug 2 — `isOwner` is module-scoped and defaults `false`**
The remove button is conditionally rendered based on `isOwner`, and the event delegation also guards with `if (!isOwner) return`. Both use the same flag, so even if someone manipulates the DOM to add a remove button, the delegation guard blocks the Firestore delete. This is correct.

**Bug 3 — No Firestore security rules**
A non-owner could call `deleteDoc` on a `listItems` document directly via the console. This is a Firestore rules concern rather than a JS bug — worth tightening before launch.

The code is otherwise clean. Here's what to test:

1. Go to `lists.html`, click a list name — should navigate to `list-detail.html` showing the list's items
2. Items should be sorted newest-added first with title, artist, rating, and review text
3. Click **Remove** on an item — card disappears, empty state appears if last item removed
4. The **← My Lists** link returns to `lists.html`

Ready for 4d?

---

Let's clean these bugs before proceeding to tighten security

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:35:31 PM)*

Looking back at the review — Bug 1 and Bug 2 don't actually require fixes, and Bug 3 is the security work. Let me clarify:

**Bug 1** — I flagged the `?? 0` fallback for `addedAt`, but then said it's unreachable in practice since the user navigates to the detail page separately. I'll apply the `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` fix anyway for consistency with the `lists.js` pattern:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:35:32 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/list-detail.js`**

*Remove:*
```
      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0));
```
*Insert:*

> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\list-detail.js has been updated successfully.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:35:39 PM)*

**Bug 2** — `isOwner` defaulting to `false` is actually correct behavior. The delegation guard (`if (!isOwner) return`) means a non-owner can never trigger a Firestore delete through the UI even if they manually inject a button. No change needed.

**Bug 3** — This is the Firestore security rules work. Currently the database is in test mode, which allows any authenticated user to read and write anything. Before launch you'll want rules that enforce:

- Users can only write/delete their own reviews, lists, listItems, and follows
- Users can only read public data (other users' profiles, reviews, lists)

Want to tackle the Firestore rules now, or finish 4d first and do security as one pass at the end?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 2:36:39 PM)*

Let's do the security pass at the end. When do you think it would be most appropriate considering the last features we need to develop?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:36:47 PM)*

Given what's left to build, the most appropriate time is **after Feature 5 (Listening Queue)** and before any final polish or deployment.

Here's the reasoning:

- Security rules are written against your **final data model** — every collection and access pattern needs to be accounted for. If you write rules now and then Feature 5 introduces a new `queue` collection, you'd have to revisit the rules anyway.
- Features 4d and 5 will solidify exactly what reads non-owners need (public lists, queue visibility, etc.), which directly informs what the rules should allow.
- Once rules are locked in, testing becomes more strict — so doing it last means you're validating the complete app, not a partial one.

**Proposed order:**
1. 4d — Lists on public profiles
2. Feature 5 (5a–5d) — Listening Queue
3. Security rules pass
4. Final responsive/polish pass

Does that work for you?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:52:07 PM)*

Now add the `loadLists` function before `showError`:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:52:15 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/public-profile.js`**

*Remove:*
```
function showError(message) {
```
*Insert:*
```
async function loadLists(uid, displayName) {
  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", uid))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      listsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">${escapeHtml(displayName)} hasn't created any lists yet.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    const lists = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));

    // Fetch item counts for all lists in parallel
    const countSnaps = await Promise.all(
      lists.map(list =>
        getDocs(query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", list.id)))
      )
    );

    listsList.innerHTML = lists.map((list, i) => {
      const count = countSnaps[i].size;
      return `
        <a class="list-card" href="list-detail.html?id=${encodeURIComponent(list.id)}"
           data-id="${list.id}">
          <div class="list-card-body">
            <span class="list-name-link">${escapeHtml(list.name)}</span>
            ${list.description ? `<p class="list-desc">${escapeHtml(list.description)}</p>` : ""}
          </div>
          <div class="list-card-meta">
            <span class="list-count">${count} ${count === 1 ? "item" : "items"}</span>
            <span class="user-arrow">›</span>
          </div>
        </a>
      `;
    }).join("");

    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    listsList.querySelectorAll(".list-card").forEach(card => {
      card.addEventListener("click", () => {
        sessionStorage.setItem("viewListId", card.dataset.id);
      });
    });

  } catch (err) {
    listsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
  }
}

function showError(message) {
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 2:52:44 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `list-name-link` inside an `<a>` tag renders as a `<span>`, not a link**
On the public profile, the list card is the `<a>` tag itself, so the inner `list-name-link` is correctly a `<span>`. But the CSS for `.list-name-link` sets `color: #3dd6c8` and `display: block` — both of which work fine on a span. No issue.

**Bug 2 — `loadLists` not added to Firestore imports**
`loadLists` uses `getDocs`, `collection`, `query`, `where` — all already imported at the top of `public-profile.js`. No change needed.

**Bug 3 — `showError` called before `listsList` is assigned**
`showError` is called in the auth guard for "No user specified" at line 28, before `loadProfile` is ever called. `listsList` is assigned at the top of the module so it's always available. No issue.

The code is clean. Here's what to test:

1. Visit another user's public profile — the Lists section should appear below Reviews
2. If they have lists, each card shows the name, description, item count, and links to the detail page
3. On the detail page, no Remove button appears since you're not the owner
4. If they have no lists, shows the empty state message

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:08:37 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**

*Remove:*

```
function renderModalLists(lists) {
  if (lists.length === 0) {
    modalLists.innerHTML = `<p class="modal-empty">No lists yet. <a href="lists.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Create one first.</a></p>`;
    return;
  }
  modalLists.innerHTML = lists.map(list => `
```

*Insert:*
```
function renderModalLists(lists) {
  const listButtons = lists.map(list => `
    <button class="modal-list-btn" data-list-id="${list.id}">
      ${escapeHtml(list.name)}
    </button>
  `).join("");

  modalLists.innerHTML = listButtons + `
    <div id="modal-create-row">
      <button class="modal-new-btn" id="modal-new-btn">+ New List</button>
    </div>
  `;

  document.getElementById("modal-new-btn").addEventListener("click", showInlineCreateForm);
}

function showInlineCreateForm() {
  document.getElementById("modal-create-row").innerHTML = `
    <form id="modal-create-form" class="modal-create-form">
      <input type="text" id="modal-list-name" placeholder="List name" required autocomplete="off" />
      <button type="submit" class="modal-create-btn">Create</button>
    </form>
  `;
  document.getElementById("modal-list-name").focus();
  document.getElementById("modal-create-form").addEventListener("submit", handleModalCreate);
}

async function handleModalCreate(e) {
  e.preventDefault();
  const input = document.getElementById("modal-list-name");
  const name = input.value.trim();
  if (!name) return;

  const submitBtn = e.target.querySelector("button[type=submit]");
  submitBtn.disabled = true;
  submitBtn.textContent = "Creating...";

  try {
    const docRef = await addDoc(collection(db, "lists"), {
      userId: currentUserId,
      name,
      description: "",
      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    });

    const newList = { id: docRef.id, name, description: "", createdAt: null };
    // Prepend so it appears at the top (newest first)
    cachedLists = cachedLists ? [newList, ...cachedLists] : [newList];
    renderModalLists(cachedLists);
  } catch (err) {
    submitBtn.disabled = false;
    submitBtn.textContent = "Create";
    input.value = "";
    input.placeholder = "Failed — try again";
  }
}
```

```
}
```

```
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:08:58 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `modal-create-form input` overrides global `input` width**
The global `input { width: 100% }` in `reviews.css` would override `flex: 1` inside the form. The `.modal-create-form input { width: auto }` fix is already in the CSS above to counteract this. ✓

**Bug 2 — `renderModalLists` re-attaches a fresh `click` listener on `modal-new-btn` every time it's called**
`renderModalLists` is called after every successful list creation to re-render. Each call does `document.getElementById("modal-new-btn").addEventListener("click", showInlineCreateForm)`. Since `innerHTML` is replaced each time, the old element is destroyed and a new one created, so there's no listener duplication. ✓

**Bug 3 — `cachedLists` updated in modal but `lists.js` doesn't know about it**
If the user creates a list via the modal and then navigates to `lists.html`, the new list will appear there correctly since `lists.js` fetches fresh from Firestore on page load. The cache is only local to `reviews.js`. ✓

The code is clean. Here's what to test:

1. Open the modal with `+ List` on a review — a `+ New List` button appears at the bottom
2. If you have existing lists, they appear above it
3. Click `+ New List` — the button is replaced by an inline input + Create button
4. Type a name and submit — the new list appears immediately in the modal
5. Click the new list — review is added, modal closes with "Added!"

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>Tune-In — Listening Queue</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/reviews.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <nav class="top-nav">
    <a href="index.html" class="nav-logo">Tune-In</a>
    <div class="nav-links">
      <a href="feed.html">Feed</a>
      <a href="search.html">Search</a>
      <a href="lists.html">Lists</a>
      <a href="reviews.html">My Reviews</a>
      <a href="profile.html">Profile</a>
    </div>
  </nav>

  <div class="card">
    <h1>Listening Queue</h1>

    <form id="queue-form">
      <div class="type-toggle">
        <button type="button" class="type-btn active" data-type="song">Song</button>
        <button type="button" class="type-btn" data-type="album">Album</button>
      </div>
      <input type="hidden" id="queue-type" value="song" />

      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="queue-artist">Artist</label>
        <input type="text" id="queue-artist" placeholder="Artist name" required />
      </div>

      <div class="form-group">
        <label for="queue-title" id="title-label">Song Title</label>
        <input type="text" id="queue-title" placeholder="Song title" required />
      </div>

      <button type="submit" class="submit-btn" id="add-btn">Add to Queue</button>
      <p class="status-msg" id="status-msg"></p>
    </form>

    <div class="divider" style="margin-top: 1.75rem;"></div>

    <div id="queue-list" class="reviews-list">
      <p class="empty-state">Loading your queue...</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script type="module" src="js/queue.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:12:19 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/queue.js`**
```js
import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
import {
  collection, query, where, getDocs,
  addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp
} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";

const queueForm = document.getElementById("queue-form");
const queueTypeInput = document.getElementById("queue-type");
const queueArtist = document.getElementById("queue-artist");
const queueTitle = document.getElementById("queue-title");
const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
const addBtn = document.getElementById("add-btn");
const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
const queueList = document.getElementById("queue-list");

let currentUid = null;

// --- Auth guard ---
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUid = user.uid;
  await loadQueue();
});

// --- Type toggle ---
document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn").forEach(btn => {
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn").forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    btn.classList.add("active");
    queueTypeInput.value = btn.dataset.type;
    const isSong = btn.dataset.type === "song";
    titleLabel.textContent = isSong ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    queueTitle.placeholder = isSong ? "Song title" : "Album title";
  });
});

// --- Add to queue ---
queueForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  if (!currentUid) return;

  const artist = queueArtist.value.trim();
  const title = queueTitle.value.trim();
  const type = queueTypeInput.value;
  if (!artist || !title) return;

  addBtn.disabled = true;
  addBtn.textContent = "Adding...";

  try {
    await addDoc(collection(db, "queue"), {
      userId: currentUid,
      artist,
      title,
      type,
      addedAt: serverTimestamp()
    });

    queueArtist.value = "";
    queueTitle.value = "";
    showStatus("Added to queue.");
    await loadQueue();
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to add. Please try again.", true);
  } finally {
    addBtn.disabled = false;
    addBtn.textContent = "Add to Queue";
  }
});

// --- Load queue ---
async function loadQueue() {
  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", currentUid))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Your queue is empty. Add something above.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    const items = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));

    queueList.innerHTML = items.map(item => `
      <div class="review-card" data-id="${item.id}">
        <div class="review-header">
          <div>
            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(item.title)}</div>
            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(item.artist)}</div>
          </div>
          <div class="review-meta">
            <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(item.type)}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="review-footer">
          <span class="review-date">${formatDate(item.addedAt)}</span>
          <div class="review-actions">
            <button class="write-review-btn" data-id="${item.id}"
              data-title="${escapeAttr(item.title)}"
              data-artist="${escapeAttr(item.artist)}"
              data-type="${escapeAttr(item.type)}">Write Review</button>
            <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${item.id}">Remove</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load queue. Please try again.</p>`;
  }
}

// --- Event delegation: remove and write review ---
queueList.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
  if (e.target.closest(".delete-btn")) {
    await handleRemove(e.target.closest(".delete-btn"));
    return;
  }
  if (e.target.closest(".write-review-btn")) {
    handleWriteReview(e.target.closest(".write-review-btn"));
    return;
  }
});

async function handleRemove(btn) {
  const itemId = btn.dataset.id;
  btn.disabled = true;
  btn.textContent = "Removing...";

  try {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "queue", itemId));
    queueList.querySelector(`.review-card[data-id="${itemId}"]`).remove();

    if (queueList.querySelectorAll(".review-card").length === 0) {
      queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Your queue is empty. Add something above.</p>`;
    }
  } catch (err) {
    btn.disabled = false;
    btn.textContent = "Remove";
  }
}

function handleWriteReview(btn) {
  sessionStorage.setItem("prefill", JSON.stringify({
    title: btn.dataset.title,
    artist: btn.dataset.artist,
    type: btn.dataset.type,
    queueId: btn.dataset.id
  }));
  window.location.href = "review-new.html";
}

function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
  statusMsg.textContent = message;
  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status-msg error" : "status-msg success";
  setTimeout(() => {
    statusMsg.textContent = "";
    statusMsg.className = "status-msg";
  }, 3000);
}

function formatDate(timestamp) {
  if (!timestamp) return "";
  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
}

function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}

// Attribute-safe escaping — escapes quotes but not < > for use in data-* values
function escapeAttr(str = "") {
  return str.replace(/&/g, "&amp;").replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\queue.js

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:17:05 PM)*

Now reviewing 5a:

**Bug 1 — `escapeAttr` is redundant**
`escapeAttr` skips `<` and `>` escaping since it's used for `data-*` attributes, but HTML parsers don't interpret `<` or `>` inside attribute values as tags. The simpler `escapeHtml` would actually be fine and safer here. However `escapeAttr` does no harm, just unnecessary complexity.

**Bug 2 — Queue item added with `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` fallback sorts correctly, but new items sort to the bottom on reload**
Same pending `serverTimestamp()` issue from before — `loadQueue()` is called immediately after `addDoc`, so the new item has `addedAt: null`, which the `?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` fallback correctly pushes to the top. ✓

**Bug 3 — `prefill` sessionStorage key set by `handleWriteReview` but nothing reads it yet**
`review-new.js` doesn't read `sessionStorage.getItem("prefill")` — that's 5b's job. This is intentional and expected at this stage.

The code is otherwise clean. Here's what to test for 5a:

1. Go to `queue.html` — shows the add form and empty state
2. Toggle Song/Album — label and placeholder update
3. Add a song — it appears in the list below with type badge, date, and action buttons
4. Click **Remove** — card disappears

Ready to move to 5b?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:19:34 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/review-new.js`**

*Remove:*
```
// --- Auth guard ---
let currentUser = null;
onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUser = user;
});
```
*Insert:*
```
// --- Auth guard ---
let currentUser = null;
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  currentUser = user;
});

// --- Queue prefill ---
let prefill = null;
try {
  const raw = sessionStorage.getItem("prefill");
  if (raw) {
    prefill = JSON.parse(raw);
    sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
  }
} catch (e) {
  sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
}
```

```
  titleInput.value = prefill.title || "";
  if (prefill.type === "album") {
    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    document.querySelector('.type-btn[data-type="album"]').classList.add("active");
    reviewTypeInput.value = "album";
    titleLabel.textContent = "Album Title";
    titleInput.placeholder = "Album title";

}

// --- Star rating ---
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:19:59 PM)*

One bug found:

**Bug — `deleteDoc` failure shows a misleading error**
If `addDoc` succeeds but `deleteDoc` fails, the single catch block shows "Failed to submit review. Please try again." — but the review *was* submitted. The user would see an error and try resubmitting, creating a duplicate review. The queue deletion should be fire-and-forget since the review is the primary action.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:20:03 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/review-new.js`**

*Remove:*
```
  try {
    await addDoc(collection(db, "reviews"), review);
    if (prefill?.queueId) {
      await deleteDoc(doc(db, "queue", prefill.queueId));
    }
    window.location.href = "reviews.html";
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to submit review. Please try again.", true);
    submitBtn.disabled = false;
    submitBtn.textContent = "Submit Review";
  }
```
*Insert:*
```
  try {
    await addDoc(collection(db, "reviews"), review);
  } catch (err) {
    showStatus("Failed to submit review. Please try again.", true);
    submitBtn.disabled = false;
    submitBtn.textContent = "Submit Review";
    return;
  }

  // Review submitted — remove from queue if it came from there (best-effort)
  if (prefill?.queueId) {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "queue", prefill.queueId)).catch(() => {});
  }
  window.location.href = "reviews.html";
```

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";

     5→// --- Auth guard ---
     6→let currentUser = null;
     7→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
     8→  unsubscribe();
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  currentUser = user;
    14→});
    15→
    16→// --- Queue prefill ---
    17→let prefill = null;
    18→try {
    19→  const raw = sessionStorage.getItem("prefill");
    20→  if (raw) {
    21→    prefill = JSON.parse(raw);
    22→    sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
    23→  }
    24→} catch (e) {
    25→  sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
    26→}
    27→
    28→// --- Type toggle ---
    29→const typeButtons = document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn");
    30→const reviewTypeInput = document.getElementById("review-type");
    31→const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
    32→const titleInput = document.getElementById("review-title");
    33→
    34→typeButtons.forEach((btn) => {
    35→  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    36→    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    37→    btn.classList.add("active");
    38→    const selected = btn.dataset.type;
    39→    reviewTypeInput.value = selected;
    40→    titleLabel.textContent = selected === "song" ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    41→    titleInput.placeholder = selected === "song" ? "Song title" : "Album title";
    42→  });
    43→});
    44→
    45→// Apply prefill from queue if present
    46→if (prefill) {
    47→  document.getElementById("review-artist").value = prefill.artist || "";
    48→  titleInput.value = prefill.title || "";
    49→  if (prefill.type === "album") {
    50→    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    51→    document.querySelector('.type-btn[data-type="album"]').classList.add("active");
    52→    reviewTypeInput.value = "album";
    53→    titleLabel.textContent = "Album Title";
    54→    titleInput.placeholder = "Album title";
    55→  }
    56→}
    57→
    58→// --- Star rating ---
    59→const starContainer = document.getElementById("star-container");
    60→const stars = document.querySelectorAll(".star");
    61→const ratingInput = document.getElementById("review-rating");
    62→let selectedRating = 0;
    63→
    64→stars.forEach((star) => {
    65→  star.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
    66→    const val = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    67→    stars.forEach(s => {
    68→      s.classList.toggle("hovered", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= val);
    69→    });
    70→  });
    71→
    72→  star.addEventListener("click", () => {
    73→    selectedRating = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    74→    ratingInput.value = selectedRating;
    75→    stars.forEach(s => {
    76→      s.classList.toggle("selected", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= selectedRating);
    77→    });
    78→  });
    79→});
    80→
    81→// mouseleave on the container prevents flickering between individual stars
    82→starContainer.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
    83→  stars.forEach(s => s.classList.remove("hovered"));
    84→});
    85→
    86→// --- Form submission ---
    87→const form = document.getElementById("review-form");
    88→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    89→
    90→form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    91→  e.preventDefault();
    92→
    93→  if (!currentUser) {
    94→    showStatus("You must be logged in to submit a review.", true);
    95→    return;
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  if (selectedRating === 0) {
    99→    showStatus("Please select a star rating.", true);
   100→    return;
… (truncated — 137 lines total)
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:37:06 PM)*

The code is clean. Going through it carefully:

- **Auth guard** — `unsubscribe()` called correctly, `currentUser` null check on submit handles the edge case ✓
- **Prefill parsing** — wrapped in try/catch, `sessionStorage.removeItem` called in both success and error paths so stale data never persists ✓
- **Prefill application** — `prefill.artist || ""` and `prefill.title || ""` safely handle missing fields; type defaults to song if `prefill.type` is anything other than `"album"` ✓
- **`data-*` attribute escaping** — `escapeHtml` in `queue.js` HTML-encodes the attributes, the browser decodes them back when reading `dataset.*`, so values arrive in sessionStorage as the original strings ✓
- **Double-submit prevention** — button disabled immediately on submit, re-enabled only in the error path ✓
- **Queue deletion** — separated from the review write with `.catch(() => {})` so a failed deletion never triggers a misleading error or allows a duplicate review ✓
- **`deleteDoc` on a missing document** — Firestore treats this as a no-op, so if the queue item was already removed it won't throw ✓

One UX note worth flagging: after submitting a review from the queue, the redirect goes to `reviews.html` rather than back to `queue.html`. If a user has several items in their queue they want to review back-to-back, they'd have to navigate back manually. Want me to redirect to `queue.html` when the submission came from a prefill, or leave it pointing to `reviews.html`?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 3:37:48 PM)*

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:37:54 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/review-new.js`**

*Remove:*
```
  // Review submitted — remove from queue if it came from there (best-effort)
  if (prefill?.queueId) {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "queue", prefill.queueId)).catch(() => {});
  }
  window.location.href = "reviews.html";
```
*Insert:*
```
  // Review submitted — remove from queue if it came from there (best-effort)
  if (prefill?.queueId) {
    await deleteDoc(doc(db, "queue", prefill.queueId)).catch(() => {});
    window.location.href = "queue.html";
  } else {
    window.location.href = "reviews.html";
  }
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:39:55 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js`**
```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     6→import { setDoc, doc, collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     7→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     8→
     9→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
    10→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    11→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    12→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    13→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→
    16→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    17→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    18→  if (!user) {
    19→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    20→    return;
    21→  }
    22→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    23→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    24→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    25→  loadFollowStats(user.uid);
    26→});
    27→
    28→// --- Follower / following counts ---
    29→async function loadFollowStats(uid) {
    30→  try {
    31→    const [followersSnap, followingSnap] = await Promise.all([
    32→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", uid))),
    33→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid)))
    34→    ]);
    35→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = followersSnap.size;
    36→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = followingSnap.size;
    37→  } catch (err) {
    38→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = "—";
    39→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = "—";
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→// --- Update display name ---
    44→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    45→  e.preventDefault();
    46→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    47→  if (!newName) return;
    48→
    49→  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
    50→  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    51→    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    52→    return;
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  const saveBtn = editForm.querySelector(".save-btn");
    56→  saveBtn.disabled = true;
    57→  saveBtn.textContent = "Saving...";
    58→
    59→  try {
    60→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    61→    // setDoc with merge:true acts as upsert — safe for accounts created before Firestore docs were added
    62→    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", auth.currentUser.uid), { displayName: newName, displayNameLower: newName.toLowerCase() }, { merge: true });
    63→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    64→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    65→  } catch (err) {
    66→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    67→  } finally {
    68→    saveBtn.disabled = false;
    69→    saveBtn.textContent = "Save";
    70→  }
    71→});
    72→
    73→// --- Log out ---
    74→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    75→  await signOut(auth);
    76→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    77→});
    78→
    79→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    80→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    81→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    82→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    83→}
    84→
```

```
  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
```

```
// --- Follower / following counts ---
async function loadFollowStats(uid) {
```

*Insert:*
```
// --- Stats: followers, following, queue count ---
async function loadStats(uid) {
  try {
    const [followersSnap, followingSnap, queueSnap] = await Promise.all([
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", uid))),
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))),
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", uid)))
    ]);
    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = followersSnap.size;
    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = followingSnap.size;
    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = queueSnap.size;
  } catch (err) {
    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = "—";
    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = "—";
    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = "—";
  }
}
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\profile.js has been updated successfully.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:40:33 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `stats-row` has three items now but `profile.css` has no wrapping**
The `.stats-row` uses `display: flex` with `gap: 1.5rem`. Three stats fit fine on desktop, but on narrow mobile screens they could overflow. The existing `@media (max-width: 480px)` block in `profile.css` doesn't handle the stats row. Adding `flex-wrap: wrap` keeps them clean at any width.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:40:35 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/profile.css`**

*Remove:*
```
.stats-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.75rem;
}
```
*Insert:*
```
.stats-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.75rem;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:41:37 PM)*

Two issues found:

**Bug 1 — `loadStats` not awaited in the auth listener**
The auth callback at line 17 is `(user) => {}` — not async — so `loadStats(user.uid)` returns an unawaited Promise. Any error that escaped `loadStats`'s try/catch would be a silent unhandled rejection. All other auth listeners in the project are `async` with `await`. This should match.

**Bug 2 — `profile-nav` overflows on mobile with 6 links**
The inline `.profile-nav` style uses `flex: 1` on each link with no `flex-wrap`. Six links at `flex: 1` on a 320px screen results in illegibly small buttons. Needs `flex-wrap: wrap`.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:41:41 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/profile.js`**

*Remove:*
```
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
  loadStats(user.uid);
});
```
*Insert:*
```
const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
  unsubscribe();
  if (!user) {
    window.location.href = "login.html";
    return;
  }
  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
  await loadStats(user.uid);
});
```

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc, setDoc, deleteDoc, serverTimestamp } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const profileNameEl = document.getElementById("profile-name");
     6→const profileSubtext = document.getElementById("profile-subtext");
     7→const followBtn = document.getElementById("follow-btn");
     8→const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
     9→const listsList = document.getElementById("lists-list");
    10→
    11→let currentUser = null;
    12→let isFollowing = false;
    13→
    14→// Read uid from URL query string, fall back to sessionStorage for servers that strip query strings
    15→const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    16→const profileUid = params.get("uid") || sessionStorage.getItem("viewProfileUid");
    17→sessionStorage.removeItem("viewProfileUid");
    18→
    19→// --- Auth guard ---
    20→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    21→  unsubscribe();
    22→
    23→  if (!user) {
    24→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    25→    return;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  if (!profileUid) {
    29→    showError("No user specified.");
    30→    return;
    31→  }
    32→
    33→  // Prevent users from viewing their own public profile — redirect to profile page
    34→  if (profileUid === user.uid) {
    35→    window.location.href = "profile.html";
    36→    return;
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  currentUser = user;
    40→  await loadProfile(profileUid);
    41→});
    42→
    43→async function loadProfile(uid) {
    44→  try {
    45→    // Fetch user document
    46→    const userSnap = await getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid));
    47→
    48→    if (!userSnap.exists()) {
    49→      showError("User not found.");
    50→      return;
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    const userData = userSnap.data();
    54→    const displayName = userData.displayName || "Unknown User";
    55→    profileNameEl.textContent = displayName;
    56→    document.title = `Tune-In — ${displayName}`;
    57→
    58→    // Load follow state, reviews, and lists in parallel
    59→    await Promise.all([
    60→      loadFollowState(currentUser.uid, uid),
    61→      loadReviews(uid, displayName),
    62→      loadLists(uid, displayName)
    63→    ]);
    64→
    65→  } catch (err) {
    66→    showError("Failed to load profile. Please try again.");
    67→  }
    68→}
```

```js
155→            </div>

   163→  } catch (err) {
   164→    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews.</p>`;
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→
   168→async function loadLists(uid, displayName) {
   169→  try {
   170→    const snap = await getDocs(
   171→      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", uid))
   172→    );
   173→
   174→    if (snap.empty) {
   175→      listsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">${escapeHtml(displayName)} hasn't created any lists yet.</p>`;
   176→      return;
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    const lists = snap.docs
   180→      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
   181→      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
   182→
   183→    // Fetch item counts for all lists in parallel
   184→    const countSnaps = await Promise.all(
   185→      lists.map(list =>
   186→        getDocs(query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", list.id)))
   187→      )
   188→    );
   189→
   190→    listsList.innerHTML = lists.map((list, i) => {
   191→      const count = countSnaps[i].size;
   192→      return `
   193→        <a class="list-card" href="list-detail.html?id=${encodeURIComponent(list.id)}"
   194→           data-id="${list.id}">
   195→          <div class="list-card-body">
   196→            <span class="list-name-link">${escapeHtml(list.name)}</span>
   197→            ${list.description ? `<p class="list-desc">${escapeHtml(list.description)}</p>` : ""}
   198→          </div>
   199→          <div class="list-card-meta">
   200→            <span class="list-count">${count} ${count === 1 ? "item" : "items"}</span>
   201→            <span class="user-arrow">›</span>
   202→          </div>
   203→        </a>
   204→      `;
   205→    }).join("");
   206→
   207→    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
   208→    listsList.querySelectorAll(".list-card").forEach(card => {
   209→      card.addEventListener("click", () => {
   210→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewListId", card.dataset.id);
   211→      });
   212→    });
   213→
   214→  } catch (err) {
   215→    listsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load lists.</p>`;
   216→  }
   217→}
   218→
   219→function showError(message) {
   220→  profileNameEl.textContent = "Error";
   221→  followBtn.style.display = "none";
   222→  reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">${escapeHtml(message)}</p>`;
   223→  listsList.innerHTML = "";
   224→}
   225→
   226→function formatDate(timestamp) {
   227→  if (!timestamp) return "";
   228→  const date = new Date(timestamp.seconds * 1000);
   229→  return date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric" });
   230→}
   231→
   232→function escapeHtml(str = "") {
   233→  return str
   234→    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
   235→    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
   236→    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
   237→    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
   238→}
   239→
```

*Remove:*

```
function showError(message) {
  profileNameEl.textContent = "Error";
  followBtn.style.display = "none";

```
*Insert:*
```
async function loadQueue(uid, displayName) {
  try {
    const snap = await getDocs(
      query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", uid))
    );

    if (snap.empty) {
      queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">${escapeHtml(displayName)} hasn't added anything to their queue yet.</p>`;
      return;
    }

    const items = snap.docs
      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? 0));

    queueList.innerHTML = items.map(item => `
      <div class="review-card">
        <div class="review-header">
          <div>
            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(item.title)}</div>
            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(item.artist)}</div>
          </div>
          <div class="review-meta">
            <span class="type-badge">${escapeHtml(item.type)}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="review-footer">
          <span class="review-date">${formatDate(item.addedAt)}</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    `).join("");

  } catch (err) {
    queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load queue.</p>`;
  }
}

function showError(message) {
  profileNameEl.textContent = "Error";
  followBtn.style.display = "none";
  reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">${escapeHtml(message)}</p>`;
  listsList.innerHTML = "";
  queueList.innerHTML = "";
}
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:44:48 PM)*

Now reviewing what was just written:

**Bug 1 — `review-footer` on queue cards has no `margin-top` when there's no review text**
Queue cards have no review text, so the footer renders directly under the header. `.review-footer` has `margin-top: 0.75rem` which handles this correctly. ✓

**Bug 2 — `addedAt` sort fallback uses `?? 0`**
Unlike lists and queue in other files where we used `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` for pending `serverTimestamp()`, here the data is read-only — someone else's queue fetched from Firestore will always have a resolved timestamp. The `?? 0` fallback is safe here. ✓

**Bug 3 — `review-footer` missing `<p>` tag on date span**
The queue card uses `<span class="review-date">` while other review cards use `<p class="review-date">`. The CSS targets `.review-date` by class so both render identically. ✓

The code is clean. Here's what to test:

1. Visit another user's public profile — a **Listening Queue** section appears below Lists
2. Their queue items show title, artist, type badge, and date added — no action buttons
3. If their queue is empty, the appropriate empty state message shows

That completes Feature 5. We're now ready for the Firestore security rules pass whenever you are.

```js
    37→    return;
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  currentUser = user;
    41→  await loadProfile(profileUid);
    42→});
    43→
    44→async function loadProfile(uid) {
    45→  try {
    46→    // Fetch user document
    47→    const userSnap = await getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid));
    48→
    49→    if (!userSnap.exists()) {
    50→      showError("User not found.");
    51→      return;
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    const userData = userSnap.data();
    55→    const displayName = userData.displayName || "Unknown User";
    56→    profileNameEl.textContent = displayName;
    57→    document.title = `Tune-In — ${displayName}`;
    58→
    59→    // Load follow state, reviews, lists, and queue in parallel
    60→    await Promise.all([
    61→      loadFollowState(currentUser.uid, uid),
    62→      loadReviews(uid, displayName),
    63→      loadLists(uid, displayName),
    64→      loadQueue(uid, displayName)
    65→    ]);
    66→
    67→  } catch (err) {
    68→    showError("Failed to load profile. Please try again.");
    69→  }
    70→}
    71→
    72→async function loadFollowState(currentUid, targetUid) {
    73→  const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUid}_${targetUid}`);
    74→  const [followSnap, followerSnap] = await Promise.all([
    75→    getDoc(followDocRef),
    76→    getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", targetUid)))
    77→  ]);
    78→
    79→  isFollowing = followSnap.exists();
    80→  updateFollowButton();
    81→  profileSubtext.textContent = formatFollowerCount(followerSnap.size);
    82→  followBtn.disabled = false;
    83→}
    84→
    85→followBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    86→  if (!currentUser || !profileUid) return;
    87→
    88→  followBtn.disabled = true;
    89→  const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUser.uid}_${profileUid}`);
    90→
    91→  try {
    92→    if (isFollowing) {
    93→      await deleteDoc(followDocRef);
    94→      isFollowing = false;
    95→    } else {
    96→      await setDoc(followDocRef, {
    97→        followerId: currentUser.uid,
    98→        followingId: profileUid,
    99→        createdAt: serverTimestamp()
   100→      });
… (truncated — 277 lines total)
```

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:49:16 PM)*

Three things found:

**Bug 1 — `loadFollowState` has no try/catch**
It's the only one of the four parallel functions without error handling. If either Firestore read inside it fails, the error propagates through `Promise.all` to `loadProfile`'s catch, which calls `showError` — wiping out reviews, lists, and queue even if they all loaded successfully. The fix is a try/catch that gracefully degrades: leave the follow button disabled and the follower count blank rather than nuking the whole profile.

**Bug 2 — `loadLists` sort uses `?? 0` fallback**
Unlike `lists.js` which was fixed to use `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER`, the sort here at line 181 still uses `?? 0`. On the public profile this is read-only data so a pending timestamp is very unlikely, but it's inconsistent with the fix applied elsewhere.

**Bug 3 — `showError` leaves `listsList` and `queueList` empty with no message**
When the profile fails to load, `reviewsList` gets the error message but `listsList` and `queueList` are set to `""` — blank sections with no text. A user would see two empty areas below the error with no explanation.

Fixing all three:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:49:21 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/public-profile.js`**

*Remove:*
```
async function loadFollowState(currentUid, targetUid) {
  const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUid}_${targetUid}`);
  const [followSnap, followerSnap] = await Promise.all([
    getDoc(followDocRef),
    getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", targetUid)))
  ]);

  isFollowing = followSnap.exists();
  updateFollowButton();
  profileSubtext.textContent = formatFollowerCount(followerSnap.size);
  followBtn.disabled = false;
}
```
*Insert:*
```
async function loadFollowState(currentUid, targetUid) {
  try {
    const followDocRef = doc(db, "follows", `${currentUid}_${targetUid}`);
    const [followSnap, followerSnap] = await Promise.all([
      getDoc(followDocRef),
      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", targetUid)))
    ]);

    isFollowing = followSnap.exists();
    updateFollowButton();
    profileSubtext.textContent = formatFollowerCount(followerSnap.size);
    followBtn.disabled = false;
  } catch (err) {
    // Leave follow button disabled and follower count blank — don't break the rest of the profile
  }
}
```

```
      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
```
*Insert:*

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:51:09 PM)*

Six collections confirmed: `users`, `reviews`, `follows`, `lists`, `listItems`, `queue`. Now writing the rules:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:51:25 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/firestore.rules`**
```rules
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {

    // ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    // Any signed-in user
    function isAuth() {
      return request.auth != null;
    }

    // The signed-in user owns the document by a given field value
    function isOwner(uid) {
      return isAuth() && request.auth.uid == uid;
    }

    // Incoming data contains all required keys
    function hasFields(fields) {
      return request.resource.data.keys().hasAll(fields);
    }

    // ── users/{userId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Read:   any signed-in user (search, display names on public profiles)
    // Create: the user themselves, with required fields and matching uid
    // Update: the user themselves — displayName/displayNameLower only;
    //         uid, email, and createdAt are immutable
    // Delete: not allowed (no account deletion feature)

    match /users/{userId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isOwner(userId)
        && hasFields(['uid', 'displayName', 'displayNameLower', 'email', 'createdAt'])
        && request.resource.data.uid == userId
        && request.resource.data.displayName is string
        && request.resource.data.displayName.size() > 0;

      allow update: if isOwner(userId)
        && request.resource.data.uid == resource.data.uid
        && request.resource.data.email == resource.data.email
        && request.resource.data.displayName is string
        && request.resource.data.displayName.size() > 0;

      allow delete: if false;
    }

    // ── reviews/{reviewId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Read:   any signed-in user (public profiles, following feed)
    // Create: the user themselves, with required fields and valid rating
    // Update: not allowed (no edit review feature)
    // Delete: the owner only

    match /reviews/{reviewId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'title', 'artist', 'type', 'rating'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
        && request.resource.data.title is string
        && request.resource.data.title.size() > 0
        && request.resource.data.artist is string
        && request.resource.data.artist.size() > 0
        && request.resource.data.rating is number
        && request.resource.data.rating >= 1
        && request.resource.data.rating <= 5;

      allow update: if false;

      allow delete: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
    }

    // ── follows/{followId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Document ID is always "{followerId}_{followingId}".
    // Read:   any signed-in user (follower counts, follow state)
    // Create: the follower only; cannot follow yourself; ID must match data
    // Update: not allowed (follows are create/delete only)
    // Delete: the follower only

    match /follows/{followId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['followerId', 'followingId'])
        && request.resource.data.followerId == request.auth.uid
        && request.resource.data.followerId != request.resource.data.followingId
        && followId == request.resource.data.followerId + '_' + request.resource.data.followingId;

      allow update: if false;

      allow delete: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.followerId == request.auth.uid;
    }

    // ── lists/{listId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Read:   any signed-in user (public profiles)
    // Create: the user themselves, with a non-empty name
    // Update: the owner only; userId field is immutable
    // Delete: the owner only

    match /lists/{listId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'name'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
        && request.resource.data.name is string
        && request.resource.data.name.size() > 0;

      allow update: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
        && request.resource.data.userId == resource.data.userId;

      allow delete: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
    }

    // ── listItems/{itemId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Document ID is always "{listId}_{reviewId}".
    // Read:   any signed-in user (list detail, public profiles)
    // Create: the owner of the list only
    // Update: not allowed (items are create/delete only)
    // Delete: the owner only

    match /listItems/{itemId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'listId', 'reviewId'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;

      allow update: if false;

      allow delete: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
    }

    // ── queue/{itemId} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // Read:   any signed-in user (public profiles)
    // Create: the user themselves, with required fields
    // Update: not allowed (queue items are create/delete only)
    // Delete: the owner only

    match /queue/{itemId} {
      allow read: if isAuth();

      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'title', 'artist', 'type'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
        && request.resource.data.title is string
        && request.resource.data.title.size() > 0
        && request.resource.data.artist is string
        && request.resource.data.artist.size() > 0;

      allow update: if false;

      allow delete: if isAuth()
        && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
    }

  }
}
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\firestore.rules

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:51:36 PM)*

Now to apply these rules:

**Steps in the Firebase console:**
1. Go to [console.firebase.google.com](https://console.firebase.google.com) → your project
2. Click **Firestore Database** in the left sidebar
3. Click the **Rules** tab
4. Replace all existing content with the contents of `firestore.rules`
5. Click **Publish**

**What each rule enforces:**

| Collection | Read | Create | Update | Delete |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `users` | Any signed-in user | Owner, required fields, uid matches | Owner, uid/email immutable | Denied |
| `reviews` | Any signed-in user | Owner, valid fields, rating 1–5 | Denied | Owner only |
| `follows` | Any signed-in user | Follower only, no self-follow, ID matches data | Denied | Follower only |
| `lists` | Any signed-in user | Owner, non-empty name | Owner, userId immutable | Owner only |
| `listItems` | Any signed-in user | Owner, required fields | Denied | Owner only |
| `queue` | Any signed-in user | Owner, non-empty title/artist | Denied | Owner only |

**Notable protections added:**
- A user cannot follow themselves (`followerId != followingId`)
- The `follows` document ID is validated against the data it contains — prevents creating a follow doc with a mismatched ID
- `userId`, `uid`, and `email` fields are immutable after creation — prevents privilege escalation
- Review ratings are range-validated server-side (1–5), not just client-side
- `update` is explicitly denied on `reviews`, `follows`, `listItems`, and `queue` since none of those have edit features

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:54:13 PM)*

Two issues found after cross-referencing the rules against every write operation in the app:

**Bug 1 — `listItems` create rule doesn't verify list ownership**
The rule only checks `userId == request.auth.uid`, but doesn't verify the `listId` refers to a list the user actually owns. Without this, User A could call `setDoc` directly to add items to User B's list. Needs a cross-document lookup to confirm the target list belongs to the current user.

**Bug 2 — `users` update rule doesn't validate `displayNameLower`**
`displayNameLower` is written alongside `displayName` in `profile.js` but the update rule only validates `displayName`. A malicious client could write any value for `displayNameLower`, breaking search since `search.js` queries on that field.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 3:56:23 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/firestore.rules`**

*Remove:*
```
      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'listId', 'reviewId'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
```
*Insert:*
```
      allow create: if isAuth()
        && hasFields(['userId', 'listId', 'reviewId'])
        && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
        && get(/databases/$(database)/documents/lists/$(request.resource.data.listId)).data.userId == request.auth.uid;
```

```
firebase deploy --only firestore:rules
```

Or paste the contents of `firestore.rules` directly into the Firebase console under **Firestore → Rules**.

The app is now fully locked down. Here's a quick summary of where everything stands:

**All features shipped and tested:**

- Lists — create, detail, add reviews inline (4a–4d)
- Listening Queue — add, remove, write review with prefill (5a–5d)

- Public profiles show reviews, lists, and queue (5d)

**Security rules covering all six collections:**
- `users` — immutable uid/email, validated displayNameLower
- `reviews` — rating range 1–5, required fields
- `follows` — self-follow prevention, compound ID enforcement
- `lists` — owner-only update/delete
- `listItems` — cross-document ownership check on create
- `queue` — owner-only, required fields

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 4:02:50 PM)*

Here's the current navigation landscape:

- **`index.html`** — unauthenticated landing only, no nav
- **`profile.html`** — card-internal `.profile-nav` with 6 links, no top bar
- **Every other page** — `.top-nav` bar, but link sets are inconsistent (Feed is missing from some, profile nav duplicates them all)

Here are three directions to consider:

---

**Option A — Unified top navbar only**
Standardize `.top-nav` across every authenticated page with the same 6 links: Feed, Search, Lists, Queue, My Reviews, Profile. Remove the inline nav from `profile.html`. Simple, low-effort, familiar.

---

**Option B — Landing stays + `home.html` dashboard**
Keep `index.html` as the unauthenticated landing. Add a `home.html` post-login hub showing: your stats (followers, following, queue count), quick-action buttons (Write a Review, Add to Queue), and a preview of your feed. Redirect login → `home.html`. Unified top nav everywhere. The logo in the navbar links back to `home.html`.

---

**Option C — Bottom tab bar (mobile-first)**
Replace the top nav with a fixed bottom tab bar — 5 icons for Feed, Search, Lists, Queue, Profile. Very app-like, works well on mobile. Bigger CSS lift but matches how music apps like Spotify feel.

---

**My recommendation: Option B.** It gives you a natural landing spot after login, surfaces key info without hunting through pages, and the unified nav cleanup is straightforward given your existing `.top-nav` + `.card` structure. Option C could layer on top later.

Which direction appeals to you, or do you want to combine elements?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 4:04:26 PM)*

Let's do option B. Can we also update the styling to give the app greater contrast and a sleeker feel?
