```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs,
     4→  deleteDoc, doc, getDoc, setDoc, addDoc, serverTimestamp
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
     9→const listModal = document.getElementById("list-modal");
    10→const modalLists = document.getElementById("modal-lists");
    11→const modalClose = document.getElementById("modal-close");
    12→
    13→let currentUserId = null;
    14→// Map of reviewId → review data, used when adding to a list
    15→const reviewsMap = {};
    16→// Tracks which review is being added to a list
    17→let activeReviewId = null;
    18→// Cached list of user's lists — invalidated when a list is created/deleted elsewhere
    19→let cachedLists = null;
    20→
    21→// --- Auth guard ---
    22→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    23→  unsubscribe();
    24→  if (!user) {
    25→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    26→    return;
    27→  }
    28→  currentUserId = user.uid;
    29→  await loadReviews(user.uid);
    30→});
    31→
    32→async function loadReviews(userId) {
    33→  try {
    34→    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    35→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    36→
    37→    if (snapshot.empty) {
    38→      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
    39→        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
    40→      `;
    41→      return;
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    45→    const reviews = [];
    46→    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    47→    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
    48→
    49→    // Cache reviews for use in the Add to List modal
    50→    reviews.forEach(r => { reviewsMap[r.id] = r; });
    51→
    52→    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    53→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    54→      return `
    55→        <div class="review-card" data-id="${review.id}">
    56→          <div class="review-header">
    57→            <div>
    58→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    59→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    60→            </div>
    61→            <div class="review-meta">
    62→              <span class="type-badge type-${review.type}">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
    63→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    64→            </div>
    65→          </div>
    66→          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    67→          <div class="review-footer">
    68→            <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
    69→            <div class="review-actions">
    70→              <button class="add-to-list-btn" data-id="${review.id}">+ List</button>
    71→              <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${review.id}">Delete</button>
    72→            </div>
    73→          </div>
    74→        </div>
    75→      `;
    76→    }).join("");
    77→
    78→  } catch (err) {
    79→    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
    80→  }
    81→}
    82→
    83→// --- Event delegation: delete and add-to-list ---
    84→reviewsList.addEventListener("click", async (e) => {
    85→  if (e.target.closest(".delete-btn")) {
    86→    await handleDelete(e.target.closest(".delete-btn"));
    87→    return;
    88→  }
    89→  if (e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn")) {
    90→    await handleOpenModal(e.target.closest(".add-to-list-btn"));
    91→    return;
    92→  }
    93→});
    94→
    95→async function handleDelete(btn) {
    96→  if (!confirm("Delete this review? This cannot be undone.")) return;
    97→
    98→  const reviewId = btn.dataset.id;
    99→  btn.disabled = true;
   100→  btn.textContent = "Deleting...";
… (truncated — 276 lines total)
```

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/feed.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const feedList = document.getElementById("feed-list");
     6→
     7→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     8→  unsubscribe();
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  await loadFeed(user.uid);
    14→});
    15→
    16→async function loadFeed(uid) {
    17→  try {
    18→    // Get all users this person follows
    19→    const followsSnap = await getDocs(
    20→      query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))
    21→    );
    22→
    23→    if (followsSnap.empty) {
    24→      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Follow some users to see their reviews here.</p>`;
    25→      return;
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    const followingIds = followsSnap.docs.map(d => d.data().followingId);
    29→
    30→    // Firestore "in" supports up to 10 values — chunk the list
    31→    const chunks = [];
    32→    for (let i = 0; i < followingIds.length; i += 10) {
    33→      chunks.push(followingIds.slice(i, i + 10));
    34→    }
    35→
    36→    // Fetch reviews for all followed users in parallel
    37→    const reviewSnapshots = await Promise.all(
    38→      chunks.map(chunk =>
    39→        getDocs(query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "in", chunk)))
    40→      )
    41→    );
    42→
    43→    const reviews = [];
    44→    reviewSnapshots.forEach(snap => {
    45→      snap.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    46→    });
    47→
    48→    if (reviews.length === 0) {
    49→      feedList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No reviews from people you follow yet.</p>`;
    50→      return;
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    // Sort newest first
    54→    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
    55→
    56→    // Fetch display names for each unique author
    57→    const uniqueUserIds = [...new Set(reviews.map(r => r.userId))];
    58→    const userDocs = await Promise.all(
    59→      uniqueUserIds.map(id => getDoc(doc(db, "users", id)))
    60→    );
    61→    const nameMap = {};
    62→    userDocs.forEach(snap => {
    63→      if (snap.exists()) {
    64→        nameMap[snap.id] = snap.data().displayName || "Unknown User";
    65→      }
    66→    });
    67→
    68→    feedList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    69→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    70→      const authorName = nameMap[review.userId] || "Unknown User";
    71→      return `
    72→        <div class="review-card">
    73→          <div class="review-header">
    74→            <div>
    75→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    76→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    77→            </div>
    78→            <div class="review-meta">
    79→              <span class="type-badge type-${review.type}">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
    80→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    81→            </div>
    82→          </div>
    83→          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    84→          <div class="review-footer">
    85→            <a class="review-author" href="public-profile.html?uid=${encodeURIComponent(review.userId)}"
    86→               data-uid="${review.userId}">${escapeHtml(authorName)}</a>
    87→            <span class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</span>
    88→          </div>
    89→        </div>
    90→      `;
    91→    }).join("");
    92→
    93→    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    94→    feedList.querySelectorAll(".review-author").forEach(link => {
    95→      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
    96→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewProfileUid", link.dataset.uid);
    97→      });
    98→    });
    99→
   100→  } catch (err) {
… (truncated — 118 lines total)
```

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/queue.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs,
     4→  addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const queueForm = document.getElementById("queue-form");
     9→const queueTypeInput = document.getElementById("queue-type");
    10→const queueArtist = document.getElementById("queue-artist");
    11→const queueTitle = document.getElementById("queue-title");
    12→const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
    13→const addBtn = document.getElementById("add-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→const queueList = document.getElementById("queue-list");
    16→
    17→let currentUid = null;
    18→
    19→// --- Auth guard ---
    20→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    21→  unsubscribe();
    22→  if (!user) {
    23→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    24→    return;
    25→  }
    26→  currentUid = user.uid;
    27→  await loadQueue();
    28→});
    29→
    30→// --- Type toggle ---
    31→document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn").forEach(btn => {
    32→  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    33→    document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn").forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    34→    btn.classList.add("active");
    35→    queueTypeInput.value = btn.dataset.type;
    36→    const isSong = btn.dataset.type === "song";
    37→    titleLabel.textContent = isSong ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    38→    queueTitle.placeholder = isSong ? "Song title" : "Album title";
    39→  });
    40→});
    41→
    42→// --- Add to queue ---
    43→queueForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    44→  e.preventDefault();
    45→  if (!currentUid) return;
    46→
    47→  const artist = queueArtist.value.trim();
    48→  const title = queueTitle.value.trim();
    49→  const type = queueTypeInput.value;
    50→  if (!artist || !title) return;
    51→
    52→  addBtn.disabled = true;
    53→  addBtn.textContent = "Adding...";
    54→
    55→  try {
    56→    await addDoc(collection(db, "queue"), {
    57→      userId: currentUid,
    58→      artist,
    59→      title,
    60→      type,
    61→      addedAt: serverTimestamp()
    62→    });
    63→
    64→    queueArtist.value = "";
    65→    queueTitle.value = "";
    66→    showStatus("Added to queue.");
    67→    await loadQueue();
    68→  } catch (err) {
    69→    showStatus("Failed to add. Please try again.", true);
    70→  } finally {
    71→    addBtn.disabled = false;
    72→    addBtn.textContent = "Add to Queue";
    73→  }
    74→});
    75→
    76→// --- Load queue ---
    77→async function loadQueue() {
    78→  try {
    79→    const snap = await getDocs(
    80→      query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", currentUid))
    81→    );
    82→
    83→    if (snap.empty) {
    84→      queueList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Your queue is empty. Add something above.</p>`;
    85→      return;
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    const items = snap.docs
    89→      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
    90→      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));
    91→
    92→    queueList.innerHTML = items.map(item => `
    93→      <div class="review-card" data-id="${item.id}">
    94→        <div class="review-header">
    95→          <div>
    96→            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(item.title)}</div>
    97→            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(item.artist)}</div>
```

```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     6→import { setDoc, doc, collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     7→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     8→
     9→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
    10→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    11→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    12→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    13→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→
    16→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    17→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    18→  unsubscribe();
    19→  if (!user) {
    20→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    21→    return;
    22→  }
    23→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    24→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    25→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    26→  await loadStats(user.uid);
    27→});
    28→
    29→// --- Stats: followers, following, queue count ---
    30→async function loadStats(uid) {
    31→  try {
    32→    const [followersSnap, followingSnap, queueSnap] = await Promise.all([
    33→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", uid))),
    34→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))),
    35→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", uid)))
    36→    ]);
    37→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = followersSnap.size;
    38→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = followingSnap.size;
    39→    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = queueSnap.size;
    40→  } catch (err) {
    41→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = "—";
    42→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = "—";
    43→    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = "—";
    44→  }
    45→}
    46→
    47→// --- Update display name ---
    48→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    49→  e.preventDefault();
    50→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    51→  if (!newName) return;
    52→
    53→  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
    54→  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    55→    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    56→    return;
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  const saveBtn = editForm.querySelector(".save-btn");
    60→  saveBtn.disabled = true;
    61→  saveBtn.textContent = "Saving...";
    62→
    63→  try {
    64→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    65→    // setDoc with merge:true acts as upsert — safe for accounts created before Firestore docs were added
    66→    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", auth.currentUser.uid), { displayName: newName, displayNameLower: newName.toLowerCase() }, { merge: true });
    67→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    68→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    69→  } catch (err) {
    70→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    71→  } finally {
    72→    saveBtn.disabled = false;
    73→    saveBtn.textContent = "Save";
    74→  }
    75→});
    76→
    77→// --- Log out ---
    78→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    79→  await signOut(auth);
    80→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    81→});
    82→
    83→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    84→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    85→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    86→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    87→}
    88→
```

```js
    10→const queueList = document.getElementById("queue-list");

    12→let currentUser = null;

    30→    showError("No user specified.");
```

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/follows.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc
     4→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     5→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     6→
     7→const usersList = document.getElementById("users-list");
     8→const tabFollowers = document.getElementById("tab-followers");
     9→const tabFollowing = document.getElementById("tab-following");
    10→
    11→let currentUid = null;
    12→// Cache both tabs after first load to avoid re-fetching on tab switch
    13→const cache = { followers: null, following: null };
    14→// Discard stale async responses when tabs are switched rapidly
    15→let requestCount = 0;
    16→
    17→const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    18→const tabParam = params.get("tab") || sessionStorage.getItem("followsTab");
    19→sessionStorage.removeItem("followsTab");
    20→let activeTab = tabParam === "following" ? "following" : "followers";
    21→
    22→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    23→  unsubscribe();
    24→  if (!user) {
    25→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    26→    return;
    27→  }
    28→  currentUid = user.uid;
    29→  setActiveTab(activeTab);
    30→  await loadTab(activeTab);
    31→});
    32→
    33→// --- Tab toggle ---
    34→tabFollowers.addEventListener("click", () => {
    35→  if (!currentUid || activeTab === "followers") return;
    36→  activeTab = "followers";
    37→  setActiveTab("followers");
    38→  loadTab("followers");
    39→});
    40→
    41→tabFollowing.addEventListener("click", () => {
    42→  if (!currentUid || activeTab === "following") return;
    43→  activeTab = "following";
    44→  setActiveTab("following");
    45→  loadTab("following");
    46→});
    47→
    48→function setActiveTab(tab) {
    49→  tabFollowers.classList.toggle("active", tab === "followers");
    50→  tabFollowing.classList.toggle("active", tab === "following");
    51→  document.title = tab === "followers" ? "Tune-In — Followers" : "Tune-In — Following";
    52→}
    53→
    54→// --- Load a tab ---
    55→async function loadTab(tab) {
    56→  if (cache[tab]) {
    57→    renderUsers(cache[tab], tab);
    58→    return;
    59→  }
    60→
    61→  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
    62→  usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>`;
    63→
    64→  try {
    65→    const isFollowers = tab === "followers";
    66→    const field = isFollowers ? "followingId" : "followerId";
    67→    const uidField = isFollowers ? "followerId" : "followingId";
    68→
    69→    const snap = await getDocs(
    70→      query(collection(db, "follows"), where(field, "==", currentUid))
    71→    );
    72→
    73→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    74→
    75→    if (snap.empty) {
    76→      cache[tab] = [];
    77→      renderUsers([], tab);
    78→      return;
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    // Fetch user documents for all follows in parallel
    82→    const uids = snap.docs.map(d => d.data()[uidField]);
    83→    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uids.map(uid => getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid))));
    84→
    85→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    86→
    87→    const users = userSnaps
    88→      .filter(s => s.exists())
    89→      .map(s => ({ uid: s.id, displayName: s.data().displayName || "Unknown User" }))
    90→      .sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));
    91→
    92→    cache[tab] = users;
    93→    renderUsers(users, tab);
    94→  } catch (err) {
    95→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    96→    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load. Please try again.</p>`;
    97→  }
    98→}
    99→
   100→// --- Render ---
… (truncated — 133 lines total)
```

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs,
     4→  doc, getDoc, deleteDoc
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const listTitle = document.getElementById("list-title");
     9→const listDesc = document.getElementById("list-desc");
    10→const itemsList = document.getElementById("items-list");
    11→
    12→// Read listId from URL query string with sessionStorage fallback
    13→const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    14→const listId = params.get("id") || sessionStorage.getItem("viewListId");
    15→sessionStorage.removeItem("viewListId");
    16→
    17→let isOwner = false;
    18→
    19→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    20→  unsubscribe();
    21→  if (!user) {
    22→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    23→    return;
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  if (!listId) {
    27→    showError("No list specified.");
    28→    return;
    29→  }
    30→
    31→  await loadList(user.uid);
    32→});
    33→
    34→async function loadList(currentUid) {
    35→  try {
    36→    // Fetch the list document to get name, description, and verify ownership
    37→    const listSnap = await getDoc(doc(db, "lists", listId));
    38→
    39→    if (!listSnap.exists()) {
    40→      showError("List not found.");
    41→      return;
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    const listData = listSnap.data();
    45→    isOwner = listData.userId === currentUid;
    46→
    47→    listTitle.textContent = listData.name;
    48→    document.title = `Tune-In — ${listData.name}`;
    49→
    50→    if (listData.description) {
    51→      listDesc.textContent = listData.description;
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    await loadItems();
    55→  } catch (err) {
    56→    showError("Failed to load list. Please try again.");
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→async function loadItems() {
    61→  try {
    62→    const snap = await getDocs(
    63→      query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", listId))
    64→    );
    65→
    66→    if (snap.empty) {
    67→      itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">This list has no items yet.</p>`;
    68→      return;
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    // Sort by addedAt descending — most recently added first
    72→    const items = snap.docs
    73→      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
    74→      .sort((a, b) => (b.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.addedAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));
    75→
    76→    itemsList.innerHTML = items.map(item => {
    77→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(item.rating) || 0));
    78→      return `
    79→        <div class="review-card" data-id="${item.id}">
    80→          <div class="review-header">
    81→            <div>
    82→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(item.title)}</div>
    83→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(item.artist)}</div>
    84→            </div>
    85→            <div class="review-meta">
    86→              <span class="type-badge type-${item.type}">${escapeHtml(item.type)}</span>
    87→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    88→            </div>
    89→          </div>
    90→          ${item.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(item.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    91→          <div class="review-footer">
    92→            <p class="review-date">${formatDate(item.createdAt)}</p>
    93→            ${isOwner ? `<button class="delete-btn" data-id="${item.id}">Remove</button>` : ""}
    94→          </div>
    95→        </div>
    96→      `;
    97→    }).join("");
    98→
    99→  } catch (err) {
   100→    itemsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load items. Please try again.</p>`;
… (truncated — 144 lines total)
```

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→// --- Auth guard ---
     6→let currentUser = null;
     7→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
     8→  unsubscribe();
     9→  if (!user) {
    10→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    11→    return;
    12→  }
    13→  currentUser = user;
    14→});
    15→
    16→// --- Queue prefill ---
    17→let prefill = null;
    18→try {
    19→  const raw = sessionStorage.getItem("prefill");
    20→  if (raw) {
    21→    prefill = JSON.parse(raw);
    22→    sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
    23→  }
    24→} catch (e) {
    25→  sessionStorage.removeItem("prefill");
    26→}
    27→
    28→// --- Type toggle ---
    29→const typeButtons = document.querySelectorAll(".type-btn");
    30→const reviewTypeInput = document.getElementById("review-type");
    31→const titleLabel = document.getElementById("title-label");
    32→const titleInput = document.getElementById("review-title");
    33→
    34→typeButtons.forEach((btn) => {
    35→  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    36→    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    37→    btn.classList.add("active");
    38→    const selected = btn.dataset.type;
    39→    reviewTypeInput.value = selected;
    40→    titleLabel.textContent = selected === "song" ? "Song Title" : "Album Title";
    41→    titleInput.placeholder = selected === "song" ? "Song title" : "Album title";
    42→  });
    43→});
    44→
    45→// Apply prefill from queue if present
    46→if (prefill) {
    47→  document.getElementById("review-artist").value = prefill.artist || "";
    48→  titleInput.value = prefill.title || "";
    49→  if (prefill.type === "album") {
    50→    typeButtons.forEach(b => b.classList.remove("active"));
    51→    document.querySelector('.type-btn[data-type="album"]').classList.add("active");
    52→    reviewTypeInput.value = "album";
    53→    titleLabel.textContent = "Album Title";
    54→    titleInput.placeholder = "Album title";
    55→  }
    56→}
    57→
    58→// --- Star rating ---
    59→const starContainer = document.getElementById("star-container");
    60→const stars = document.querySelectorAll(".star");
    61→const ratingInput = document.getElementById("review-rating");
    62→let selectedRating = 0;
    63→
    64→stars.forEach((star) => {
    65→  star.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
    66→    const val = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    67→    stars.forEach(s => {
    68→      s.classList.toggle("hovered", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= val);
    69→    });
    70→  });
    71→
    72→  star.addEventListener("click", () => {
    73→    selectedRating = parseInt(star.dataset.value);
    74→    ratingInput.value = selectedRating;
    75→    stars.forEach(s => {
    76→      s.classList.toggle("selected", parseInt(s.dataset.value) <= selectedRating);
    77→    });
    78→  });
    79→});
    80→
    81→// mouseleave on the container prevents flickering between individual stars
    82→starContainer.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => {
    83→  stars.forEach(s => s.classList.remove("hovered"));
    84→});
    85→
    86→// --- Form submission ---
    87→const form = document.getElementById("review-form");
    88→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    89→
    90→form.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    91→  e.preventDefault();
    92→
    93→  if (!currentUser) {
    94→    showStatus("You must be logged in to submit a review.", true);
    95→    return;
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  if (selectedRating === 0) {
    99→    showStatus("Please select a star rating.", true);
   100→    return;
```

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/lists.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs,
     4→  addDoc, deleteDoc, doc, serverTimestamp
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const createForm = document.getElementById("create-list-form");
     9→const listNameInput = document.getElementById("list-name");
    10→const listDescInput = document.getElementById("list-desc");
    11→const createBtn = document.getElementById("create-btn");
    12→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    13→const listsContainer = document.getElementById("lists-container");
    14→
    15→let currentUid = null;
    16→
    17→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    18→  unsubscribe();
    19→  if (!user) {
    20→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    21→    return;
    22→  }
    23→  currentUid = user.uid;
    24→  await loadLists();
    25→});
    26→
    27→// --- Create list ---
    28→createForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    29→  e.preventDefault();
    30→  if (!currentUid) return;
    31→
    32→  const name = listNameInput.value.trim();
    33→  const description = listDescInput.value.trim();
    34→  if (!name) return;
    35→
    36→  createBtn.disabled = true;
    37→  createBtn.textContent = "Creating...";
    38→
    39→  try {
    40→    await addDoc(collection(db, "lists"), {
    41→      userId: currentUid,
    42→      name,
    43→      description,
    44→      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    45→    });
    46→    listNameInput.value = "";
    47→    listDescInput.value = "";
    48→    showStatus("List created.");
    49→    await loadLists();
    50→  } catch (err) {
    51→    showStatus("Failed to create list. Please try again.", true);
    52→  } finally {
    53→    createBtn.disabled = false;
    54→    createBtn.textContent = "Create List";
    55→  }
    56→});
    57→
    58→// --- Load lists ---
    59→async function loadLists() {
    60→  try {
    61→    const snap = await getDocs(
    62→      query(collection(db, "lists"), where("userId", "==", currentUid))
    63→    );
    64→
    65→    if (snap.empty) {
    66→      listsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No lists yet. Create one above.</p>`;
    67→      return;
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    // Sort newest first client-side (avoids composite index requirement)
    71→    const lists = snap.docs
    72→      .map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...d.data() }))
    73→      .sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER));
    74→    const countSnaps = await Promise.all(
    75→      lists.map(list =>
    76→        getDocs(query(collection(db, "listItems"), where("listId", "==", list.id)))
    77→      )
    78→    );
    79→
    80→    listsContainer.innerHTML = lists.map((list, i) => {
    81→      const count = countSnaps[i].size;
    82→      return `
    83→        <div class="list-card" data-id="${list.id}">
    84→          <div class="list-card-body">
    85→            <a class="list-name-link" href="list-detail.html?id=${encodeURIComponent(list.id)}"
    86→               data-id="${list.id}">${escapeHtml(list.name)}</a>
    87→            ${list.description ? `<p class="list-desc">${escapeHtml(list.description)}</p>` : ""}
    88→          </div>
    89→          <div class="list-card-meta">
    90→            <span class="list-count">${count} ${count === 1 ? "item" : "items"}</span>
    91→            <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${list.id}">Delete</button>
    92→          </div>
    93→        </div>
    94→      `;
    95→    }).join("");
    96→
    97→    // sessionStorage fallback for npx serve clean URLs
    98→    listsContainer.querySelectorAll(".list-name-link").forEach(link => {
    99→      link.addEventListener("click", () => {
   100→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewListId", link.dataset.id);
```

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/search.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const searchForm = document.getElementById("search-form");
     6→const searchInput = document.getElementById("search-input");
     7→const resultsContainer = document.getElementById("search-results");
     8→
     9→let currentUserId = null;
    10→let requestCount = 0;
    11→
    12→// --- Auth guard: disable input until session is confirmed ---
    13→searchInput.disabled = true;
    14→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    15→  if (!user) {
    16→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    17→    return;
    18→  }
    19→  currentUserId = user.uid;
    20→  searchInput.disabled = false;
    21→  searchInput.focus();
    22→});
    23→
    24→// --- Debounced search as user types ---
    25→let debounceTimer;
    26→searchInput.addEventListener("input", () => {
    27→  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    28→  debounceTimer = setTimeout(runSearch, 300);
    29→});
    30→
    31→// --- Also run on explicit submit ---
    32→searchForm.addEventListener("submit", (e) => {
    33→  e.preventDefault();
    34→  clearTimeout(debounceTimer);
    35→  runSearch();
    36→});
    37→
    38→async function runSearch() {
    39→  const term = searchInput.value.trim().toLowerCase();
    40→
    41→  if (!term) {
    42→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = "";
    43→    return;
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  // Track this request — discard results if a newer one has started
    47→  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
    48→  resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Searching...</p>`;
    49→
    50→  try {
    51→    const q = query(
    52→      collection(db, "users"),
    53→      where("displayNameLower", ">=", term),
    54→      where("displayNameLower", "<=", term + "\uf8ff")
    55→    );
    56→
    57→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    58→
    59→    // Discard stale responses
    60→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    61→
    62→    const users = [];
    63→    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => {
    64→      if (docSnap.id !== currentUserId) {
    65→        // Use docSnap.id as the uid — it's always the user's UID regardless of document fields
    66→        users.push({ uid: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() });
    67→      }
    68→    });
    69→
    70→    if (users.length === 0) {
    71→      resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No users found for "${escapeHtml(term)}".</p>`;
    72→      return;
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = users.map(user => `
    76→      <a href="public-profile.html" data-uid="${escapeHtml(user.uid)}" class="user-card">
    77→        <span class="user-name">${escapeHtml(user.displayName)}</span>
    78→        <span class="user-arrow">→</span>
    79→      </a>
    80→    `).join("");
    81→
    82→    // Store uid in sessionStorage on click so it survives navigation
    83→    resultsContainer.querySelectorAll(".user-card").forEach(card => {
    84→      card.addEventListener("click", () => {
    85→        sessionStorage.setItem("viewProfileUid", card.dataset.uid);
    86→      });
    87→    });
    88→
    89→  } catch (err) {
    90→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    91→    resultsContainer.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Search failed. Please try again.</p>`;
    92→  }
    93→}
    94→
    95→function escapeHtml(str = "") {
    96→  return str
    97→    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    98→    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    99→    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
   100→    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
… (truncated — 102 lines total)
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 5:39:52 PM)*

Found three bugs. Fixing them now:

**Bug 1 — `follows.html` default tab has no `active` class**: Both tab buttons are unstyled until auth resolves, causing a visual flash.

**Bug 2 — `top-nav` uses `height` instead of `min-height`**: With the padding now on nav links, they're slightly wider/taller. `height: 4.5rem` clips any wrapped links on narrow screens. Needs `min-height`.

**Bug 3 — `search.js` puts no UID in the `href`** of search result links: Every other navigation to `public-profile.html` includes `?uid=...` in the URL. Search results link to bare `public-profile.html`, meaning opening a result in a new tab will fail (sessionStorage doesn't cross tabs).

Three bugs fixed:

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 5:42:59 PM)*

Here's the plan before I implement:

1. **Flash-prevention inline script** — a tiny `<head>` script on every page that reads `localStorage` and applies the theme class before the first paint, preventing a flicker
2. **`js/theme.js`** — shared module that wires the toggle button, updates the icon, and saves the preference to `localStorage`
3. **Toggle button in nav** — added to all navs and the auth card; uses sun/moon icons
4. **Light mode CSS block** in `reviews.css` and `auth.css` — `html.light-mode` scoped overrides for body background, nav, cards, text, inputs, and all supporting components

The class is applied to `<html>` (not `<body>`) so the inline `<head>` script can apply it before anything renders.

Let me implement each step now.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:09:13 PM)*

The structure is clear — `type: "user"` entries have the user's message as a string, and `type: "assistant"` entries have a content array with text blocks. I'll write a small Node script to extract and format those into a readable transcript.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:09:20 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/format-transcript.js`**
```js
// format-transcript.js
// Usage: node format-transcript.js
// Reads the Claude session JSONL and writes a readable transcript to transcript.md

const fs = require("fs");
const path = require("path");

const INPUT = path.join(
  process.env.USERPROFILE,
  ".claude", "projects",
  "C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm",
  "e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl"
);
const OUTPUT = path.join(__dirname, "transcript.md");

const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, "utf8").trim().split("\n");

const turns = [];

for (const line of lines) {
  let entry;
  try { entry = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }

  // User turns
  if (entry.type === "user" && entry.message?.role === "user") {
    const content = entry.message.content;
    const text = typeof content === "string"
      ? content
      : Array.isArray(content)
        ? content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n")
        : null;
    if (text) turns.push({ role: "User", text: text.trim(), ts: entry.timestamp });
  }

  // Assistant turns — collect only final text blocks (skip tool_use / thinking)
  if (entry.type === "assistant" && entry.message?.role === "assistant") {
    const content = entry.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const text = content
      .filter(b => b.type === "text")
      .map(b => b.text)
      .join("\n")
      .trim();
    if (text) turns.push({ role: "Claude", text, ts: entry.timestamp });
  }
}

// Build markdown output
let out = "# Tune-In — Full Session Transcript\n\n";
for (const turn of turns) {
  const date = turn.ts ? new Date(turn.ts).toLocaleString() : "";
  out += `---\n\n**${turn.role}**${date ? ` *(${date})*` : ""}\n\n${turn.text}\n\n`;
}

fs.writeFileSync(OUTPUT, out, "utf8");
console.log(`Transcript written to: ${OUTPUT}`);
console.log(`Total turns: ${turns.length}`);
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\format-transcript.js

```bash
const fs = require('fs');
const lines = fs.readFileSync('C:/Users/brock/.claude/projects/C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm/e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl', 'utf8').trim().split('\n');
```

```bash
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type === 'user' && e.message?.role === 'user') {
    const c = e.message.content;
    if (typeof c === 'string') { realUser++; continue; }
    if (Array.isArray(c)) {
      if (c.some(b => b.type === 'tool_result')) { toolResult++; continue; }
      if (c.some(b => b.type === 'text' && b.text?.includes('conversation has been compacted'))) { compaction++; continue; }
      realUser++;
    }
  }
}
console.log('Real user messages:', realUser);
console.log('Tool result messages:', toolResult);
console.log('Compaction messages:', compaction);
"
```

```md
  1910→1. Primary Request and Intent:
  1911→   The user is building "Tune-In" — a Letterboxd-style music review social platform using HTML, CSS modules, and Firebase. The project is deployed to GitHub Pages (repo: br907888/tune-in). The user requested incremental feature development with code reviews and bug fixes after each feature, responsive CSS passes after each feature, and explicit walkthroughs for Firebase setup steps.   1914→   - Firebase Authentication (Email/Password) via CDN ES modules (v10.14.1)
  1915→   - Firestore database with collections: `users`, `reviews`, `follows`
  1916→   - ES module imports via `https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/`
  1917→   - `sessionStorage` for passing uid between pages (workaround for `npx serve` clean URLs stripping query strings)
  1918→   - Firestore prefix range query using `\uf8ff` sentinel for case-insensitive search on `displayNameLower`
  1919→   - Event delegation for dynamic DOM elements
  1920→   - `Promise.all` for parallel Firestore reads
  1921→   - `setDoc` with `{ merge: true }` as upsert pattern
  1922→   - Composite document ID pattern (`{followerId}_{followingId}`) for O(1) follow lookups
  1923→   - `isSubmitting` flag to prevent `onAuthStateChanged` redirect race
  1924→   - Request counter pattern (`requestCount`) to discard stale async search results
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:21:29 PM)*

No — the current script only extracts Claude's plain text responses and your messages. It skips all tool calls, meaning the actual code written/edited, file reads, and terminal command output are not in the transcript.

Here's what's currently included vs excluded:

| Included | Excluded |
|---|---|
| Your messages | Code written via `Write`/`Edit` tools |
| Claude's text explanations | `Read` file contents |
| | `Bash` commands and their output |
| | Tool results (Firestore rules, CSS blocks, etc.) |

If you want the full record including code, I can update the script to also capture tool calls. The tradeoff is the transcript becomes much longer. You have two options:

1. **Full** — include every tool call and its result (code, bash output, file reads, everything)
2. **Code only** — include `Write`/`Edit`/`Bash` tool calls but skip `Read` results (gives you the changes made without all the file reads that were just for context)

Which would you prefer, or would you rather keep the current clean conversation-only format?

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:23:59 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/format-transcript.js`**
```js
// format-transcript.js
// Usage: node format-transcript.js
// Writes a fully readable transcript (messages + tool calls + results) to transcript.md

const fs = require("fs");
const path = require("path");

const INPUT = path.join(
  process.env.USERPROFILE,
  ".claude", "projects",
  "C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm",
  "e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl"
);
const OUTPUT = path.join(__dirname, "transcript.md");

const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, "utf8").trim().split("\n");

// ── Pass 1: index all tool results by tool_use_id ──────────────────────────
const toolResults = {};
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type !== "user" || e.message?.role !== "user") continue;
  const content = e.message.content;
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
  for (const block of content) {
    if (block.type !== "tool_result" || !block.tool_use_id) continue;
    const text = Array.isArray(block.content)
      ? block.content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n")
      : typeof block.content === "string" ? block.content : "";
    if (text.trim()) toolResults[block.tool_use_id] = text.trim();
  }
}

// ── Pass 2: build turns ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const turns = [];

for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }

  // ── User turns ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (e.type === "user" && e.message?.role === "user") {
    const content = e.message.content;
    // Skip pure tool-result messages (already paired with their tool calls)
    if (Array.isArray(content) && content.some(b => b.type === "tool_result")) continue;

    const text = typeof content === "string"
      ? content
      : Array.isArray(content)
        ? content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n")
        : null;
    if (!text) continue;
    const trimmed = text.trim();
    // Skip injected compaction summaries
    if (trimmed.startsWith("This session is being continued from a previous conversation")) continue;
    turns.push({ role: "User", text: trimmed, ts: e.timestamp });
  }

  // ── Assistant turns ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (e.type === "assistant" && e.message?.role === "assistant") {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;

    const parts = [];

    // Plain text from Claude
    const textContent = content
      .filter(b => b.type === "text")
      .map(b => b.text)
      .join("\n")
      .trim();
    if (textContent) parts.push(textContent);

    // Tool calls (with their results folded in)
    for (const block of content.filter(b => b.type === "tool_use")) {
      const result = toolResults[block.id] || null;
      parts.push(formatToolCall(block, result));
    }

    if (parts.length > 0) {
      turns.push({ role: "Claude", text: parts.join("\n\n"), ts: e.timestamp });
    }
  }
}

// ── Tool call formatter ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function formatToolCall(block, result) {
  const name  = block.name;
  const input = block.input || {};
  let out = "";

  switch (name) {

    case "Write": {
      const filePath = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      const ext = filePath.split(".").pop() || "";
      out = `**✏️ Write \`${filePath}\`**\n\`\`\`${ext}\n${(input.content || "").trimEnd()}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) out += `\n> ✓ ${result.split("\n")[0]}`;
      break;
    }

    case "Edit": {
      const filePath = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      out = `**✏️ Edit \`${filePath}\`**`;
      if (input.old_string !== undefined) {
        out += `\n\n*Remove:*\n\`\`\`\n${input.old_string}\n\`\`\``;
        out += `\n*Insert:*\n\`\`\`\n${input.new_string || ""}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      if (result) out += `\n> ✓ ${result.split("\n")[0]}`;
      break;
    }

    case "Bash": {
      const cmd = (input.command || "").trim();
      out = `**💻 Bash**\n\`\`\`bash\n${cmd}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) {
        const capped = result.length > 3000
          ? result.slice(0, 3000) + "\n… (output truncated)"
          : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`\n${capped}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }

    case "Read": {
      const filePath = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      const ext = filePath.split(".").pop() || "";
      out = `**📖 Read \`${filePath}\`**`;
      if (result) {
        const resultLines = result.split("\n");
        const preview = resultLines.length > 100
          ? resultLines.slice(0, 100).join("\n") + `\n… (truncated — ${resultLines.length} lines total)`
          : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`${ext}\n${preview}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }

    case "Glob": {
      out = `**🔍 Glob \`${input.pattern || ""}\`**${input.path ? ` in \`${input.path}\`` : ""}`;
      if (result) {
        const capped = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + "\n…" : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`\n${capped}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }

    case "Grep": {
      out = `**🔍 Grep \`${input.pattern || ""}\`**${input.path ? ` in \`${input.path}\`` : ""}`;
      if (result) {
        const capped = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + "\n…" : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`\n${capped}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }

    default: {
      out = `**🔧 ${name}**\n\`\`\`json\n${JSON.stringify(input, null, 2).slice(0, 800)}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) out += `\n> Result: ${result.slice(0, 300)}`;
    }
  }

  return out;
}

// ── Write output ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
let out = "# Tune-In — Full Session Transcript\n\n";
for (const turn of turns) {
  const date = turn.ts ? new Date(turn.ts).toLocaleString() : "";
  out += `---\n\n**${turn.role}**${date ? ` *(${date})*` : ""}\n\n${turn.text}\n\n`;
}

fs.writeFileSync(OUTPUT, out, "utf8");
console.log(`Transcript written to: ${OUTPUT}`);
console.log(`Total turns: ${turns.length}`);
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\format-transcript.js has been updated successfully.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:33:00 PM)*

**💻 Bash**
```bash
node << 'EOF'
const fs = require('fs');
const lines = fs.readFileSync('C:/Users/brock/.claude/projects/C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm/e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl', 'utf8').trim().split('\n');

// Collect tool results
const toolResults = {};
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type !== 'user' || e.message?.role !== 'user') continue;
  const content = e.message.content;
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
  for (const block of content) {
    if (block.type !== 'tool_result' || !block.tool_use_id) continue;
    const text = Array.isArray(block.content)
      ? block.content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n')
      : typeof block.content === 'string' ? block.content : '';
    if (text.trim()) toolResults[block.tool_use_id] = text.trim();
  }
}

// Find first tool_use entry and simulate output
let count = 0;
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type === 'assistant' && e.message?.role === 'assistant') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (Array.isArray(content) && content.some(b => b.type === 'tool_use')) {
      const tu = content.find(b => b.type === 'tool_use');
      const hasResult = !!toolResults[tu.id];
      console.log('Tool:', tu.name, '| id:', tu.id.slice(0,12), '| has result:', hasResult);
      if (++count >= 5) break;
    }
  }
}
EOF
```

```bash
node << 'EOF'
const fs = require('fs');
const lines = fs.readFileSync('C:/Users/brock/.claude/projects/C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm/e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl', 'utf8').trim().split('\n');

const turns = [];
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type === 'assistant' && e.message?.role === 'assistant') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const hasTool = content.some(b => b.type === 'tool_use');
    const hasText = content.some(b => b.type === 'text');
    if (hasTool) turns.push({ ts: e.timestamp, hasTool, hasText, toolName: content.find(b => b.type === 'tool_use').name });
  }
  if (turns.length >= 3) break;
}
console.log(JSON.stringify(turns, null, 2));
EOF
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:34:11 PM)*

**💻 Bash**
```bash
node << 'EOF'
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const INPUT = path.join(process.env.USERPROFILE, '.claude', 'projects', 'C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm', 'e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl');
const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, 'utf8').trim().split('\n');

// Pass 1
const toolResults = {};
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type !== 'user' || e.message?.role !== 'user') continue;
  const content = e.message.content;
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
  for (const block of content) {
    if (block.type !== 'tool_result' || !block.tool_use_id) continue;
    const text = Array.isArray(block.content) ? block.content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n') : typeof block.content === 'string' ? block.content : '';
    const cleaned = text.replace(/<system-reminder>[\s\S]*?<\/system-reminder>/g, '').trim();
    if (cleaned) toolResults[block.tool_use_id] = cleaned;
  }
}

// Pass 2 - count what ends up in turns
const turns = [];
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type === 'assistant' && e.message?.role === 'assistant') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const parts = [];
    const textContent = content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n').trim();
    if (textContent) parts.push(textContent);
    for (const block of content.filter(b => b.type === 'tool_use')) {
      parts.push('TOOL:' + block.name);
    }
    if (parts.length > 0) turns.push({ hasTool: parts.some(p => p.startsWith('TOOL:')), text: parts[0].slice(0, 40) });
  }
}

const withTool = turns.filter(t => t.hasTool).length;
const textOnly = turns.filter(t => !t.hasTool).length;
console.log('turns with tool:', withTool);
console.log('turns text only:', textOnly);
console.log('total turns:', turns.length);
console.log('first 3 turns:', JSON.stringify(turns.slice(0, 3), null, 2));
EOF
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:34:33 PM)*

**💻 Bash**
```bash
node << 'EOF'
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');
const INPUT = path.join(process.env.USERPROFILE, '.claude', 'projects', 'C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm', 'e29a692e-c8fb-4385-957d-e170df167ea6.jsonl');
const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, 'utf8').trim().split('\n');

function formatToolCall(block, result) {
  const name  = block.name;
  const input = block.input || {};
  let out = "";
  switch (name) {
    case "Write": {
      const filePath = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      const ext = filePath.split(".").pop() || "";
      out = "Write: " + filePath + " ext=" + ext + " contentLen=" + (input.content||"").length;
      break;
    }
    default: out = "DEFAULT:" + name;
  }
  return out;
}

// Find first Write entry
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type === 'assistant' && e.message?.role === 'assistant') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const tu = content.find(b => b.type === 'tool_use' && b.name === 'Write');
    if (tu) {
      console.log('raw result:', formatToolCall(tu, null));
      console.log('file_path:', tu.input.file_path);
      console.log('content length:', (tu.input.content||'').length);
      break;
    }
  }
}
EOF
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:37:29 PM)*

**Step 1 of 3 — `js/theme.js`**

This handles reading the saved preference, toggling the class on `<html>`, persisting to `localStorage`, and updating the button icon.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:37:32 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/theme.js`**
```js
// theme.js — light/dark mode toggle
// Applied to <html> so the inline head script can set it before first paint.

const STORAGE_KEY = "tune-in-theme";

const btn = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");

function isLight() {
  return document.documentElement.classList.contains("light-mode");
}

function updateIcon() {
  btn.textContent = isLight() ? "☀" : "☾";
  btn.setAttribute("aria-label", isLight() ? "Switch to dark mode" : "Switch to light mode");
}

btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle("light-mode");
  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, isLight() ? "light" : "dark");
  updateIcon();
});

// Sync icon to whatever state was applied by the inline head script
updateIcon();
```
> ✓ File created successfully at: C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\theme.js

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:37:36 PM)*

Done. `theme.js` is a plain module — no Firebase imports needed. It applies the toggle to the `<html>` element so the class is available to all CSS selectors.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:38:25 PM)*

One bug — `btn` is not null-checked. If any page loads `theme.js` but is missing the `id="theme-toggle"` button (e.g. a page where the button wasn't added yet, or `index.html` which has no nav), the script will throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null` and crash — which would also break any other scripts on that page since module errors are silent but stop execution.

Fixing it now:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:38:28 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/theme.js`**

*Remove:*
```
const btn = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");

function isLight() {
  return document.documentElement.classList.contains("light-mode");
}

function updateIcon() {
  btn.textContent = isLight() ? "☀" : "☾";
  btn.setAttribute("aria-label", isLight() ? "Switch to dark mode" : "Switch to light mode");
}

btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle("light-mode");
  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, isLight() ? "light" : "dark");
  updateIcon();
});

// Sync icon to whatever state was applied by the inline head script
updateIcon();
```
*Insert:*
```
const btn = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");
if (!btn) throw new Error("theme.js: no #theme-toggle button found on this page");

function isLight() {
  return document.documentElement.classList.contains("light-mode");
}

function updateIcon() {
  btn.textContent = isLight() ? "☀" : "☾";
  btn.setAttribute("aria-label", isLight() ? "Switch to dark mode" : "Switch to light mode");
}

btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle("light-mode");
  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, isLight() ? "light" : "dark");
  updateIcon();
});

// Sync icon to whatever state was applied by the inline head script
updateIcon();
```
> ✓ The file C:\Users\brock\OneDrive\Desktop\dig4503\midterm\js\theme.js has been updated successfully.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:38:34 PM)*

The null check throws a descriptive error instead of letting it silently cascade into broken behavior. This makes it easy to catch during development if any page is missing the button.

Everything else is clean:
- `documentElement.classList` correctly targets `<html>` (consistent with what the inline head script will use)
- `localStorage` read is intentionally not in this file — that belongs in the inline `<head>` script to prevent flash
- `updateIcon()` on init correctly syncs the icon to whatever the head script already applied

Ready for Step 2 — the CSS light mode block?

```
/* ── Empty state ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

.empty-state {
  text-align: center;
  color: #5eead4;
  padding: 2rem 0;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}

/* ── Light mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

html.light-mode body {
  background:
    radial-gradient(ellipse at 75% 5%,  rgba(129, 140, 248, 0.06) 0%, transparent 45%),
    radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 85%, rgba(61,  214, 200, 0.05) 0%, transparent 45%),
    linear-gradient(160deg, #f0faf9 0%, #eaf5f3 100%);
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .top-nav {
  background: linear-gradient(
    100deg,
    rgba(242, 238, 255, 0.97) 0%,
    rgba(246, 251, 255, 0.97) 50%,
    rgba(236, 252, 250, 0.97) 100%
  );
  border-bottom-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
  box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.08), 0 1px 0 rgba(129, 140, 248, 0.08);
}

html.light-mode .nav-links a {
  color: #1a6e62;
  border-color: transparent;
}

html.light-mode .nav-links a:hover {
  color: #0c4a40;
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
}

html.light-mode .theme-toggle {
  color: #1a6e62;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.3);
}

html.light-mode .theme-toggle:hover {
  color: #0c4a40;
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
}

html.light-mode .card {
  background: #ffffff;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 40px rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.08), 0 0 0 1px rgba(129, 140, 248, 0.04);
}

html.light-mode .card::before {
  opacity: 0.6;
}

html.light-mode label {
  color: #0f5c50;
}

html.light-mode input,
html.light-mode textarea {
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.03);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.3);
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode input:focus,
html.light-mode textarea:focus {
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

html.light-mode .type-btn {
  background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
  color: #1a6e62;
}

html.light-mode .type-btn.active {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.14);
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0c4a40;
}

html.light-mode .review-card {
  background: #f5fffe;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.18);
  border-left-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.45);
}

html.light-mode .review-card:hover {
  background: #edfcfa;
  border-left-color: #3dd6c8;
  box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.12);
}

html.light-mode .review-title {
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .review-artist {

}

html.light-mode .review-text {

}

}

html.light-mode .review-author {
  color: #0d7a6a;
}

}

}

  border-top-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
}

html.light-mode .user-card {
  background: #f5fffe;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
}

html.light-mode .user-card:hover {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.08);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.38);
}

html.light-mode .user-name {
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .list-card {
  background: #f5fffe;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
}

html.light-mode a.list-card:hover {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.08);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.38);
  box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
}

html.light-mode .list-name-link {
  color: #0d7a6a;
}

html.light-mode .list-desc,
html.light-mode .list-count {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .list-detail-desc,
html.light-mode .profile-subtext {
  color: #2d8a7a;
}

html.light-mode .back-link {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .back-link:hover {
  color: #0c4a40;
}

html.light-mode .optional-label {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .modal {
  background: #ffffff;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
}

html.light-mode .modal-title {
  color: #0d7a6a;
}

html.light-mode .modal-close {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .modal-close:hover {
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .modal-list-btn {
  background: #f5fffe;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .modal-list-btn:hover:not(:disabled) {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.09);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.38);
}

html.light-mode .modal-new-btn {
  color: #3d8a7c;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
}

html.light-mode .modal-new-btn:hover {
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0c4a40;
}

html.light-mode .modal-loading,
html.light-mode .modal-empty {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .follow-btn {
  color: #0d7a6a;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.4);
}

html.light-mode .follow-btn:hover:not(:disabled) {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
  border-color: #3dd6c8;
}

html.light-mode .follow-btn.following {
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.12);
}

html.light-mode .stats-row .stat-value {
  color: #0d7a6a;
}

html.light-mode .stat-label {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .info-label {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .info-value {
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .edit-section {
  border-top-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.2);
}

html.light-mode .edit-section h2 {
  color: #0f5c50;
}

html.light-mode .status-msg.success,
html.light-mode .status.success { color: #166534; }

html.light-mode .status-msg.error,
html.light-mode .status.error   { color: #991b1b; }

/* Home page — light mode */

html.light-mode body.home-page {
  background:
    radial-gradient(ellipse at 75% 5%,  rgba(129, 140, 248, 0.06) 0%, transparent 45%),
    radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 85%, rgba(61,  214, 200, 0.05) 0%, transparent 45%),

}

html.light-mode .hero-banner {
  border-bottom-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.15);
}

html.light-mode .hero-eyebrow {
  color: #0f5c50;
}

html.light-mode .hero-subtitle {
  color: #2d8a7a;
}

html.light-mode .strip-stat {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.22);
}

html.light-mode .strip-stat:hover {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.97);
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.38);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 28px rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.12);
}

html.light-mode .strip-stat .stat-value {
  color: #0d7a6a;
}

html.light-mode .strip-stat .stat-label {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .feed-more-link {
  color: #3d8a7c;
}

html.light-mode .feed-more-link:hover {
  color: #0c4a40;
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:41:40 PM)*

Going through the full light-mode block systematically against the component list.

**Issues found:**

1. **Nav logo contrast** — The logo gradient goes `#3dd6c8 → #a5f3fc → #c4b5fd`. On a near-white nav background `#a5f3fc` (light sky blue) has very poor contrast. Needs a darker gradient in light mode.

2. **Placeholder text** — `input` and `textarea` have no `::placeholder` override. Browser default placeholder on a near-white input will likely be too light to read.

3. **`.delete-btn` color** — `#f87171` is a soft red designed for dark backgrounds. On a light card (`#f5fffe`) its contrast ratio drops below readable. Needs a darker red.

4. **`.write-review-btn` / `.add-to-list-btn`** — No light mode override. Their `rgba(61,214,200,0.3)` border and `#3dd6c8` text on a white card are fine visually but could benefit from a slightly stronger border to stay readable.

Fixing all four now:

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:41:43 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css`**

*Remove:*
```
html.light-mode .nav-links a {
  color: #1a6e62;
  border-color: transparent;
}
```
*Insert:*
```
html.light-mode .nav-logo {
  background: linear-gradient(120deg, #0d9488 0%, #0e7a6e 55%, #6d28d9 100%);
  -webkit-background-clip: text;
  -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
  background-clip: text;
}

html.light-mode .nav-links a {
  color: #1a6e62;
  border-color: transparent;
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:42:06 PM)*

Four edge cases fixed:

| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| Nav logo contrast | Replaced light-sky gradient with darker `#0d9488 → #0e7a6e → #6d28d9` |
| Placeholder text | Added `::placeholder { color: #7ab8b0 }` to both stylesheets |
| Delete button | `#f87171 → #b91c1c` (darker red, readable on white) |
| Action micro-buttons | Added stronger border + darker teal text for light backgrounds |

```
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .logo {
  color: #0d7a6a;
  text-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(13, 122, 106, 0.2);
}

html.light-mode .tagline {
  color: #0f5c50;
}

html.light-mode .description {
  color: #2d6e62;
}

html.light-mode .cta-btn {
  background: #3dd6c8;
  color: #0c1a18;
}

html.light-mode .theme-toggle-fixed {
  color: #1a6e62;
  border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.3);
}

html.light-mode .theme-toggle-fixed:hover {
  color: #0c4a40;
  background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
}

@media (max-width: 480px) {
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:49:24 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/theme.js`**
```js
1→// theme.js — light/dark mode toggle
     2→// Applied to <html> so the inline head script can set it before first paint.
     3→
     4→const STORAGE_KEY = "tune-in-theme";
     5→
     6→const btn = document.getElementById("theme-toggle");
     7→if (!btn) throw new Error("theme.js: no #theme-toggle button found on this page");
     8→
     9→function isLight() {
    10→  return document.documentElement.classList.contains("light-mode");
    11→}
    12→
    13→function updateIcon() {
    14→  btn.textContent = isLight() ? "☀" : "☾";
    15→  btn.setAttribute("aria-label", isLight() ? "Switch to dark mode" : "Switch to light mode");
    16→}
    17→
    18→btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    19→  document.documentElement.classList.toggle("light-mode");
    20→  localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, isLight() ? "light" : "dark");
    21→  updateIcon();
    22→});
    23→
    24→// Sync icon to whatever state was applied by the inline head script
    25→updateIcon();
    26→
```

```js
1→import {
     2→  createUserWithEmailAndPassword,
     3→  signInWithEmailAndPassword,
     4→  signOut,
     5→  onAuthStateChanged,
     6→  updateProfile
     7→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     8→import { setDoc, doc, serverTimestamp } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     9→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
    10→
    11→// --- Tab switching ---
    12→const loginTab = document.getElementById("login-tab");
    13→const signupTab = document.getElementById("signup-tab");
    14→const loginForm = document.getElementById("login-form");
    15→const signupForm = document.getElementById("signup-form");
    16→
    17→loginTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
    18→  loginTab.classList.add("active");
    19→  signupTab.classList.remove("active");
    20→  loginForm.classList.remove("hidden");
    21→  signupForm.classList.add("hidden");
    22→  // Disable hidden form inputs so browser validation ignores them
    23→  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = true);
    24→  loginForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
    25→  clearError();
    26→});
    27→
    28→signupTab.addEventListener("click", () => {
    29→  signupTab.classList.add("active");
    30→  loginTab.classList.remove("active");
    31→  signupForm.classList.remove("hidden");
    32→  loginForm.classList.add("hidden");
    33→  loginForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = true);
    34→  signupForm.querySelectorAll("input").forEach(i => i.disabled = false);
    35→  clearError();
    36→});
    37→
    38→// Flag to prevent onAuthStateChanged from racing with form submission redirects
    39→let isSubmitting = false;
    40→
    41→// --- Sign Up ---
    42→signupForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    43→  e.preventDefault();
    44→  isSubmitting = true;
    45→
    46→  const submitBtn = signupForm.querySelector(".submit-btn");
    47→  submitBtn.disabled = true;
    48→  submitBtn.textContent = "Creating account...";
    49→
    50→  const displayName = document.getElementById("signup-name").value.trim();
    51→  const email = document.getElementById("signup-email").value.trim();
    52→  const password = document.getElementById("signup-password").value;
    53→
    54→  let user = null;
    55→  try {
    56→    ({ user } = await createUserWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password));
    57→    await updateProfile(user, { displayName });
    58→    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", user.uid), {
    59→      uid: user.uid,
    60→      displayName,
    61→      displayNameLower: displayName.toLowerCase(),
    62→      email,
    63→      createdAt: serverTimestamp()
    64→    });
    65→    window.location.href = "home.html";
    66→  } catch (err) {
    67→    // If Auth user was created but a later step failed, delete the partial account
    68→    if (user) await user.delete().catch(() => {});
    69→    isSubmitting = false;
    70→    submitBtn.disabled = false;
    71→    submitBtn.textContent = "Create Account";
    72→    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
    73→  }
    74→});
    75→
    76→// --- Log In ---
    77→loginForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    78→  e.preventDefault();
    79→  isSubmitting = true;
    80→  const email = document.getElementById("login-email").value.trim();
    81→  const password = document.getElementById("login-password").value;
    82→
    83→  try {
    84→    await signInWithEmailAndPassword(auth, email, password);
    85→    window.location.href = "home.html";
    86→  } catch (err) {
    87→    isSubmitting = false;
    88→    showError(friendlyError(err.code));
    89→  }
    90→});
    91→
    92→// --- Redirect if already logged in ---
    93→// Only fires when the page loads with an existing session, not during form submissions
    94→onAuthStateChanged(auth, (user) => {
    95→  if (user && !isSubmitting) window.location.href = "home.html";
    96→});
    97→
    98→// --- Helpers ---
    99→function showError(message) {
   100→  const el = document.getElementById("auth-error");
… (truncated — 123 lines total)
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:49:35 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/home.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import { collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     3→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     4→
     5→const homeNameEl   = document.getElementById("home-name");
     6→const feedPreview  = document.getElementById("feed-preview");
     7→
     8→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
     9→  unsubscribe();
    10→
    11→  if (!user) {
    12→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    13→    return;
    14→  }
    15→
    16→  homeNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "there";
    17→
    18→  await Promise.all([
    19→    loadStats(user.uid),
    20→    loadFeedPreview(user.uid)
    21→  ]);
    22→});
    23→
    24→async function loadStats(uid) {
    25→  try {
    26→    const [followersSnap, followingSnap, reviewsSnap, queueSnap] = await Promise.all([
    27→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"),  where("followingId", "==", uid))),
    28→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"),  where("followerId",  "==", uid))),
    29→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "reviews"),  where("userId",      "==", uid))),
    30→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "queue"),    where("userId",      "==", uid)))
    31→    ]);
    32→    document.getElementById("stat-followers").textContent = followersSnap.size;
    33→    document.getElementById("stat-following").textContent = followingSnap.size;
    34→    document.getElementById("stat-reviews").textContent   = reviewsSnap.size;
    35→    document.getElementById("stat-queue").textContent     = queueSnap.size;
    36→  } catch (err) {
    37→    // Leave dashes — non-critical
    38→  }
    39→}
    40→
    41→async function loadFeedPreview(uid) {
    42→  try {
    43→    const followSnap = await getDocs(
    44→      query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))
    45→    );
    46→
    47→    if (followSnap.empty) {
    48→      feedPreview.innerHTML = `
    49→        <p class="empty-state">
    50→          You're not following anyone yet.<br>
    51→          <a href="search.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Find friends to follow →</a>
    52→        </p>`;
    53→      return;
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    const followingIds = followSnap.docs.map(d => d.data().followingId);
    57→
    58→    // Chunk into groups of 10 (Firestore "in" limit)
    59→    const chunks = [];
    60→    for (let i = 0; i < followingIds.length; i += 10) {
    61→      chunks.push(followingIds.slice(i, i + 10));
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    const snapshots = await Promise.all(
    65→      chunks.map(chunk =>
    66→        getDocs(query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "in", chunk)))
    67→      )
    68→    );
    69→
    70→    const all = [];
    71→    snapshots.forEach(snap => snap.forEach(d => all.push({ id: d.id, ...d.data() })));
    72→
    73→    if (all.length === 0) {
    74→      feedPreview.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">No reviews from people you follow yet.</p>`;
    75→      return;
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    all.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
    79→    const recent = all.slice(0, 5);
    80→
    81→    // Fetch display names for authors in parallel
    82→    const uniqueUids = [...new Set(recent.map(r => r.userId))];
    83→    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uniqueUids.map(id => getDoc(doc(db, "users", id))));
    84→    const nameMap = {};
    85→    userSnaps.forEach(snap => {
    86→      if (snap.exists()) nameMap[snap.id] = snap.data().displayName || "Unknown";
    87→    });
    88→
    89→    feedPreview.innerHTML = recent.map(review => {
    90→      const rating  = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    91→      const author  = nameMap[review.userId] || "Unknown";
    92→      return `
    93→        <div class="review-card">
    94→          <div class="review-header">
    95→            <div>
    96→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    97→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    98→            </div>
    99→            <div class="review-meta">
   100→              <span class="type-badge type-${review.type}">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
… (truncated — 134 lines total)
```
