```js
1→import {
     2→  onAuthStateChanged,
     3→  signOut,
     4→  updateProfile
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     6→import { setDoc, doc, collection, query, where, getDocs } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     7→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     8→
     9→const displayNameEl = document.getElementById("display-name");
    10→const emailEl = document.getElementById("user-email");
    11→const editForm = document.getElementById("edit-form");
    12→const editNameInput = document.getElementById("edit-name");
    13→const logoutBtn = document.getElementById("logout-btn");
    14→const statusMsg = document.getElementById("status-msg");
    15→
    16→// --- Guard: redirect to login if not authenticated ---
    17→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    18→  unsubscribe();
    19→  if (!user) {
    20→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    21→    return;
    22→  }
    23→  displayNameEl.textContent = user.displayName || "No name set";
    24→  emailEl.textContent = user.email;
    25→  editNameInput.value = user.displayName || "";
    26→  await loadStats(user.uid);
    27→});
    28→
    29→// --- Stats: followers, following, queue count ---
    30→async function loadStats(uid) {
    31→  try {
    32→    const [followersSnap, followingSnap, queueSnap] = await Promise.all([
    33→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followingId", "==", uid))),
    34→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "follows"), where("followerId", "==", uid))),
    35→      getDocs(query(collection(db, "queue"), where("userId", "==", uid)))
    36→    ]);
    37→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = followersSnap.size;
    38→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = followingSnap.size;
    39→    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = queueSnap.size;
    40→  } catch (err) {
    41→    document.getElementById("followers-count").textContent = "—";
    42→    document.getElementById("following-count").textContent = "—";
    43→    document.getElementById("queue-count").textContent = "—";
    44→  }
    45→}
    46→
    47→// --- Update display name ---
    48→editForm.addEventListener("submit", async (e) => {
    49→  e.preventDefault();
    50→  const newName = editNameInput.value.trim();
    51→  if (!newName) return;
    52→
    53→  // Guard: auth.currentUser can be null if Firebase hasn't resolved the session yet
    54→  if (!auth.currentUser) {
    55→    showStatus("Session not ready. Please try again.", true);
    56→    return;
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  const saveBtn = editForm.querySelector(".save-btn");
    60→  saveBtn.disabled = true;
    61→  saveBtn.textContent = "Saving...";
    62→
    63→  try {
    64→    await updateProfile(auth.currentUser, { displayName: newName });
    65→    // setDoc with merge:true acts as upsert — safe for accounts created before Firestore docs were added
    66→    await setDoc(doc(db, "users", auth.currentUser.uid), { displayName: newName, displayNameLower: newName.toLowerCase() }, { merge: true });
    67→    displayNameEl.textContent = newName;
    68→    showStatus("Name updated successfully.");
    69→  } catch (err) {
    70→    showStatus("Failed to update name. Please try again.", true);
    71→  } finally {
    72→    saveBtn.disabled = false;
    73→    saveBtn.textContent = "Save";
    74→  }
    75→});
    76→
    77→// --- Log out ---
    78→logoutBtn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
    79→  await signOut(auth);
    80→  window.location.href = "index.html";
    81→});
    82→
    83→function showStatus(message, isError = false) {
    84→  statusMsg.textContent = message;
    85→  statusMsg.className = isError ? "status error" : "status success";
    86→  setTimeout(() => { statusMsg.textContent = ""; statusMsg.className = "status"; }, 3000);
    87→}
    88→
```

```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs, doc, getDoc

     5→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     6→
     7→const usersList = document.getElementById("users-list");
     8→const tabFollowers = document.getElementById("tab-followers");
     9→const tabFollowing = document.getElementById("tab-following");
    10→
    11→let currentUid = null;
    12→// Cache both tabs after first load to avoid re-fetching on tab switch
    13→const cache = { followers: null, following: null };
    14→// Discard stale async responses when tabs are switched rapidly
    15→let requestCount = 0;
    16→
    17→const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    18→const tabParam = params.get("tab") || sessionStorage.getItem("followsTab");
    19→sessionStorage.removeItem("followsTab");
    20→let activeTab = tabParam === "following" ? "following" : "followers";
    21→
    22→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    23→  unsubscribe();
    24→  if (!user) {
    25→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    26→    return;
    27→  }
    28→  currentUid = user.uid;
    29→  setActiveTab(activeTab);
    30→  await loadTab(activeTab);
    31→});
    32→
    33→// --- Tab toggle ---
    34→tabFollowers.addEventListener("click", () => {
    35→  if (!currentUid || activeTab === "followers") return;
    36→  activeTab = "followers";
    37→  setActiveTab("followers");
    38→  loadTab("followers");
    39→});
    40→
    41→tabFollowing.addEventListener("click", () => {
    42→  if (!currentUid || activeTab === "following") return;
    43→  activeTab = "following";
    44→  setActiveTab("following");
    45→  loadTab("following");
    46→});
    47→
    48→function setActiveTab(tab) {
    49→  tabFollowers.classList.toggle("active", tab === "followers");
    50→  tabFollowing.classList.toggle("active", tab === "following");
    51→  document.title = tab === "followers" ? "Tune-In — Followers" : "Tune-In — Following";
    52→}
    53→
    54→// --- Load a tab ---
    55→async function loadTab(tab) {
    56→  if (cache[tab]) {
    57→    renderUsers(cache[tab], tab);
    58→    return;
    59→  }
    60→
    61→  const thisRequest = ++requestCount;
    62→  usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state">Loading...</p>`;
    63→
    64→  try {
    65→    const isFollowers = tab === "followers";
    66→    const field = isFollowers ? "followingId" : "followerId";
    67→    const uidField = isFollowers ? "followerId" : "followingId";
    68→
    69→    const snap = await getDocs(
    70→      query(collection(db, "follows"), where(field, "==", currentUid))
    71→    );
    72→
    73→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    74→
    75→    if (snap.empty) {
    76→      cache[tab] = [];
    77→      renderUsers([], tab);
    78→      return;
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    // Fetch user documents for all follows in parallel
    82→    const uids = snap.docs.map(d => d.data()[uidField]);
    83→    const userSnaps = await Promise.all(uids.map(uid => getDoc(doc(db, "users", uid))));
    84→
    85→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    86→
    87→    const users = userSnaps
    88→      .filter(s => s.exists())
    89→      .map(s => ({ uid: s.id, displayName: s.data().displayName || "Unknown User" }))
    90→      .sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName));
    91→
    92→    cache[tab] = users;
    93→    renderUsers(users, tab);
    94→  } catch (err) {
    95→    if (thisRequest !== requestCount) return;
    96→    usersList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load. Please try again.</p>`;
    97→  }
    98→}
    99→
   100→// --- Render ---
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:50:00 PM)*

Here's a thorough review of edge cases found across the codebase:

---

**No critical bugs.** The code is solid. Here are the minor edge cases worth knowing about:

### 1. `type-badge` with unsanitized CSS class (`type-${review.type}`)
**Files:** `home.js:100`, `public-profile.js:157`, and likely `reviews.js`, `feed.js`
The `type-${review.type}` class is inserted directly from Firestore data. If a review was somehow stored with a malformed `type` (e.g. containing a space or special character), it would create an invalid class. The existing `escapeHtml` prevents XSS in the text content, but doesn't sanitize the class attribute. Since `type` is only ever set from a controlled `<select>` in `review-new.js`, this is low risk in practice.

### 2. `theme.js` throws on pages without `#theme-toggle`
**File:** `js/theme.js:7`
The `throw new Error(...)` guard was added intentionally, but a thrown error from a module script is silent in most browsers (it appears in the console but doesn't break the page). However, if any page ever loads `theme.js` without the button, the toggle simply won't work. This is safe given every page now has the button.

### 3. `auth.js` — `isSubmitting` flag never resets on successful login redirect race
**File:** `js/auth.js:79`
In the login flow, `isSubmitting = true` is set before `signInWithEmailAndPassword`. If the redirect (`window.location.href = "home.html"`) fires but the page doesn't immediately unload (rare but possible in some browsers), `onAuthStateChanged` could fire first and also redirect. The flag handles this correctly. No bug, but worth noting.

### 4. Light mode — `logout-btn` has no light-mode color override
**File:** `css/reviews.css` (light mode section)
`.logout-btn` uses `color: #f87171` (light red) on a white card background in light mode — the contrast is actually fine, but there's no explicit light-mode override for it. The red danger color reads well, so this is cosmetic rather than a bug.

### 5. `escapeHtml` doesn't escape single quotes
**File:** multiple JS files
The `escapeHtml` function escapes `&`, `<`, `>`, and `"`, but not `'`. This is fine for HTML attribute values that use double-quote delimiters (which is the case everywhere in this codebase), but is worth noting for completeness.

### 6. `home.js` — feed preview inline `onclick` uses unescaped UID
**File:** `js/home.js:109`
```js
onclick="sessionStorage.setItem('viewProfileUid','${review.userId}')"
```
Firebase UIDs are alphanumeric and safe, so this won't cause issues. But if the `userId` field were ever tampered with in Firestore, it could break the `onclick` attribute. Low risk given Firebase generates UIDs.

---

**Summary:** Nothing that needs fixing now. The most structurally noteworthy is #1 (unsanitized CSS class from Firestore data), but since `type` only comes from a controlled form `<select>`, it's not exploitable in normal use.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:52:39 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/home.js`**

*Remove:*
```
function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}
```
*Insert:*
```
function escapeHtml(str = "") {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
    .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
    .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
    .replace(/"/g, "&quot;");
}

const VALID_TYPES = new Set(["album", "song"]);
function safeType(type) {
  return VALID_TYPES.has(type) ? type : "";
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:53:46 PM)*

All 7 occurrences are fixed. Here's what changed:

- Added `safeType()` to **6 files** (`home.js`, `reviews.js`, `feed.js`, `queue.js`, `list-detail.js`, `public-profile.js`)
- `safeType` uses a `Set` whitelist — only `"album"` or `"song"` pass through; anything else returns `""`, so the class becomes `type-badge type-` (harmless, just no color variant applied)
- The visible badge text still uses `escapeHtml(review.type)` unchanged, so a malformed value would display as readable text but not inject a class

**User** *(3/13/2026, 7:58:43 PM)*

That's perfect, no need to review. For the final feature, I want to implement the ability for the user to search up a song or album they may have reviewed in their review tab. Let's break it up iteratively. How would you approach this?

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 7:58:54 PM)*

Here's the approach broken into two steps:

**Step 1 — HTML**: Add a search input above the reviews list in `reviews.html`. Simple text field, no new dependencies.

**Step 2 — JS**: In `reviews.js`, filter the already-loaded reviews array in memory as the user types. Since all reviews are fetched once on page load, this requires no additional Firestore reads — just re-render the subset that matches the query against `title` and `artist`.

The key design decision is filtering client-side against the in-memory array rather than querying Firestore on each keystroke. This keeps it fast and avoids extra reads. The search would match on both title and artist so a user can find a review by either field.

Ready to start with Step 1?

---

**User** *(3/13/2026, 7:59:43 PM)*

Yes, this plan looks sound

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:02:31 PM)*

**📖 Read `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/reviews.js`**
```js
1→import { onAuthStateChanged } from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-auth.js";
     2→import {
     3→  collection, query, where, getDocs,
     4→  deleteDoc, doc, getDoc, setDoc, addDoc, serverTimestamp
     5→} from "https://www.gstatic.com/firebasejs/10.14.1/firebase-firestore.js";
     6→import { auth, db } from "./firebase-config.js";
     7→
     8→const reviewsList = document.getElementById("reviews-list");
     9→const listModal = document.getElementById("list-modal");
    10→const modalLists = document.getElementById("modal-lists");
    11→const modalClose = document.getElementById("modal-close");
    12→
    13→let currentUserId = null;
    14→// Map of reviewId → review data, used when adding to a list
    15→const reviewsMap = {};
    16→// Tracks which review is being added to a list
    17→let activeReviewId = null;
    18→// Cached list of user's lists — invalidated when a list is created/deleted elsewhere
    19→let cachedLists = null;
    20→
    21→// --- Auth guard ---
    22→const unsubscribe = onAuthStateChanged(auth, async (user) => {
    23→  unsubscribe();
    24→  if (!user) {
    25→    window.location.href = "login.html";
    26→    return;
    27→  }
    28→  currentUserId = user.uid;
    29→  await loadReviews(user.uid);
    30→});
    31→
    32→async function loadReviews(userId) {
    33→  try {
    34→    const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), where("userId", "==", userId));
    35→    const snapshot = await getDocs(q);
    36→
    37→    if (snapshot.empty) {
    38→      reviewsList.innerHTML = `
    39→        <p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>
    40→      `;
    41→      return;
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    // Sort client-side by createdAt descending
    45→    const reviews = [];
    46→    snapshot.forEach(docSnap => reviews.push({ id: docSnap.id, ...docSnap.data() }));
    47→    reviews.sort((a, b) => (b.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0) - (a.createdAt?.seconds ?? 0));
    48→
    49→    // Cache reviews for use in the Add to List modal
    50→    reviews.forEach(r => { reviewsMap[r.id] = r; });
    51→
    52→    reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    53→      const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    54→      return `
    55→        <div class="review-card" data-id="${review.id}">
    56→          <div class="review-header">
    57→            <div>
    58→              <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
    59→              <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
    60→            </div>
    61→            <div class="review-meta">
    62→              <span class="type-badge type-${safeType(review.type)}">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
    63→              <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
    64→            </div>
    65→          </div>
    66→          ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
    67→          <div class="review-footer">
    68→            <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
    69→            <div class="review-actions">
    70→              <button class="add-to-list-btn" data-id="${review.id}">+ List</button>
    71→              <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${review.id}">Delete</button>
    72→            </div>
    73→          </div>
    74→        </div>
    75→      `;
    76→    }).join("");
    77→
    78→  } catch (err) {
    79→    reviewsList.innerHTML = `<p class="empty-state" style="color:#f87171;">Failed to load reviews. Please try again.</p>`;
    80→  }
    81→}
    82→
```

```
function renderReviews(reviews, query = "") {
  if (reviews.length === 0) {
    reviewsList.innerHTML = query
      ? `<p class="empty-state">No reviews match "${escapeHtml(query)}".</p>`
      : `<p class="empty-state">No reviews yet. <a href="review-new.html" style="color:#3dd6c8;">Write your first one.</a></p>`;
    return;
  }

  reviewsList.innerHTML = reviews.map(review => {
    const rating = Math.min(5, Math.max(0, parseInt(review.rating) || 0));
    return `
      <div class="review-card" data-id="${review.id}">
        <div class="review-header">
          <div>
            <div class="review-title">${escapeHtml(review.title)}</div>
            <div class="review-artist">${escapeHtml(review.artist)}</div>
          </div>
          <div class="review-meta">
            <span class="type-badge type-${safeType(review.type)}">${escapeHtml(review.type)}</span>
            <span class="review-stars">${"★".repeat(rating)}${"☆".repeat(5 - rating)}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        ${review.reviewText ? `<p class="review-text">${escapeHtml(review.reviewText)}</p>` : ""}
        <div class="review-footer">
          <p class="review-date">${formatDate(review.createdAt)}</p>
          <div class="review-actions">
            <button class="add-to-list-btn" data-id="${review.id}">+ List</button>
            <button class="delete-btn" data-id="${review.id}">Delete</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    `;
  }).join("");
}

async function loadReviews(userId) {
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:04:37 PM)*

One issue found. `renderReviews` and `escapeHtml` are called before they're declared — `renderReviews` is used in the `searchInput` listener on line 32 and `escapeHtml` is used inside `renderReviews` on line 49, but both functions are defined further down the file. This works fine for regular `function` declarations (which are hoisted), so there's no actual runtime error here.

One real issue though: **line 83** — the local variable `q` in `loadReviews` shadows the Firestore `query` import:

```js
const q = query(collection(db, "reviews"), ...);  // 'query' here is the Firestore function
```

This worked before our changes, but now the `searchInput` listener on line 25 also uses `q` as a local variable name — that's fine since it's scoped. The `loadReviews` variable name collision with `query` (the import) has always been there and works because `q` is just storing the result, not overwriting `query`. No actual bug.

The code is clean. No errors need to be addressed — the logic is correct, `handleDelete` properly re-filters after removal, and the empty-state messaging correctly distinguishes between "no reviews at all" vs. "no search matches".

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:05:43 PM)*

Here's a testing plan for the review search feature:

---

### Test Plan: Review Search Feature

**Prerequisites:** Logged in as a user with at least a few reviews across different titles, artists, and types (album/song).

---

#### 1. Basic Search
- [ ] Type a title that matches one review — only that card appears
- [ ] Type an artist name that matches multiple reviews — all matching cards appear
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive (e.g. "tame" matches "Tame Impala")
- [ ] Clearing the input restores all reviews

#### 2. Partial Matching
- [ ] Partial title match works (e.g. "curr" matches "Curr Hits")
- [ ] Partial artist match works (e.g. "bey" matches "Beyoncé")

#### 3. No Results State
- [ ] Searching a term with no matches shows: *No reviews match "[query]".*
- [ ] The query text in the message is correctly escaped (no raw HTML injected)

#### 4. Delete While Filtered
- [ ] With a search active, delete a visible review — it is removed and the filtered view remains (other matches stay)
- [ ] Delete the last matching review while filtered — shows the "No reviews match" empty state, not the "No reviews yet" state
- [ ] Clear the search after deleting — the deleted review does not reappear

#### 5. Empty Account
- [ ] A user with no reviews sees the "No reviews yet. Write your first one." state and the search input is present but searching has no effect

#### 6. Edge Cases
- [ ] Search input with only spaces returns the full list (not the empty-match state)
- [ ] Typing and clearing rapidly does not leave a stale render

---

All cases can be verified manually in the browser. No new Firestore reads should occur during any search interaction — confirm via the Network tab that no requests are made while typing.

The existing `transcript.md` covers the first session (`e29a692e...`). The current session is `7430745c...`. I'll process the new JSONL and append it to the existing transcript.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:11:28 PM)*

**💻 Bash**
```bash
cd "C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm" && node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const INPUT = path.join(process.env.USERPROFILE, '.claude', 'projects', 'C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm', '7430745c-5096-49f5-84c7-a0ad0b6b3b58.jsonl');
const OUTPUT = path.join(__dirname, 'transcript.md');

const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, 'utf8').trim().split('\n');

const toolResults = {};
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type !== 'user' || e.message?.role !== 'user') continue;
  const content = e.message.content;
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
  for (const block of content) {
    if (block.type !== 'tool_result' || !block.tool_use_id) continue;
    const text = Array.isArray(block.content)
      ? block.content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n')
      : typeof block.content === 'string' ? block.content : '';
    const cleaned = text.replace(/<system-reminder>[\s\S]*?<\/system-reminder>/g, '').trim();
    if (cleaned) toolResults[block.tool_use_id] = cleaned;
  }
}

const turns = [];
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }

  if (e.type === 'user' && e.message?.role === 'user') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (Array.isArray(content) && content.some(b => b.type === 'tool_result')) continue;
    const text = typeof content === 'string' ? content
      : Array.isArray(content) ? content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n') : null;
    if (!text) continue;
    const trimmed = text.trim();
    if (trimmed.startsWith('This session is being continued from a previous conversation')) continue;
    turns.push({ role: 'User', text: trimmed, ts: e.timestamp });
  }

  if (e.type === 'assistant' && e.message?.role === 'assistant') {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const parts = [];
    const textContent = content.filter(b => b.type === 'text').map(b => b.text).join('\n').trim();
    if (textContent) parts.push(textContent);
    for (const block of content.filter(b => b.type === 'tool_use')) {
      const result = toolResults[block.id] || null;
      parts.push(formatToolCall(block, result));
    }
    if (parts.length > 0) turns.push({ role: 'Claude', text: parts.join('\n\n'), ts: e.timestamp });
  }
}

function formatToolCall(block, result) {
  const name = block.name;
  const input = block.input || {};
  let out = '';
  switch (name) {
    case 'Write': {
      const fp = (input.file_path || '').replace(/\\\\/g, '/');
      const ext = fp.split('.').pop() || '';
      out = \`**✏️ Write \\\`\${fp}\\\`**\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\${ext}\n\${(input.content || '').trimEnd()}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      if (result) out += \`\n> ✓ \${result.split('\n')[0]}\`;
      break;
    }
    case 'Edit': {
      const fp = (input.file_path || '').replace(/\\\\/g, '/');
      out = \`**✏️ Edit \\\`\${fp}\\\`**\`;
      if (input.old_string !== undefined) {
        out += \`\n\n*Remove:*\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\n\${input.old_string}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
        out += \`\n*Insert:*\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\n\${input.new_string || ''}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      }
      if (result) out += \`\n> ✓ \${result.split('\n')[0]}\`;
      break;
    }
    case 'Bash': {
      const cmd = (input.command || '').trim();
      out = \`**💻 Bash**\n\\\`\\\`\\\`bash\n\${cmd}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      if (result) {
        const capped = result.length > 3000 ? result.slice(0, 3000) + '\n… (output truncated)' : result;
        out += \`\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\n\${capped}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      }
      break;
    }
    case 'Read': {
      const fp = (input.file_path || '').replace(/\\\\/g, '/');
      const ext = fp.split('.').pop() || '';
      out = \`**📖 Read \\\`\${fp}\\\`**\`;
      if (result) {
        const rl = result.split('\n');
        const preview = rl.length > 100 ? rl.slice(0, 100).join('\n') + \`\n… (truncated — \${rl.length} lines total)\` : result;
        out += \`\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\${ext}\n\${preview}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      }
      break;
    }
    case 'Glob': {
      out = \`**🔍 Glob \\\`\${input.pattern || ''}\\\`**\${input.path ? \` in \\\`\${input.path}\\\`\` : ''}\`;
      if (result) { const c = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + '\n…' : result; out += \`\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\n\${c}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`; }
      break;
    }
    case 'Grep': {
      out = \`**🔍 Grep \\\`\${input.pattern || ''}\\\`**\${input.path ? \` in \\\`\${input.path}\\\`\` : ''}\`;
      if (result) { const c = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + '\n…' : result; out += \`\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\n\${c}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`; }
      break;
    }
    default: {
      out = \`**🔧 \${name}**\n\\\`\\\`\\\`json\n\${JSON.stringify(input, null, 2).slice(0, 800)}\n\\\`\\\`\\\`\`;
      if (result) out += \`\n> Result: \${result.slice(0, 300)}\`;
    }
  }
  return out;
}

let append = '\n\n---\n\n# Session 2 — Continuation\n\n';
for (const turn of turns) {
  const date = turn.ts ? new Date(turn.ts).toLocaleString() : '';
  append += \`---\n\n**\${turn.role}**\${date ? \` *(\${date})*\` : ''}\n\n\${turn.text}\n\n\`;
}

fs.appendFileSync(OUTPUT, append, 'utf8');
console.log('Appended ' + turns.length + ' turns to transcript.md');
"
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:13:59 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/append-session2.js`**
```js
const fs = require("fs");
const path = require("path");

const INPUT = path.join(
  process.env.USERPROFILE,
  ".claude", "projects",
  "C--Users-brock-OneDrive-Desktop-dig4503-midterm",
  "7430745c-5096-49f5-84c7-a0ad0b6b3b58.jsonl"
);
const OUTPUT = path.join(__dirname, "transcript.md");

const lines = fs.readFileSync(INPUT, "utf8").trim().split("\n");

const toolResults = {};
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }
  if (e.type !== "user" || e.message?.role !== "user") continue;
  const content = e.message.content;
  if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
  for (const block of content) {
    if (block.type !== "tool_result" || !block.tool_use_id) continue;
    const text = Array.isArray(block.content)
      ? block.content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n")
      : typeof block.content === "string" ? block.content : "";
    const cleaned = text.replace(/<system-reminder>[\s\S]*?<\/system-reminder>/g, "").trim();
    if (cleaned) toolResults[block.tool_use_id] = cleaned;
  }
}

const turns = [];
for (const line of lines) {
  let e; try { e = JSON.parse(line); } catch { continue; }

  if (e.type === "user" && e.message?.role === "user") {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (Array.isArray(content) && content.some(b => b.type === "tool_result")) continue;
    const text = typeof content === "string" ? content
      : Array.isArray(content) ? content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n") : null;
    if (!text) continue;
    const trimmed = text.trim();
    if (trimmed.startsWith("This session is being continued from a previous conversation")) continue;
    turns.push({ role: "User", text: trimmed, ts: e.timestamp });
  }

  if (e.type === "assistant" && e.message?.role === "assistant") {
    const content = e.message.content;
    if (!Array.isArray(content)) continue;
    const parts = [];
    const textContent = content.filter(b => b.type === "text").map(b => b.text).join("\n").trim();
    if (textContent) parts.push(textContent);
    for (const block of content.filter(b => b.type === "tool_use")) {
      parts.push(formatToolCall(block, toolResults[block.id] || null));
    }
    if (parts.length > 0) turns.push({ role: "Claude", text: parts.join("\n\n"), ts: e.timestamp });
  }
}

function formatToolCall(block, result) {
  const name = block.name;
  const input = block.input || {};
  let out = "";
  switch (name) {
    case "Write": {
      const fp = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      const ext = fp.split(".").pop() || "";
      out = `**Write \`${fp}\`**\n\`\`\`${ext}\n${(input.content || "").trimEnd()}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) out += `\n> ${result.split("\n")[0]}`;
      break;
    }
    case "Edit": {
      const fp = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      out = `**Edit \`${fp}\`**`;
      if (input.old_string !== undefined) {
        out += `\n\n*Remove:*\n\`\`\`\n${input.old_string}\n\`\`\``;
        out += `\n*Insert:*\n\`\`\`\n${input.new_string || ""}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      if (result) out += `\n> ${result.split("\n")[0]}`;
      break;
    }
    case "Bash": {
      const cmd = (input.command || "").trim();
      out = `**Bash**\n\`\`\`bash\n${cmd}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) {
        const capped = result.length > 3000 ? result.slice(0, 3000) + "\n... (truncated)" : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`\n${capped}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }
    case "Read": {
      const fp = (input.file_path || "").replace(/\\/g, "/");
      const ext = fp.split(".").pop() || "";
      out = `**Read \`${fp}\`**`;
      if (result) {
        const rl = result.split("\n");
        const preview = rl.length > 100 ? rl.slice(0, 100).join("\n") + `\n... (truncated, ${rl.length} lines total)` : result;
        out += `\n\`\`\`${ext}\n${preview}\n\`\`\``;
      }
      break;
    }
    case "Glob": {
      out = `**Glob \`${input.pattern || ""}\`**${input.path ? ` in \`${input.path}\`` : ""}`;
      if (result) { const c = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + "\n..." : result; out += `\n\`\`\`\n${c}\n\`\`\``; }
      break;
    }
    case "Grep": {
      out = `**Grep \`${input.pattern || ""}\`**${input.path ? ` in \`${input.path}\`` : ""}`;
      if (result) { const c = result.length > 800 ? result.slice(0, 800) + "\n..." : result; out += `\n\`\`\`\n${c}\n\`\`\``; }
      break;
    }
    default: {
      out = `**${name}**\n\`\`\`json\n${JSON.stringify(input, null, 2).slice(0, 800)}\n\`\`\``;
      if (result) out += `\n> Result: ${result.slice(0, 300)}`;
    }
  }
  return out;
}

let append = "\n\n---\n\n# Session 2 — Continuation\n\n";
for (const turn of turns) {
  const date = turn.ts ? new Date(turn.ts).toLocaleString() : "";
  append += `---\n\n**${turn.role}**${date ? ` *(${date})*` : ""}\n\n${turn.text}\n\n`;
}

fs.appendFileSync(OUTPUT, append, "utf8");
console.log(`Appended ${turns.length} turns to transcript.md`);
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:18:47 PM)*

Here's what I'm seeing and my proposed approach:

**Current gaps:**
- The navbar has 6 links + logo + theme toggle — on screens under ~480px these will overflow or wrap into multiple lines, breaking the layout entirely
- `.page-header` (title + action button side-by-side) will squeeze on small screens
- The modal, search row, and some profile layouts have no narrow-screen adjustments

**Proposed steps:**

**Step 1 — CSS**: Add mobile nav styles (hamburger button, slide-down menu overlay) and fix remaining layout breakpoints — page-header stacking, modal full-width, list/profile headers

**Step 2 — HTML**: Add a hamburger `<button class="nav-hamburger">` to all 11 nav pages (same pattern as the theme toggle was added)

**Step 3 — JS**: Create `nav.js` — a small module that toggles the mobile menu open/closed, closes it on outside click or nav link click, and add it to all pages

This mirrors how `theme.js` was implemented — a single shared module included everywhere. The end result is a clean collapsible nav on mobile with all layout issues resolved. Ready to start with Step 1?

*Insert:*
```
/* ── Responsive ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .hero-title {
    font-size: 2.75rem;
    letter-spacing: -1px;
  }

  .stats-strip-inner {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  }

  .hero-banner::after {
    display: none;
  }

  /* Stack page-header on mid-size screens */
  .page-header {
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: flex-start;
    gap: 0.75rem;
  }

  .page-header .action-btn {
    width: 100%;
    text-align: center;
  }

  /* Stack list-detail and public-profile headers */
  .list-detail-header,
  .public-profile-header {
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.75rem;
  }

  .back-link {
    order: -1;
  }
}

/* ── Mobile nav (hamburger) ──────────────────────────────────────────────── */

@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .nav-hamburger {
    display: block;
  }

  .nav-links {
    display: none;
    position: absolute;
    top: 100%;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0;
    background: linear-gradient(
      160deg,
      rgba(20, 14, 45, 0.98) 0%,
      rgba(8, 16, 36, 0.98) 100%
    );
    border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.18);
    box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
    padding: 0.5rem 0;
  }

  .nav-links.open {
    display: flex;
  }

  .nav-links a {
    padding: 0.85rem 1.5rem;
    border-radius: 0;
    border: none;
    border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.07);
    font-size: 0.9rem;
  }

  .nav-links a:last-child {
    border-bottom: none;
  }

  .nav-links a:hover {
    background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.08);
    border-color: transparent;
  }

  .top-nav {
    padding: 0 1rem;
    position: relative;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
}

html.light-mode .nav-links {
  /* inherit — no override needed at desktop */
}

@media (max-width: 640px) {
  html.light-mode .nav-links {
    background: linear-gradient(
      160deg,
      rgba(242, 238, 255, 0.99) 0%,
      rgba(236, 252, 250, 0.99) 100%
    );
    border-bottom-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.28);
  }

  html.light-mode .nav-hamburger {
    color: #1a6e62;
    border-color: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.3);
  }

  html.light-mode .nav-hamburger:hover {
    color: #0c4a40;
    background: rgba(61, 214, 200, 0.1);
  }
}

@media (max-width: 480px) {
  body {
    padding: 5rem 0.75rem 2rem;
  }

  .card {
    padding: 1.5rem 1rem;
  }

  .card h1 {
    font-size: 1.4rem;
  }

  .review-header {
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.5rem;
  }

  .review-meta {
    flex-direction: row;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.5rem;
  }

  .review-footer {
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: flex-start;
    gap: 0.5rem;
  }

  .review-actions {
    width: 100%;
    justify-content: flex-end;
  }

  .stars {
    gap: 0.25rem;
  }

  .star {
    font-size: 1.75rem;
  }

  .hero-banner {
    padding: 3rem 1.25rem 3rem;
  }

  .hero-title {
    font-size: 2.25rem;
    letter-spacing: -0.5px;
  }

  .hero-actions {
    flex-direction: column;
  }

  .hero-actions .action-btn {
    text-align: center;
  }

  .stats-strip,
  .home-feed {
    padding-left: 1rem;
    padding-right: 1rem;
  }

  .stats-strip-inner {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
    gap: 0.5rem;
  }

  .strip-stat {
    padding: 1.1rem 1rem;
  }

  .strip-stat .stat-value {
    font-size: 1.6rem;
  }

  .edit-row {
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: stretch;
  }

  .save-btn {
    width: 100%;
  }

  .stats-row {
    gap: 1rem;
  }

  .search-row {
    flex-direction: column;
  }

  .search-btn {
    width: 100%;
  }

  .modal {
    max-height: 90vh;
    padding: 1.25rem 1rem;
  }

  .type-toggle {
    flex-direction: column;
  }
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:21:56 PM)*

**✏️ Write `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/js/nav.js`**
```js
// nav.js — mobile hamburger menu toggle

const hamburger = document.getElementById("nav-hamburger");
const navLinks  = document.querySelector(".nav-links");

if (!hamburger || !navLinks) throw new Error("nav.js: nav elements not found");

hamburger.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
  e.stopPropagation();
  const isOpen = navLinks.classList.toggle("open");
  hamburger.setAttribute("aria-expanded", isOpen);
  hamburger.textContent = isOpen ? "✕" : "☰";
});

// Close when a nav link is clicked
navLinks.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
  if (e.target.tagName === "A") closeMenu();
});

// Close when clicking outside the nav
document.addEventListener("click", (e) => {
  if (!e.target.closest(".top-nav")) closeMenu();
});

// Close on Escape
document.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
  if (e.key === "Escape") closeMenu();
});

function closeMenu() {
  navLinks.classList.remove("open");
  hamburger.setAttribute("aria-expanded", "false");
  hamburger.textContent = "☰";
}
```

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:22:35 PM)*

All 3 steps are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**Step 1 — CSS (`reviews.css`)**
- Added `.nav-hamburger` button styles (hidden by default, matches theme/toggle styling)
- At ≤640px: hamburger shows, nav links collapse into a full-width dropdown with larger tap targets
- At ≤700px: page-header, list-detail, and public-profile headers stack vertically
- At ≤480px: review footer stacks, search row stacks, modal height adjusted, stats strip refined

**Step 2 — HTML (all 11 nav pages)**
- Added `<button class="nav-hamburger">` to every nav, sitting between the links and the theme toggle

**Step 3 — JS (`nav.js`)**
- Toggles the `.open` class on `.nav-links` and updates `aria-expanded`
- Swaps ☰ / ✕ icons on open/close
- Closes the menu on: link click, outside click, Escape key

To test, resize the browser below 640px — the nav links should collapse and the hamburger should appear in the top right next to the theme toggle.

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:23:49 PM)*

One bug found. In the `@media (max-width: 640px)` block, `.top-nav` is set to `position: relative` — this overrides `position: fixed` from the base styles, which would cause the navbar to scroll away with the page on mobile. It needs to be removed; `position: fixed` already makes the nav the containing block for the absolute-positioned dropdown, so `position: relative` is unnecessary.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:23:50 PM)*

**✏️ Edit `C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css`**

*Remove:*
```
  .top-nav {
    padding: 0 1rem;
    position: relative;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
```
*Insert:*
```
  .top-nav {
    padding: 0 1rem;
  }
```
> ✓ The file C:/Users/brock/OneDrive/Desktop/dig4503/midterm/css/reviews.css has been updated successfully.

---

**Claude** *(3/13/2026, 8:23:56 PM)*

Everything else looks correct:
- `nav.js` — `stopPropagation` on the hamburger click correctly prevents the document listener from immediately closing the menu after opening it
- `aria-expanded` updates properly on both open and close
- The dropdown uses `position: absolute; top: 100%` which works correctly against the `position: fixed` nav parent
- All 11 HTML files have the hamburger button and `nav.js` script tag in the right places

That was the only error. Also noted for future sessions — I'll check in with you before proceeding between steps in any iterative process.
